rows.control_intent: Authenticate the identity of an individual before granting access to Microsoft data, and ensure the access is appropriate.
rows.capability_detail: Entra ID authentication (MFA, passwordless/FIDO2, certificate-based) with Conditional Access gating application and data access on verified identity and session conditions
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements identity authentication before access. Entra is the primary authentication authority for Microsoft cloud and federated apps. Application- and data-store authorization layers on top.
rows.config_evidence_example: Authentication-methods policy (phishing-resistant MFA); Conditional Access requiring MFA before app access
rows.operational_evidence_example: Sign-in logs evidencing authenticated access; authentication-method registration reports
rows.external_dependencies: Application/service authorization models; identity proofing at onboarding
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Entra Conditional Access: Microsoft Entra ID P1 (included in Microsoft 365 E3/E5/E7, Microsoft 365 F1/F3, Enterprise Mobility + Security E3, and Business Premium). Risk-based conditions additionally require Entra ID P2. Authentication methods: Multifactor authentication & authentication methods: Microsoft Entra ID Free (basic) and P1; authentication strengths and full policy control require Entra ID P1.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Conditional Access: available in Azure Government (GCC, GCC High, DoD). MFA and authentication methods (incl. passwordless/FIDO2, certificate-based auth): available in Azure Government (GCC/GCC High/DoD).

rows.control_intent: Account for all physical and virtual assets supporting the engagement, with owners; inventory includes data classification of the data on each asset, recovery and disposal records.
rows.capability_detail: Intune device inventory (all enrolled devices with ownership, user, and hardware attributes) as the managed-endpoint asset register; retire/wipe device actions producing an auditable disposal trail for devices leaving the engagement
rows.how_it_supports: Supplies the managed-device slice of the asset inventory and the wipe/retire action history that feeds recovery/disposal records. Non-device assets (VMs, services), unmanaged devices, and the org-wide CMDB with owner records remain external.
rows.config_evidence_example: Enrollment coverage for all engagement devices; hardware-inventory/properties collection enabled
rows.operational_evidence_example: All-devices inventory export; device-action (retire/wipe) history for disposed devices

related_microsoft.note: Data classification supplies the 'classification of data on the asset' dimension of the inventory

rows.external_dependencies: CMDB/asset register for non-managed and non-device assets; asset ownership records
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Intune Plan 1 (the base Intune service; included in Microsoft 365 bundles such as E3/E5/E7 and Enterprise Mobility + Security E3/E5, also available standalone)

rows.control_intent: Full-disk encryption on all supplier devices that access or handle Microsoft Personal/Confidential Data.
rows.capability_detail: Endpoint security disk-encryption policy enforcing BitLocker (Windows) and FileVault (macOS) with recovery-key escrow; device compliance policy requiring encryption and reporting per-device encryption status
rows.how_it_supports: Directly enforces and attests full-disk encryption on Intune-managed devices: the enforcement and reporting layer this requirement asks for. The encryption itself is performed by the OS (BitLocker/FileVault); devices not managed by Intune need equivalent enforcement.
rows.config_evidence_example: Disk-encryption profiles (BitLocker/FileVault) assigned to all engagement devices; compliance policy with encryption required
rows.operational_evidence_example: Device encryption report; compliance-policy per-device encryption status export

related_microsoft.note: Sensitivity-label encryption adds content-layer protection that travels with files: complementary to, not a substitute for, disk encryption
Conditional Access can require a compliant (encrypted) device before granting access to Microsoft data

rows.external_dependencies: BitLocker/FileVault are OS platform capabilities; equivalent enforcement for unmanaged devices
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Intune Plan 1 (the base Intune service; included in Microsoft 365 bundles such as E3/E5/E7 and Enterprise Mobility + Security E3/E5, also available standalone)

rows.control_intent: Patch management prioritizing security patches for systems processing Microsoft data: monthly vulnerability scans with twelve months of compliance reporting, risk-based prioritization, emergency patching, coverage of OS and server software, and retirement of unsupported software.
rows.capability_detail: Defender Vulnerability Management: continuous agent-based vulnerability assessment across Windows/macOS/Linux (exceeds the monthly-scan cadence), risk-based prioritization using Microsoft threat intelligence and exploit likelihood, remediation requests with tracking, end-of-support software identification, and exportable per-device assessment reporting
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the vulnerability-scanning, risk-prioritization, and unsupported-software elements of the patch program with continuous assessment and an exportable evidence trail. The patch deployment itself and the documented procedure with severity definitions, approvals, and exceptions are outside Defender.
rows.config_evidence_example: Devices onboarded to Defender for Endpoint with vulnerability management assessments active; remediation-request workflow in use
rows.operational_evidence_example: Vulnerability assessment exports trended monthly across the prior 12 months; remediation-activity history; end-of-support software report

related_microsoft.note: Windows update rings/Autopatch and configuration policies perform the patch deployment that Defender Vulnerability Management prioritizes

rows.external_dependencies: Patch management procedure with severity definitions, approvals, and exception records; patching for network devices and systems not onboarded to Defender
rows.license_requirement: Defender Vulnerability Management core capabilities: included with Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 2 (Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or standalone P2). Premium capabilities (security baselines assessment, block vulnerable applications, browser-extension/certificate/hardware-firmware assessment) require the Defender Vulnerability Management add-on, available to Defender for Endpoint Plan 2, Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Defender Suite/EDU/GOV/FLW, Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW, and Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5/G5 customers; an MDVM standalone exists for non-P2 customers.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender Vulnerability Management core (with MDE Plan 2): available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD; the MDVM add-on trial is not available in GCC High/DoD, so confirm add-on purchase options for gov tenants.

rows.control_intent: Install anti-virus/anti-malware software on all equipment connected to networks used to process Microsoft Personal/Confidential Data (servers and desktops, all operating systems including Linux), kept patched and current with definitions updated daily; records must show the protection is active.
rows.capability_detail: Microsoft Defender Antivirus (next-generation protection) across Windows, macOS, and Linux with cloud-delivered protection and multiple-times-daily security intelligence updates; device health and antivirus status reporting showing engine, platform, and definition currency per device
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements the anti-malware requirement on onboarded equipment (real-time protection with continuously current definitions across all mandated operating systems) and produces the active-protection records the evidence bullet demands.
rows.config_evidence_example: Defender for Endpoint onboarding across engagement servers and desktops; antivirus in active mode with cloud protection and daily-or-faster intelligence updates
rows.operational_evidence_example: Device health and antivirus status reports (engine/definition versions per device); threat protection reports

related_microsoft.note: Intune endpoint security antivirus policy manages and attests the Defender Antivirus configuration on managed devices

rows.external_dependencies: Server anti-malware licensing is separate (Microsoft Defender for Endpoint for Servers / Defender for Servers); equivalent records for systems not onboarded to Defender
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 1: standalone or included in Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3. P1 covers next-gen antivirus, attack surface reduction, device control, endpoint firewall, network protection, and manual response actions. EDR, automated investigation & remediation, vulnerability management, and threat analytics require Plan 2.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender for Endpoint: available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD (G3 includes Plan 1; G5/G5 Security include Plan 2); some capabilities lag commercial, so verify per feature on the MDE US Government page.

rows.control_intent: Run a DLP program: data classified, labelled, and protected; monitor systems processing Microsoft data for intrusions, loss, and unauthorized activity; program must include IDS/IPS, breach analysis, and offboarding communications.
rows.capability_detail: The intrusion-detection/prevention and breach-analysis layer of the program: EDR behavioral sensors as host IDS/IPS on endpoints and servers, network protection blocking malicious destinations, automated investigation & remediation, and the unified Defender XDR incident queue for compromise-detection monitoring and post-breach analysis; Defender for Cloud Apps anomaly detection supplies the cloud-app monitoring slice
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the host- and cloud-level IDS/IPS and system-compromise monitoring the requirement mandates, plus post-breach analysis of affected systems (incident investigation with vulnerability follow-up). The classify/label/protect DLP core is Purview's; network-appliance IDS/IPS for non-endpoint infrastructure remains external.
rows.config_evidence_example: Devices onboarded with EDR in block mode and network protection enabled; automated investigation remediation level set; Defender for Cloud Apps app connectors and anomaly policies active
rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident queue exports with resolution evidence; automated investigation history; Defender for Cloud Apps anomaly alerts

related_microsoft.note: The DLP program core (classification, labeling, protection, and egress monitoring) is Microsoft Purview DLP/Information Protection
SIEM correlation and retention for estate-wide monitoring beyond M365/endpoint telemetry

rows.external_dependencies: Network-appliance IDS/IPS for non-endpoint infrastructure; incident response and management process; offboarding communications process
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 2: standalone or included in Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender XDR (unified portal, incidents, advanced hunting, attack disruption): available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD; Microsoft Threat Experts is not available in gov clouds; preview features are commercial-only.

rows.control_intent: Maintain an incident response plan; notify Microsoft without undue delay on a Data Incident, cooperate with investigation (including scope of data accessed), remediate and track resolution.
rows.capability_detail: Auditable track-to-resolution record for every detected Data Incident: unified incident queue with timeline, ownership, and status; incident tasks encoding the response procedure's steps; automation rules stamping assignment and severity at creation; playbooks orchestrating notification/ticketing steps; SOC incident metrics (time-to-acknowledge/time-to-close) evidencing that tracking operates
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the remediation-tracking and investigation-cooperation record the requirement demands. Each incident on connected telemetry exists as a documented case from detection to closure, with notification steps automatable as playbook actions. Sentinel documents and orchestrates; the IR plan itself, the without-undue-delay notification duty to Microsoft, and incidents outside connected sources are organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Automation rules assigning owner/severity on creation; incident task templates mirroring the Data Incident procedure; notification playbook wired to the escalation path
rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident history exports with timeline and closure state; SOC incident audit metrics; playbook run history for notification steps

related_microsoft.note: Audit (Premium) crucial events and Activity explorer supply the scope-of-data-accessed forensic artifact (the stacked Purview row)
Defender XDR detections feed the unified incident queue for Microsoft-workload Data Incidents

rows.external_dependencies: IR plan and breach-notification process; SupplierWeb security contacts; incidents on systems not connected to the workspace
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027. Microsoft Sentinel automation rules are included with the workspace (consumption-priced service); Logic Apps playbooks are billed separately under Azure Logic Apps consumption meters.

rows.control_intent: Run a DLP program: data classified, labelled, and protected; monitor systems processing Microsoft data for intrusions, loss, and unauthorized activity; program must include IDS/IPS, breach analysis, and offboarding communications.
rows.capability_detail: The estate-wide monitoring layer of the program: CEF/Syslog-over-AMA ingestion of the mandated network IDS/IPS and firewall alert streams alongside Purview DLP/IRM and Defender signals, scheduled/NRT analytics and Fusion correlation across those sources, and a retained monitoring record satisfying the 'procedures for monitoring system compromise detection tools' bullet from one pane
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the monitor-information-systems slice across the whole estate. The requirement's mandated IDS/IPS tools emit alerts that Sentinel ingests, correlates, and retains, closing the network-infrastructure gap both stacked rows leave external. Sentinel monitors and evidences; the classify/label/protect DLP core is Purview's and the host IDS/IPS engine is Defender's.
rows.config_evidence_example: IDS/IPS and firewall connectors (CEF/Syslog via AMA) alongside the Purview and Defender XDR connectors; analytics rules covering intrusion and data-loss detections; alert-to-incident grouping configured
rows.operational_evidence_example: Cross-source incident and detection trend reports; connector health showing continuous ingestion from compromise-detection tools

related_microsoft.note: The DLP program core (classification, labeling, protection, and egress monitoring) is Microsoft Purview DLP/Information Protection (the stacked Purview row)
Defender for Endpoint EDR is the host IDS/IPS engine whose detections Sentinel correlates (the stacked Defender row)

rows.external_dependencies: Network-appliance IDS/IPS platforms themselves; offboarding communications process; incident response and management process
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027. Free data sources: Office 365 audit activity (SharePoint/Exchange/Teams), Azure Activity, and all Microsoft Defender security alerts/incidents ingest at no charge. Microsoft 365 E7/E5/A5/F5/G5 and E5 Security tenants additionally get a data grant of up to 5 MB/user/day covering Entra ID sign-in/audit logs, Defender for Cloud Apps shadow-IT discovery, Purview Information Protection logs, and Defender XDR advanced-hunting tables; raw Microsoft logs beyond the grant are paid ingestion.

rows.control_intent: Install anti-virus/anti-malware software on all equipment connected to networks used to process Microsoft Personal/Confidential Data (servers and desktops, all operating systems including Linux), kept patched and current with definitions updated daily; records must show the protection is active.
rows.capability_detail: Extends the anti-malware mandate to cloud and hybrid server estates: Defender for Servers deploys and licenses the integrated Microsoft Defender for Endpoint for Servers (Windows and Linux across Azure, AWS, GCP, and on-premises via Azure Arc) per protected server, with endpoint-protection assessment recommendations flagging machines whose anti-malware is missing, unhealthy, or out of date; Defender for Storage adds on-upload malware scanning for blob storage used to exchange supplier data
rows.how_it_supports: Covers the supplier's server estate, the equipment class the stacked Defender XDR row's per-user endpoint licensing does not reach. The engine is Microsoft Defender Antivirus under MDE (the stacked Defender XDR row); Defender for Servers is the per-server consumption vehicle that deploys it across clouds and evidences that protection is active, and storage malware scanning is Defender-for-Cloud-native. Desktop coverage and definition-currency operations remain the endpoint story.
rows.config_evidence_example: Defender for Servers enabled on every subscription whose servers process Microsoft data; endpoint-protection assessment recommendations remediated; Defender for Storage malware scanning on data-exchange storage accounts
rows.operational_evidence_example: Per-server plan coverage export; endpoint-protection assessment recommendation history showing active/healthy protection; storage malware-scan verdict log

related_microsoft.note: Microsoft Defender Antivirus/MDE is the anti-malware engine on servers and desktops (the stacked Defender XDR row); seam: Defender for Servers licenses and deploys it per server for cloud estates; MDE covers user endpoints under per-user licensing

rows.external_dependencies: Anti-malware deployment on equipment outside Azure/Arc onboarding; daily definition-update operations on every OS image; records retention for AV status
rows.license_requirement: Defender for Servers Plan 1: consumption-priced per protected server per hour, billed monthly. Plan 1 centers on the integrated Microsoft Defender for Endpoint for Servers license (EDR); customers already licensed for MDE for Servers can request a price adjustment through Azure support (the MDE portion is not double-charged).
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender for Servers P1/P2: GA in Azure Government; file integrity monitoring GA (not supported in GovCon Cloud Moderate).

rows.control_intent: Run a DLP program: data classified, labelled, and protected; monitor systems processing Microsoft data for intrusions, loss, and unauthorized activity; program must include IDS/IPS, breach analysis, and offboarding communications.
rows.capability_detail: Cloud-workload intrusion detection for systems processing Microsoft data in Azure/AWS/GCP: threat-detection plans for Storage (suspicious access, unusual extraction, malware distribution), Databases (SQL injection, brute force, anomalous access), Containers (runtime and control-plane attacks), Key Vault and Resource Manager (credential/control-plane abuse), and Servers (behavioral, fileless, and network-layer alerts; DNS-layer alerts with Plan 2). Alerts stream into the Microsoft Defender portal and Sentinel
rows.how_it_supports: Supplies the intrusion-detection slice for the cloud-workload plane that neither stacked detection row natively senses: storage accounts, databases, Kubernetes, and the cloud control plane. Defender for Cloud detects for cloud workloads; the DLP program core is Purview's, host IDS/IPS on endpoints is Defender XDR's, network-appliance IDS/IPS stays external, and breach analysis runs in the SOC.
rows.config_evidence_example: Workload protection plans enabled on subscriptions processing Microsoft data; alert export to the Defender portal/Sentinel configured; email notifications for high-severity alerts
rows.operational_evidence_example: Cloud workload alert history by plan; incident correlation records in the Defender portal; plan-coverage report per subscription

related_microsoft.note: The DLP program core (classification, labeling, protection, egress monitoring) is Microsoft Purview (the stacked Purview row)
Defender for Endpoint carries host-level IDS/IPS on endpoints; Defender for Cloud alerts correlate with it in the same Defender portal; seam, not overlap
Defender for Cloud security alerts ingest into Sentinel free of charge for estate-wide correlation and retention (the stacked Sentinel row)

rows.external_dependencies: Network-appliance IDS/IPS platforms; breach-analysis and offboarding-communication processes
rows.license_requirement: Consumption-priced per protected resource, metered by plan: Defender for Storage per storage account/month plus per-transaction overage above 73M/month and per-GB malware scanning; Defender for Databases per database server (Cosmos DB per provisioned RU); Defender for Containers per vCore of Kubernetes worker nodes (includes a monthly image-scan allowance); Defender for App Service per app instance; Defender for Key Vault per vault/month; Defender for Resource Manager per subscription/month; Defender for APIs by call-volume plan tier (Plans 1–5); AI Services per 1K tokens. 30-day free trial on first enablement (malware scanning excluded).
rows.cloud_availability_note: Azure Government gaps: Defender for APIs, Defender for App Service, AI Services threat protection, Defender for Cosmos DB, and DevOps security are not available; within Defender CSPM, CIEM, the Data & AI security dashboard, EASM integration, ServiceNow integration, and code-to-runtime mapping are not available; Defender for Storage sensitive data threat detection is not available (DSPM sensitive data scanning is GA).

rows.control_intent: Apply data leakage prevention to systems, networks, and devices handling sensitive information.
rows.capability_detail: Conditional Access App Control session policies blocking download/cut/copy/print of sensitive content in browser sessions to SaaS apps (including from unmanaged devices); access policies that can block native-client sign-in to force sessions through the controlled browser path; Cloud Discovery surfacing unsanctioned apps as leakage channels
rows.how_it_supports: Adds the SaaS-session and shadow-IT slice of data leakage prevention. Session controls are reverse-proxy/Edge-in-browser only; native desktop and mobile clients bypass them entirely unless access policies block native-client access, so this row never substitutes for content-aware DLP, which is Purview's.
rows.config_evidence_example: Session policies for sensitive apps (block download on unmanaged devices); access policy blocking native clients for in-scope apps; unsanctioned-app blocks pushed to Defender for Endpoint
rows.operational_evidence_example: Session-policy match/block logs; discovered-app reports showing leakage-channel remediation

related_microsoft.note: Content-aware DLP across M365, endpoints, and browsers is Microsoft Purview DLP, the control's primary implementation
Conditional Access routes app sessions through Conditional Access App Control

The stacked Defender row supplies Microsoft-researcher intelligence contextualized to the tenant; the strategic analysis and sharing program remains organizational.

rows.license_requirement: Included with Microsoft Sentinel on the workspace (no separate license); cost follows the consumption-priced data volume the capability operates over. Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Azure Government feature gaps: summary rules, SOC optimization, SIEM migration, repositories, MDTI matching analytics, URL detonation, and TI GeoLocation/WhoIs enrichment are not available; scheduled/NRT/Fusion analytics, automation rules, playbooks, watchlists, and core UEBA are GA (peer/blast-radius enrichments commercial-only). The M365 E5/G5 Sentinel data-grant benefit includes USGOV_GCCHIGH and USGOV_DOD SKUs.

rows.control_intent: Assess information security events and decide whether they qualify as incidents.
rows.capability_detail: The event-to-incident promotion engine for the estate: analytics rules evaluate ingested events against detection logic and severity, alert grouping consolidates related events, incidents are created with entity mapping and MITRE context, and automation rules apply triage decisions (severity adjustment, suppression of known-benign patterns) at scale
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the assessment mechanics across every connected source: events are evaluated, correlated, and either promoted to incidents or suppressed under recorded logic, which is the operational substance of the assess-and-decide activity. The classification procedure and the human decision on ambiguous events remain organizational; the stacked Purview and Defender rows supply the workload-native event detail this engine consumes.
rows.config_evidence_example: Analytics rule set with severity and grouping configuration; automation rules encoding triage decisions; incident-creation settings for Microsoft alert sources
rows.operational_evidence_example: Alert-to-incident promotion statistics; automation-rule disposition history; tuned-rule change record

related_microsoft.note: DLP/IRM alert queues supply the data-activity event detail (the stacked Purview row)
Defender XDR verdicts and incident correlation feed assessment for Microsoft workloads (the stacked Defender row)

rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Sentinel in the Defender portal: all generally-available unified-SecOps features are supported in GCC, GCC High, and DoD (portal URLs security.microsoft.us / security.apps.mil); preview features are commercial-cloud only; in GCC, advanced-hunting queries spanning both Sentinel and Defender XDR tables are not supported.

rows.control_intent: Respond to information security incidents in accordance with documented procedures.
rows.capability_detail: Documented response procedures made operational: automation rules execute consistent triage (assignment, severity, tagging) on every incident; Logic Apps playbooks encode procedure steps as repeatable workflows: enrichment, notification, ticketing, and containment calls into integrated enforcement products; incident tasks present the procedure's checklist inside each incident
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the according-to-documented-procedures quality of response: procedures encoded as automation run identically on every qualifying incident, with the run history proving it. Sentinel orchestrates the workflow; the containment actions themselves execute in the integrated products (Defender device isolation, Entra account disable, firewall block), and the response procedures and decisions are organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Playbook library mapped to incident-type procedures; automation rules binding playbooks to analytics rules; incident task templates
rows.operational_evidence_example: Playbook run history per incident; automation-rule execution log; incident task completion records

related_microsoft.note: Defender XDR executes endpoint/mailbox containment actions that playbooks invoke
Entra ID performs the account-disable/revoke-session actions playbooks call

rows.external_dependencies: Documented incident response procedures; SOC staffing and decision authority; Logic Apps connectors to ticketing/communication systems
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027. Microsoft Sentinel automation rules are included with the workspace (consumption-priced service); Logic Apps playbooks are billed separately under Azure Logic Apps consumption meters.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Azure Government feature gaps: summary rules, SOC optimization, SIEM migration, repositories, MDTI matching analytics, URL detonation, and TI GeoLocation/WhoIs enrichment are not available; scheduled/NRT/Fusion analytics, automation rules, playbooks, watchlists, and core UEBA are GA (peer/blast-radius enrichments commercial-only). The M365 E5/G5 Sentinel data-grant benefit includes USGOV_GCCHIGH and USGOV_DOD SKUs.

rows.control_intent: Establish procedures for identifying, collecting, and preserving evidence related to security events.
rows.capability_detail: The security-telemetry evidence layer: incident records preserving the detection, investigation timeline, entity evidence, bookmarks, and analyst actions for each event; immutable-by-design log copy in the workspace with data-lake retention up to 12 years keeping the underlying telemetry available for later proceedings; search jobs retrieving historical evidence on demand
rows.how_it_supports: Preserves the security-event evidence trail (what happened, when, what was observed, and what was done) as a durable record independent of the source systems. The evidence-handling procedure, chain-of-custody, and content-level collection (mailboxes, documents) are the stacked Purview eDiscovery row's territory; Sentinel evidences the security timeline, it does not perform legal collection.
rows.config_evidence_example: Retention configured to the evidence-retention requirement; incident and bookmark usage in investigation procedure; RBAC restricting workspace data access
rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident evidence exports (timeline, entities, bookmarks); data-lake retention settings per table; search-job retrieval records

related_microsoft.note: eDiscovery cases, holds, and defensible export perform legal evidence collection and preservation for M365 content (the stacked Purview row)

rows.external_dependencies: Forensic procedure and chain-of-custody policy; endpoint/disk forensics tooling for artifact-level evidence
rows.license_requirement: Consumption-priced retention: analytics-tier retention beyond the included 90 days is billed per GB (extendable to 2 years); Microsoft Sentinel data lake total retention up to 12 years is billed per compressed GB/month (6:1 compression assumption) plus per-GB query/processing meters when archived data is scanned. These are the meters behind 12-month (PCI DSS) and multi-year (CMMC/800-53) retention mandates.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Sentinel in the Defender portal: all generally-available unified-SecOps features are supported in GCC, GCC High, and DoD (portal URLs security.microsoft.us / security.apps.mil); preview features are commercial-cloud only; in GCC, advanced-hunting queries spanning both Sentinel and Defender XDR tables are not supported.

rows.control_intent: Produce, protect, and analyze logs of activities, exceptions, and events.
rows.capability_detail: Centralized estate-wide log management: 350+ connectors collecting Microsoft 365/Entra/Defender/Azure logs plus servers, network devices, firewalls, and third-party SaaS via CEF/Syslog-AMA and codeless connectors; workspace RBAC and table-level access control protecting the log store; KQL analysis over the consolidated record; per-table retention with data-lake tiering
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements the log-management activity this control names for every source routed to the workspace: the collection, protection, and analysis of activity/exception/event logs is Sentinel's namesake function. Source systems must generate their own records (the stacked Purview row is the M365 generator), and unconnected systems stay outside the trail.
rows.config_evidence_example: Connector coverage matching the logging standard's source list; DCRs selecting mandated events; workspace RBAC; per-table retention settings
rows.operational_evidence_example: Connector health reports showing continuous ingestion; log-volume trend by source; KQL analysis library in use

related_microsoft.note: Purview Audit generates the M365 activity record that the Microsoft 365 connector ingests; seam: Purview is the M365 log source, Sentinel is the estate-wide log platform (the stacked Purview row)
Defender XDR alerts and hunting tables are first-party log sources (alerts ingest free)

rows.external_dependencies: Logging standard defining mandated events per source; log generation on source systems; time synchronization
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027. Free data sources: Office 365 audit activity (SharePoint/Exchange/Teams), Azure Activity, and all Microsoft Defender security alerts/incidents ingest at no charge. Microsoft 365 E7/E5/A5/F5/G5 and E5 Security tenants additionally get a data grant of up to 5 MB/user/day covering Entra ID sign-in/audit logs, Defender for Cloud Apps shadow-IT discovery, Purview Information Protection logs, and Defender XDR advanced-hunting tables; raw Microsoft logs beyond the grant are paid ingestion.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Sentinel in the Defender portal: all generally-available unified-SecOps features are supported in GCC, GCC High, and DoD (portal URLs security.microsoft.us / security.apps.mil); preview features are commercial-cloud only; in GCC, advanced-hunting queries spanning both Sentinel and Defender XDR tables are not supported.

rows.control_intent: Monitor networks, systems, and applications for anomalous behavior and act on findings.
rows.capability_detail: Continuous security monitoring across networks, systems, and applications from one engine: scheduled/NRT analytics and Fusion multistage correlation over every connected source (network appliances, servers, SaaS, Microsoft workloads), ML anomaly detections and UEBA behavioral baselining surfacing anomalous behavior, alerting into the unified incident queue where automation acts on findings
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements the monitoring activity this control names at estate scope. The four stacked product rows each monitor their own plane (Purview: data activity; Entra: cloud sign-ins; MDE: endpoints; MDI: on-prem AD), and Sentinel is the layer that monitors across all of them plus the non-Microsoft estate and correlates the whole. Monitoring reaches only connected sources, and acting on findings beyond automated response is the SOC's process.
rows.config_evidence_example: Connector estate covering networks/systems/applications in scope; analytics + anomaly rules enabled per source; UEBA enabled on identity data; automation rules acting on findings
rows.operational_evidence_example: Detection and incident trend reports across sources; UEBA anomaly history; automation-rule action log

related_microsoft.note: Insider Risk Management monitors user data-handling behavior, the data-activity plane of this stack
Entra ID Protection monitors cloud sign-in risk, the cloud-identity plane
Defender for Endpoint and Defender for Identity monitor endpoints and on-prem AD, the workload detection engines whose signal Sentinel correlates, seam not overlap

rows.external_dependencies: Monitoring scope definition; SOC response process for findings; telemetry from network appliances
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027. Included with Microsoft Sentinel on the workspace (no separate license); cost follows the consumption-priced data volume the capability operates over.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Azure Government feature gaps: summary rules, SOC optimization, SIEM migration, repositories, MDTI matching analytics, URL detonation, and TI GeoLocation/WhoIs enrichment are not available; scheduled/NRT/Fusion analytics, automation rules, playbooks, watchlists, and core UEBA are GA (peer/blast-radius enrichments commercial-only). The M365 E5/G5 Sentinel data-grant benefit includes USGOV_GCCHIGH and USGOV_DOD SKUs.

rows.control_intent: Establish processes for the secure acquisition, use, management, and exit of cloud services.
rows.capability_detail: Security management of cloud services in use at the infrastructure layer: secure score and Microsoft cloud security benchmark recommendations continuously assess the configuration of Azure subscriptions and, via the free multicloud connectors, AWS accounts and GCP projects; asset inventory maintains the live register of cloud resources those services comprise
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the manage-securely-while-in-use slice of this control for IaaS/PaaS cloud services: the organization can see, score, and track the security state of every onboarded cloud environment. The stacked Defender XDR row's MDCA card governs the SaaS application layer (discovery, sanctioning, app risk); a seam, not an overlap: MDCA covers SaaS apps, Defender for Cloud covers cloud infrastructure. Acquisition, contractual, and exit processes remain organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Azure subscriptions auto-onboarded; AWS/GCP connectors established for all cloud estates in scope; secure score baseline recorded per environment
rows.operational_evidence_example: Secure score trend per cloud environment; MCSB recommendation reports; asset inventory exports for cloud-service registers

related_microsoft.note: Defender for Cloud Apps governs the SaaS application layer of cloud-service use (the stacked Defender XDR row); seam: MDCA covers SaaS apps, Defender for Cloud covers IaaS/PaaS infrastructure

rows.external_dependencies: Cloud service acquisition, contract, and exit management processes; supplier assessment; Azure Policy / IaC pipelines executing configuration remediation (Azure platform, outside this product)
rows.license_requirement: Foundational CSPM is free on every subscription/account onboarded to Defender for Cloud (secure score, MCSB security recommendations, asset inventory, multicloud connectors): no per-user license and no charge. The paid plans below are consumption-priced per protected resource.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Available in Azure Government: Foundational CSPM (asset inventory, MCSB recommendations, secure score), Defender CSPM, Defender for Servers (P1/P2), Defender for Storage, Containers, Key Vault, Resource Manager, and the SQL/open-source database plans are GA.

rows.control_intent: Obtain information about technical vulnerabilities of systems in use, evaluate exposure, and take appropriate measures.
rows.capability_detail: Cloud-estate vulnerability assessment without agents: agentless machine scanning surfaces OS and installed-software vulnerabilities and exposed secrets on VMs across Azure/AWS/GCP; agentless container vulnerability assessment covers registry images and running containers; SQL vulnerability assessment covers database estates; attack path analysis and risk prioritization evaluate which findings are exploitable in context (internet exposure, lateral movement, sensitive data reach)
rows.how_it_supports: Directly performs the obtain-and-evaluate activity this control names for the cloud resource estate: vulnerabilities are identified continuously and exposure is evaluated through the cloud security graph rather than raw severity alone. The stacked Defender XDR row's MDVM covers endpoint devices; seam: MDVM scans managed endpoints, Defender for Cloud scans cloud workloads agentlessly. Taking measures (patching, redeployment) executes outside the product.
rows.config_evidence_example: Defender CSPM enabled with agentless scanning on (Subscription Owner required); container registry VA active; attack path analysis reviewed on an operational cadence
rows.operational_evidence_example: Vulnerability findings by resource with risk prioritization; attack-path reports naming chain-breaker fixes; remediation-tracking via governance rules

related_microsoft.note: Microsoft Defender Vulnerability Management covers managed endpoint devices (the stacked Defender XDR row); seam: MDVM for endpoints, Defender for Cloud agentless scanning for cloud VMs/containers/databases

rows.external_dependencies: Patch execution (Azure Update Manager, IaC redeploys, vendor updates, all outside this product); vulnerability management procedure and SLAs; non-cloud asset scanning
rows.license_requirement: Defender CSPM: consumption-priced per billable resource per month. It protects all multicloud workloads but bills only on servers, storage accounts, databases (OSS/SQL incl. servers-on-machines), and serverless resources (conversion ratios apply). 30-day free trial on first enablement; a 1-year pre-purchase plan (Commit Units) discounts up to 22%.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Azure Government gaps: Defender for APIs, Defender for App Service, AI Services threat protection, Defender for Cosmos DB, and DevOps security are not available; within Defender CSPM, CIEM, the Data & AI security dashboard, EASM integration, ServiceNow integration, and code-to-runtime mapping are not available; Defender for Storage sensitive data threat detection is not available (DSPM sensitive data scanning is GA).

rows.control_intent: Establish, document, implement, monitor, and review configurations, including security configurations, of hardware, software, services, and networks.
rows.capability_detail: Continuous configuration monitoring for cloud resources: Microsoft cloud security benchmark recommendations evaluate subscription, network, storage, database, and service configurations against secure baselines across Azure/AWS/GCP; Defender for Servers Plan 2 adds OS security-baseline assessment (compute baselines in MCSB) and system-updates assessment on machines; misconfiguration findings are risk-ranked and tracked to closure via governance rules
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the monitor-and-review half of this control for cloud infrastructure: drift from secure configuration is detected, ranked, and reported continuously. Establishing baselines and implementing/enforcing them is performed by Azure Policy, IaC pipelines, and platform teams (and by Intune for managed devices, the stacked Intune row); Defender for Cloud finds the gap, it does not close it.
rows.config_evidence_example: MCSB assigned (default) with tuned policy parameters; Defender for Servers P2 for OS baseline assessment; governance rules assigning misconfiguration remediation with SLAs
rows.operational_evidence_example: Recommendation state history per resource type; OS baseline assessment reports; governance-rule closure metrics

related_microsoft.note: Intune establishes and enforces device/OS configuration for managed endpoints (the stacked Intune row); seam: Intune enforces device config, Defender for Cloud monitors cloud resource config

rows.external_dependencies: Azure Policy assignments and IaC pipelines that establish and enforce configurations (Azure platform, outside this product); configuration standards documentation; network device configuration outside the cloud estate
rows.license_requirement: Foundational CSPM is free on every subscription/account onboarded to Defender for Cloud (secure score, MCSB security recommendations, asset inventory, multicloud connectors): no per-user license and no charge. The paid plans below are consumption-priced per protected resource. Defender for Servers Plan 2: consumption-priced per protected server per hour, billed monthly. Includes everything in Plan 1 (incl. the integrated MDE for Servers license) plus agentless machine scanning, premium Defender Vulnerability Management capabilities, file integrity monitoring, just-in-time VM access, OS baseline/system-updates assessment, DNS-layer alerts, and a 500 MB/day free security-data ingestion allowance on the associated Log Analytics/Microsoft Sentinel workspace (applies to designated security tables).
rows.cloud_availability_note: Available in Azure Government: Foundational CSPM (asset inventory, MCSB recommendations, secure score), Defender CSPM, Defender for Servers (P1/P2), Defender for Storage, Containers, Key Vault, Resource Manager, and the SQL/open-source database plans are GA.

rows.control_intent: Regularly verify compliance with the organization's own security policy, topic-specific policies, rules, and standards.
rows.capability_detail: Continuous technical-compliance review for the cloud estate: the regulatory compliance dashboard assesses onboarded Azure/AWS/GCP resources against the Microsoft cloud security benchmark by default and against assigned standards (ISO/IEC 27001:2022 is a built-in standard; custom standards encode organization-specific rules), showing per-control pass/fail with remediation recommendations and downloadable evidence reports on a continuous assessment cycle
rows.how_it_supports: Directly performs the regular-compliance-review activity this control names, for the cloud resource scope onboarded to Defender for Cloud, and only for the technically-assessable subset of each standard. It does not review M365 workloads, on-premises systems, or administrative/procedural compliance, and its output is a technical assessment, not an attestation or audit. The stacked Purview row's Compliance Manager covers the M365/Purview estate: complementary scopes, a seam not an overlap.
rows.config_evidence_example: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 standard assigned to in-scope subscriptions/accounts (requires a paid plan); custom standards for organization-specific rules; assessment scope documented
rows.operational_evidence_example: Per-standard compliance reports on a review cadence; control pass/fail history; remediation tracking from dashboard recommendations

related_microsoft.note: Purview Compliance Manager performs the parallel review for the M365/Purview estate with improvement actions and compliance score (the stacked Purview row); complementary assessment scopes, a seam not an overlap

rows.external_dependencies: Policy compliance review for non-cloud and procedural controls; internal audit program; management review process
rows.license_requirement: Regulatory compliance dashboard: the default Microsoft cloud security benchmark (MCSB) assessment is included free with Defender for Cloud. Assigning additional built-in or custom compliance standards requires at least one paid Defender for Cloud plan on the assessed scope, meaning any plan except Defender for Servers Plan 1 and Defender for APIs Plan 1.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Regulatory compliance dashboard: GA in Azure Government (configuration via the Azure portal experience; available standards vary by cloud, so verify the standard list in the target cloud).

rows.control_intent: Implement protection against malware, supported by appropriate user awareness.
rows.capability_detail: Malware protection for cloud data and server workloads: Defender for Storage scans uploaded blobs on-write (and on-demand) with Microsoft Defender Antivirus verdicts, blocking malware distribution through cloud storage; Defender for Servers deploys the integrated MDE anti-malware engine on Windows/Linux servers across clouds, with endpoint-protection assessment flagging unprotected machines
rows.how_it_supports: Extends the anti-malware control to two planes the stacked rows do not cover: files landing in cloud storage (a distribution channel no endpoint engine sees) and cloud/hybrid server estates licensed per server rather than per user. The engine on machines is Defender Antivirus under MDE (the stacked Defender XDR row); Intune enforces the policy on managed devices (the stacked Intune row); user awareness remains organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Defender for Storage with malware scanning enabled on in-scope accounts; Defender for Servers on server subscriptions; endpoint-protection assessment recommendations remediated
rows.operational_evidence_example: Storage malware-scan verdict logs; per-server protection coverage report; blocked-upload incident records

related_microsoft.note: Microsoft Defender Antivirus/MDE is the malware engine on endpoints and servers (the stacked Defender XDR row); Defender for Cloud adds the storage-scanning plane and the per-server deployment vehicle
Intune enforces the anti-malware policy configuration on managed devices (the stacked Intune row)

rows.external_dependencies: User awareness program; anti-malware for equipment outside cloud/endpoint management
rows.license_requirement: Consumption-priced per protected resource, metered by plan: Defender for Storage per storage account/month plus per-transaction overage above 73M/month and per-GB malware scanning; Defender for Databases per database server (Cosmos DB per provisioned RU); Defender for Containers per vCore of Kubernetes worker nodes (includes a monthly image-scan allowance); Defender for App Service per app instance; Defender for Key Vault per vault/month; Defender for Resource Manager per subscription/month; Defender for APIs by call-volume plan tier (Plans 1–5); AI Services per 1K tokens. 30-day free trial on first enablement (malware scanning excluded).
rows.cloud_availability_note: Azure Government gaps: Defender for APIs, Defender for App Service, AI Services threat protection, Defender for Cosmos DB, and DevOps security are not available; within Defender CSPM, CIEM, the Data & AI security dashboard, EASM integration, ServiceNow integration, and code-to-runtime mapping are not available; Defender for Storage sensitive data threat detection is not available (DSPM sensitive data scanning is GA).

rows.control_intent: Monitor networks, systems, and applications for anomalous behavior and act on findings.
rows.capability_detail: The cloud-infrastructure plane of the monitoring stack: workload threat detection over storage accounts (anomalous access/extraction), databases (injection, brute force), Kubernetes clusters (runtime and control-plane), Key Vault and Resource Manager operations (control-plane abuse), APIs, AI services, and servers (behavioral/fileless/network-layer analytics). Alerts correlate into Defender portal incidents and stream to Sentinel
rows.how_it_supports: Adds the plane the five stacked product rows do not sense: cloud resource and control-plane behavior across Azure/AWS/GCP. Each stacked row monitors its own domain (Purview: data activity; Entra: cloud sign-ins; MDE: endpoints; MDI: on-prem AD; Sentinel: cross-source correlation); Defender for Cloud monitors cloud workloads and infrastructure. Acting on findings beyond portal-driven response is the SOC's process.
rows.config_evidence_example: Workload protection plans enabled per resource risk; alert notifications and Defender portal integration active; alert suppression tuned
rows.operational_evidence_example: Cloud alert and incident trend reports by plan; control-plane alert history; response-action records from the Defender portal

related_microsoft.note: Defender XDR correlates cloud alerts with endpoint/identity/email signal in the same portal incidents (the stacked Defender rows); seam: MDE monitors endpoints, Defender for Cloud monitors cloud workloads
Sentinel ingests Defender for Cloud alerts free and correlates them estate-wide with retention beyond the portal window (the stacked Sentinel row)

rows.external_dependencies: SOC response process; network-appliance telemetry outside cloud estates; monitoring scope definition
rows.license_requirement: Consumption-priced per protected resource, metered by plan: Defender for Storage per storage account/month plus per-transaction overage above 73M/month and per-GB malware scanning; Defender for Databases per database server (Cosmos DB per provisioned RU); Defender for Containers per vCore of Kubernetes worker nodes (includes a monthly image-scan allowance); Defender for App Service per app instance; Defender for Key Vault per vault/month; Defender for Resource Manager per subscription/month; Defender for APIs by call-volume plan tier (Plans 1–5); AI Services per 1K tokens. 30-day free trial on first enablement (malware scanning excluded).
rows.cloud_availability_note: Azure Government gaps: Defender for APIs, Defender for App Service, AI Services threat protection, Defender for Cosmos DB, and DevOps security are not available; within Defender CSPM, CIEM, the Data & AI security dashboard, EASM integration, ServiceNow integration, and code-to-runtime mapping are not available; Defender for Storage sensitive data threat detection is not available (DSPM sensitive data scanning is GA).

rows.control_intent: Monitor system components for anomalies indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, or errors.
rows.capability_detail: Estate-wide component monitoring from one engine: analytics rules and ML anomaly detections over every connected source, including infrastructure, network appliance, and application telemetry the workload products don't see, plus UEBA behavioral baselining; error/availability anomalies surfaced from operational log streams (service health, heartbeat flat-lines, error-rate spikes); all findings alerting into the unified incident queue
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the cross-component monitoring layer of this criterion, including the errors-and-availability dimension the stacked Defender row leaves external: anomaly detection runs on whatever telemetry is routed in, Microsoft or not. The stack: Purview watches data activity, Entra cloud sign-ins, Defender the workload planes, Sentinel monitors across and beyond them. Coverage follows the connector estate, and the monitoring program itself is organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Connector estate spanning in-scope system components; analytics + anomaly rules per component class; heartbeat/flat-line detections for error conditions; UEBA enabled
rows.operational_evidence_example: Cross-source anomaly and detection trend reports; connector health showing monitoring continuity; alert-to-incident statistics

rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027. Free data sources: Office 365 audit activity (SharePoint/Exchange/Teams), Azure Activity, and all Microsoft Defender security alerts/incidents ingest at no charge. Microsoft 365 E7/E5/A5/F5/G5 and E5 Security tenants additionally get a data grant of up to 5 MB/user/day covering Entra ID sign-in/audit logs, Defender for Cloud Apps shadow-IT discovery, Purview Information Protection logs, and Defender XDR advanced-hunting tables; raw Microsoft logs beyond the grant are paid ingestion.

rows.control_intent: Evaluate security events to determine whether they are security incidents.
rows.capability_detail: Event-to-incident evaluation across every connected source: analytics rules assess ingested events, alert grouping and Fusion correlation consolidate related signals, including joining Defender XDR alerts with non-Microsoft telemetry into one incident, and the incident record captures the evaluation outcome, severity, and analyst disposition with a full audit trail
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the evaluation mechanics for the sources the stacked rows leave external: non-Microsoft events are assessed by the same recorded logic as Microsoft ones, and cross-source correlation determines incident scope no single workload product can see. The incident-declaration procedure and severity matrix remain organizational; Defender XDR's AIR verdicts pre-assess the Microsoft-workload slice.
rows.config_evidence_example: Analytics rules with severity and entity mapping; incident-creation and grouping settings; automation rules recording triage dispositions
rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident classification/severity history with disposition audit trail; alert-to-incident promotion statistics across sources

Defender XDR correlation and AIR verdicts pre-assess Microsoft-workload events (the stacked Defender row); seam: Defender evaluates its workloads, Sentinel evaluates the estate

rows.external_dependencies: Incident declaration procedure and severity matrix; SOC triage staffing and decision authority
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027.

rows.control_intent: Respond to identified security incidents via a defined program (contain, remediate, communicate).
rows.capability_detail: Response-program orchestration: automation rules apply the program's triage decisions on every incident; Logic Apps playbooks execute the communicate-and-coordinate steps (notification, ticketing, stakeholder updates) and invoke containment in integrated products, including non-Microsoft systems via 400+ Logic Apps connectors (firewalls, ITSM, identity platforms); incident tasks track program steps to completion
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the program-execution workflow the stacked rows leave external: a defined response program encoded as automation runs consistently and leaves a run history, and playbooks reach the non-Microsoft systems Defender's response hands cannot. Sentinel orchestrates; containment executes in the integrated enforcement products, and the program itself (roles, legal/notification duties) is organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Playbook library per incident type incl. non-Microsoft containment integrations; automation rules binding playbooks; incident task templates for program steps
rows.operational_evidence_example: Playbook run history; incident task completion records; response-time metrics per incident type

related_microsoft.note: Defender XDR executes Microsoft-estate containment (isolate device, quarantine, disable user) that playbooks invoke (the stacked Defender row)
Audit and eDiscovery preserve compromise-scope evidence for the response record (the stacked Purview row)

rows.external_dependencies: Incident response program (roles, escalation, communications, legal/notification duties); Logic Apps connector credentials to third-party systems
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027. Microsoft Sentinel automation rules are included with the workspace (consumption-priced service); Logic Apps playbooks are billed separately under Azure Logic Apps consumption meters.

rows.control_intent: Use detection and monitoring procedures to identify configuration changes introducing new vulnerabilities and susceptibilities to newly discovered vulnerabilities.
rows.capability_detail: The criterion's two named activities as one continuous engine for cloud infrastructure: Microsoft cloud security benchmark recommendations detect configuration changes that introduce security weaknesses across Azure/AWS/GCP resources, and agentless machine/container/SQL vulnerability scanning identifies susceptibility to newly discovered vulnerabilities, with attack path analysis ranking which combinations are actually exploitable
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements the detection-and-monitoring procedure this criterion names for the cloud resource scope: misconfiguration detection and vulnerability identification are the product's namesake activities, running continuously rather than on scan windows. The stacked Defender XDR row covers the endpoint estate via MDVM; seam, not overlap. Remediation of what is found executes outside the product.
rows.config_evidence_example: Defender CSPM with agentless scanning enabled across cloud estates; MCSB active as the configuration baseline; attack path review cadence
rows.operational_evidence_example: Recommendation and vulnerability finding history; new-finding alerting records; attack-path trend reports

related_microsoft.note: MDVM performs the equivalent detection for managed endpoint devices (the stacked Defender XDR row); seam: endpoints vs cloud resources

rows.external_dependencies: Remediation execution (Azure Policy, IaC, patching, all outside this product); change management procedure; non-cloud infrastructure scanning
rows.license_requirement: Defender CSPM: consumption-priced per billable resource per month. It protects all multicloud workloads but bills only on servers, storage accounts, databases (OSS/SQL incl. servers-on-machines), and serverless resources (conversion ratios apply). 30-day free trial on first enablement; a 1-year pre-purchase plan (Commit Units) discounts up to 22%.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Azure Government gaps: Defender for APIs, Defender for App Service, AI Services threat protection, Defender for Cosmos DB, and DevOps security are not available; within Defender CSPM, CIEM, the Data & AI security dashboard, EASM integration, ServiceNow integration, and code-to-runtime mapping are not available; Defender for Storage sensitive data threat detection is not available (DSPM sensitive data scanning is GA).

rows.control_intent: Implement controls to prevent or detect and act upon the introduction of unauthorized or malicious software.
rows.capability_detail: Malicious-software controls for cloud and hybrid server estates and cloud storage: Defender for Servers deploys the integrated MDE anti-malware engine per server across Azure/AWS/GCP/Arc with endpoint-protection assessment verifying coverage; Defender for Storage malware scanning detects and can block malicious files introduced through blob uploads, a software-introduction channel endpoint controls never see
rows.how_it_supports: Extends the criterion's control set to server workloads (licensed per server, not per user) and to the cloud-storage introduction path. The engine and its EDR response live under MDE (the stacked Defender XDR row); Intune enforces device-level software controls (the stacked Intune row). Application allow-listing and change control for cloud workloads remain external.
rows.config_evidence_example: Defender for Servers enabled on server subscriptions; Defender for Storage malware scanning with blocking posture on in-scope accounts
rows.operational_evidence_example: Server protection coverage reports; storage malware detection/block logs; unhealthy-protection remediation history

related_microsoft.note: Defender Antivirus/MDE is the engine that prevents/detects malicious software on machines (the stacked Defender XDR row)
Intune App Control for Business and AV policy enforce software controls on managed devices (the stacked Intune row)

rows.external_dependencies: Application allow-listing for server workloads; software change control; storage accounts outside plan coverage
rows.license_requirement: Defender for Servers Plan 1: consumption-priced per protected server per hour, billed monthly. Plan 1 centers on the integrated Microsoft Defender for Endpoint for Servers license (EDR); customers already licensed for MDE for Servers can request a price adjustment through Azure support (the MDE portion is not double-charged). Consumption-priced per protected resource, metered by plan: Defender for Storage per storage account/month plus per-transaction overage above 73M/month and per-GB malware scanning; Defender for Databases per database server (Cosmos DB per provisioned RU); Defender for Containers per vCore of Kubernetes worker nodes (includes a monthly image-scan allowance); Defender for App Service per app instance; Defender for Key Vault per vault/month; Defender for Resource Manager per subscription/month; Defender for APIs by call-volume plan tier (Plans 1–5); AI Services per 1K tokens. 30-day free trial on first enablement (malware scanning excluded).
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender for Servers P1/P2: GA in Azure Government; file integrity monitoring GA (not supported in GovCon Cloud Moderate).

rows.control_intent: Monitor system components for anomalies indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, or errors.
rows.capability_detail: Anomaly monitoring for the cloud-infrastructure component class: threat detection over storage access patterns, database queries, Kubernetes runtime, Key Vault/Resource Manager control-plane operations, API traffic, and server behavior, with Microsoft Threat Intelligence-backed analytics generating severity-ranked alerts that correlate into Defender portal incidents
rows.how_it_supports: Adds cloud workloads and the cloud control plane to the four-product monitoring stack this criterion already carries (Purview: data handling; Entra: sign-in anomalies; Defender XDR: endpoint/identity/email; Sentinel: estate-wide correlation); seam on every side, overlap on none. Environmental/disaster monitoring and anomalies on unmonitored components remain external.
rows.config_evidence_example: Workload protection plans enabled on in-scope cloud components; alert routing to the SOC; suppression rules tuned to the environment
rows.operational_evidence_example: Alert trend and disposition reports by plan; incident correlation records; coverage report per component class

related_microsoft.note: Defender XDR monitors endpoints/identities/email and correlates cloud alerts into shared incidents (the stacked Defender row); seam, not overlap
Sentinel ingests Defender for Cloud alerts free for estate-wide correlation (the stacked Sentinel row)

rows.external_dependencies: Availability/disaster monitoring (Azure Monitor/Service Health, outside this product); SOC triage process; components outside plan coverage
rows.license_requirement: Consumption-priced per protected resource, metered by plan: Defender for Storage per storage account/month plus per-transaction overage above 73M/month and per-GB malware scanning; Defender for Databases per database server (Cosmos DB per provisioned RU); Defender for Containers per vCore of Kubernetes worker nodes (includes a monthly image-scan allowance); Defender for App Service per app instance; Defender for Key Vault per vault/month; Defender for Resource Manager per subscription/month; Defender for APIs by call-volume plan tier (Plans 1–5); AI Services per 1K tokens. 30-day free trial on first enablement (malware scanning excluded).
rows.cloud_availability_note: Azure Government gaps: Defender for APIs, Defender for App Service, AI Services threat protection, Defender for Cosmos DB, and DevOps security are not available; within Defender CSPM, CIEM, the Data & AI security dashboard, EASM integration, ServiceNow integration, and code-to-runtime mapping are not available; Defender for Storage sensitive data threat detection is not available (DSPM sensitive data scanning is GA).

rows.control_intent: Perform ongoing and/or separate evaluations of whether internal control components are present and functioning (COSO Principle 16).
rows.capability_detail: An ongoing-evaluation mechanism for cloud-infrastructure controls: the regulatory compliance dashboard continuously assesses onboarded Azure/AWS/GCP resources against assigned standards (SOC 2 is a built-in standard) and the Microsoft cloud security benchmark, reporting per-control pass/fail with evidence-ready summary reports; secure score trends evidence whether technical controls are present and functioning over time
rows.how_it_supports: Supplies a continuous technical evaluation for the cloud slice of the control environment: one input to management's COSO Principle 16 evaluation program, not the program itself. Assessment-scope boundary: it evaluates onboarded cloud resources against technically-assessable criteria only: not M365 workloads, not manual/procedural controls, and it is not an attestation or a SOC examination. Compliance Manager (the stacked Purview row) is the M365-side counterpart, with complementary scopes.
rows.config_evidence_example: SOC 2 standard assigned to in-scope subscriptions (requires a paid plan); dashboard review integrated into the control-monitoring cadence
rows.operational_evidence_example: Per-standard compliance reports over time; secure score trend; remediation tracking from dashboard findings

related_microsoft.note: Purview Compliance Manager provides the parallel ongoing evaluation for the M365 estate (the stacked Purview row); complementary assessment scopes, a seam not an overlap

rows.external_dependencies: Management's evaluation program and internal audit; evaluation of manual/procedural controls; the service auditor's examination itself
rows.license_requirement: Regulatory compliance dashboard: the default Microsoft cloud security benchmark (MCSB) assessment is included free with Defender for Cloud. Assigning additional built-in or custom compliance standards requires at least one paid Defender for Cloud plan on the assessed scope, meaning any plan except Defender for Servers Plan 1 and Defender for APIs Plan 1.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Regulatory compliance dashboard: GA in Azure Government (configuration via the Azure portal experience; available standards vary by cloud, so verify the standard list in the target cloud).

rows.control_intent: Risk analysis (Required): accurately and thoroughly assess risks and vulnerabilities to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of ePHI.
rows.capability_detail: PHI/medical SITs + trainable classifiers locating ePHI across M365; DSPM data-risk analytics (oversharing, unprotected sensitive data)
rows.how_it_supports: Feeds the risk analysis its foundational input: where ePHI actually lives and how exposed it is. The analysis itself (threat modeling, likelihood/impact, documentation) is a program activity.
rows.config_evidence_example: PHI-relevant SITs enabled; DSPM data risk assessments scoped to clinical/PHI sites
rows.operational_evidence_example: Data explorer ePHI location export; DSPM risk assessment reports feeding the risk register

rows.external_dependencies: Enterprise risk-analysis methodology (e.g., NIST SP 800-30); non-M365 system discovery
rows.legacy_dependencies: Enterprise risk-analysis methodology (e.g., NIST SP 800-30); non-M365 system discovery

rows.control_intent: Access control, encryption and decryption (Addressable): implement a mechanism to encrypt and decrypt ePHI.

rows.how_it_supports: Implements item-level ePHI encryption with access rights enforcement, on top of inherited M365 service encryption. Encryption in EHR/clinical systems and databases is outside Purview.

Risk-based conditions additionally require Entra ID P2. Risky-sign-in detection: Microsoft Entra ID Protection (risk-based policies, risky users/sign-ins): Microsoft Entra ID P2 (included in Microsoft 365 E5/E7, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security), and Enterprise Mobility + Security E5) or Microsoft Entra Suite.

ID Protection risk detections, risky-account investigation, and SIEM connectivity: available in Azure Government; Microsoft Entra threat-intelligence detection is not available in gov clouds.

rows.control_intent: Access control: unique user identification, emergency access, automatic logoff, and encryption/decryption for systems holding ePHI.
rows.capability_detail: Unique identities in Entra ID (unique user identification) and Conditional Access sign-in-frequency/session controls (automatic logoff); emergency-access (break-glass) accounts
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the unique-user-identification and automatic-logoff specifications for Entra-integrated ePHI systems, plus break-glass for emergency access. Encryption is Purview/platform; EHR-native access control is separate.
rows.config_evidence_example: Unique-identity policy; Conditional Access session/sign-in-frequency controls; break-glass accounts
rows.operational_evidence_example: Sign-in logs (per-user attribution); session-timeout enforcement evidence

related_microsoft.note: Sensitivity-label encryption addresses the encryption/decryption specification for ePHI content

rows.external_dependencies: Automatic logoff / access control in the EHR; platform/data encryption
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Entra Conditional Access: Microsoft Entra ID P1 (included in Microsoft 365 E3/E5/E7, Microsoft 365 F1/F3, Enterprise Mobility + Security E3, and Business Premium). Risk-based conditions additionally require Entra ID P2.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Conditional Access: available in Azure Government (GCC, GCC High, DoD).

rows.control_intent: Person or entity authentication: verify that a person or entity seeking access to ePHI is the one claimed.
rows.capability_detail: Entra ID authentication with MFA and phishing-resistant methods; Conditional Access requiring verified authentication before ePHI app access
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements person/entity authentication for Entra-integrated ePHI systems: the primary control for §164.312(d). EHR-native authentication is the dependency.
rows.config_evidence_example: Authentication-strengths policy (phishing-resistant MFA); Conditional Access requiring MFA
rows.operational_evidence_example: Sign-in logs by authentication method; MFA-enforcement evidence

Risk-based conditions additionally require Entra ID P2.
rows.cloud_availability_note: MFA and authentication methods (incl. passwordless/FIDO2, certificate-based auth): available in Azure Government (GCC/GCC High/DoD). Conditional Access: available in Azure Government (GCC, GCC High, DoD).

rows.control_intent: Security awareness, protection from malicious software (Addressable): procedures for guarding against, detecting, and reporting malicious software.
rows.capability_detail: Antivirus policy managing Microsoft Defender Antivirus on endpoints that access ePHI (real-time protection, scheduled scans, security-intelligence updates, tamper protection); compliance policies requiring anti-malware and an acceptable threat level
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the guarding/detecting configuration layer on managed endpoints: enforcing that anti-malware runs, stays current, and cannot be tampered with. The detection engine itself is Microsoft Defender; awareness training and reporting procedures are organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Antivirus policy with real-time and tamper protection; compliance policy requiring AV/threat level
rows.operational_evidence_example: Antivirus policy status reports; compliance reports showing AV state on ePHI-accessing devices
