rows.control_intent: Workstation use and workstation security: specify proper workstation functions, manner of use, and surroundings, and implement safeguards restricting workstations that access ePHI to authorized users.
rows.capability_detail: Configuration profiles technically enforcing workstation-use policy (screen lock and idle timeout, device restrictions, hardened settings) and compliance policies requiring credentials so only authorized users unlock managed workstations
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the technical-enforcement layer of the workstation-use and workstation-security standards on managed workstations. These are physical safeguards at heart; the written policies, workstation positioning, and facility controls remain organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Hardening/restriction profiles incl. screen lock and idle timeout on ePHI-accessing workstations; compliance credential requirements
rows.operational_evidence_example: Per-setting configuration status reports; compliance reports for workstation population
rows.external_dependencies: Written workstation-use policies; physical safeguards (positioning, privacy screens, facility access)
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Intune Plan 1 (the base Intune service; included in Microsoft 365 bundles such as E3/E5/E7 and Enterprise Mobility + Security E3/E5, also available standalone)

rows.control_intent: Device and media controls: govern the receipt, removal, and movement of hardware and electronic media containing ePHI (Standard); maintain a record of hardware/media movements and the persons responsible (Accountability, Addressable).
rows.capability_detail: Device inventory tying each enrolled device to its primary user (the accountability record for managed hardware); enrollment and retire/wipe lifecycle recording devices entering and leaving management; remote wipe/retire removing ePHI before disposal or re-use
rows.how_it_supports: Implements receipt/removal governance and the accountability record for Intune-managed devices: an auditable per-device trail of who holds it and when it left service. Loose electronic media (drives, tapes) and facility movement logging remain organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Enrollment coverage for ePHI-accessing devices; device-action workflow for offboarding hardware
rows.operational_evidence_example: All-devices inventory export with primary users; retire/wipe action history for disposed devices

rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Intune Plan 1 (the base Intune service; included in Microsoft 365 bundles such as E3/E5/E7 and Enterprise Mobility + Security E3/E5, also available standalone)

rows.control_intent: Access control, encryption and decryption (Addressable): implement a mechanism to encrypt and decrypt ePHI.
rows.capability_detail: Disk-encryption policy enforcing BitLocker/FileVault on workstations, compliance policies requiring encryption on mobile devices, and app protection policies encrypting org data inside managed mobile apps
rows.how_it_supports: Enforces and attests the device-level encryption mechanism for ePHI on managed endpoints: the OS performs the encryption; Intune mandates, escrows recovery keys, and reports it. ePHI in EHR/clinical systems and databases is a separate layer; content-level M365 encryption is the Purview seam.
rows.config_evidence_example: Disk-encryption profiles on ePHI-accessing devices; compliance policy requiring encryption; app protection policies for mobile ePHI access
rows.operational_evidence_example: Encryption status report; compliance-policy encryption state per device

related_microsoft.note: Label-based encryption and Customer Key implement content-layer ePHI encryption in M365

rows.external_dependencies: EHR/database encryption; enterprise key management; encryption for unmanaged devices
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Intune Plan 1 (the base Intune service; included in Microsoft 365 bundles such as E3/E5/E7 and Enterprise Mobility + Security E3/E5, also available standalone)

rows.control_intent: Risk analysis (Required): accurately and thoroughly assess risks and vulnerabilities to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of ePHI.
rows.capability_detail: Defender Vulnerability Management: continuous vulnerability and misconfiguration assessment with exposure scoring for the devices that create, store, or access ePHI; threat analytics correlating active threat campaigns with the organization's exposure
rows.how_it_supports: Feeds the risk analysis its technical-vulnerability input (which systems handling ePHI are exposed, to what, at what severity) alongside the Purview row's where-does-ePHI-live input. The risk analysis itself (threat modeling, likelihood/impact, documentation) is a program activity.
rows.config_evidence_example: Devices handling ePHI onboarded with MDVM assessments active; exposure-score reporting into the risk-analysis cadence
rows.operational_evidence_example: Vulnerability and exposure exports cited in the risk-analysis workpapers; threat analytics exposure reports

related_microsoft.note: Data classification and DSPM supply the where-ePHI-lives and how-exposed input (the stacked Purview row)

rows.external_dependencies: Risk-analysis methodology and documentation; vulnerability data for non-endpoint systems (medical devices, infrastructure)
rows.license_requirement: Defender Vulnerability Management core capabilities: included with Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 2 (Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or standalone P2). Premium capabilities (security baselines assessment, block vulnerable applications, browser-extension/certificate/hardware-firmware assessment) require the Defender Vulnerability Management add-on, available to Defender for Endpoint Plan 2, Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Defender Suite/EDU/GOV/FLW, Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW, and Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5/G5 customers; an MDVM standalone exists for non-P2 customers.

rows.control_intent: Information system activity review (Required): regularly review records of system activity: audit logs, access reports, incident tracking.
rows.capability_detail: The security-incident-tracking slice of activity review: unified incident queue with classification/resolution history, device and identity timelines, advanced hunting over 30 days of raw cross-workload telemetry, and exportable incident/alert reports for review cadences
rows.how_it_supports: Produces the incident tracking reports the control names and the review surface for security activity on systems handling ePHI, stacking with the Purview row's audit-log/access-report slice. The documented review procedure and non-Microsoft system logs remain organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Incident queue review cadence documented; hunting queries saved for recurring reviews; alert/incident report exports scheduled
rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident review records with classification and resolution; hunting/review evidence exports

related_microsoft.note: Unified audit log and Audit (Premium) crucial events are the audit-log/access-report core of this control (the stacked Purview row)
Sentinel retains and reviews logs from non-Microsoft systems handling ePHI

rows.external_dependencies: Documented review procedure and cadence; logs from non-Microsoft/clinical systems
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 2: standalone or included in Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW.

rows.control_intent: Security awareness, protection from malicious software (Addressable): procedures for guarding against, detecting, and reporting malicious software.
rows.capability_detail: Microsoft Defender Antivirus next-generation protection (real-time, behavior-based, cloud-delivered) on endpoints accessing ePHI; attack surface reduction rules blocking malware vectors; Safe Attachments/Safe Links stopping malicious content at the email entry point; malware detection reporting for the procedures' reporting element
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements guarding against and detecting malicious software: the engine layer under the Intune policy-management row on this safeguard. Reporting procedures and the awareness-program context are organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: AV active mode with cloud protection and tamper protection; ASR rules in block mode; Safe Attachments/Safe Links applied to workforce mailboxes
rows.operational_evidence_example: Malware detection and remediation reports; blocked-attachment reports; AV health/currency reports

related_microsoft.note: Intune antivirus and compliance policies configure, enforce, and attest the anti-malware layer on managed devices (the stacked Intune row)

rows.external_dependencies: Malicious-software reporting procedures; awareness training; anti-malware for unmanaged/clinical systems
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 1: standalone or included in Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3. P1 covers next-gen antivirus, attack surface reduction, device control, endpoint firewall, network protection, and manual response actions. EDR, automated investigation & remediation, vulnerability management, and threat analytics require Plan 2. Microsoft Defender for Office 365 Plan 1: standalone, Microsoft 365 Business Premium, Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Office 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW. Effective July 1, 2026 it is also included in Microsoft 365 E3/G3 and Office 365 E3/G3; that rollout began June 2026 and Microsoft expects it to complete during 2026.

rows.control_intent: Security incident procedures, response and reporting (Required): identify and respond to suspected/known incidents, mitigate, and document incidents and outcomes.
rows.capability_detail: Incident identification via cross-workload alert correlation; response and mitigation tooling: device isolation, file quarantine, account disable, mailbox remediation, automated investigation & remediation; Action center providing the auditable record of every response action for incident documentation
rows.how_it_supports: Implements identify/respond/mitigate/document for security incidents touching the Microsoft estate, the operational counterpart to the Purview row's forensic-evidence role. Breach-notification decisioning under the Breach Notification Rule and the procedures themselves are organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: AIR remediation levels set; incident notification rules to the response team; response playbooks referencing Defender actions
rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident records with timeline, actions taken (Action center), and resolution classification; documented outcomes per incident

related_microsoft.note: Audit (Premium) scope-of-access forensics and eDiscovery evidence preservation document the incident (the stacked Purview row)
Sentinel SOAR extends response to non-Microsoft systems

rows.external_dependencies: Incident response procedures; HIPAA Breach Notification Rule assessment and notification duties; response for non-Microsoft/clinical systems
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 2: standalone or included in Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW.

rows.control_intent: Information system activity review (Required): regularly review records of system activity: audit logs, access reports, incident tracking.
rows.capability_detail: The activity-review engine for every system handling ePHI: M365 audit records (via the free Office 365 connector), EHR/clinical-system and infrastructure logs (via Syslog/CEF-AMA), and Defender signals reviewed continuously by scheduled analytics rules rather than periodic manual sampling; workbooks as the recurring review surface; the incident queue as the incident-tracking record the control names
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements the regularly-review activity this control names: automated, continuous review of audit logs, access reports, and incident tracking across the whole ePHI estate, including the EHR/clinical-system logs both stacked rows defer outward. The documented review procedure, cadence, and reviewer assignment remain organizational, and unconnected systems stay outside the review.
rows.config_evidence_example: EHR/clinical log sources connected via Syslog/CEF-AMA alongside the Office 365 and Defender connectors; scheduled analytics covering the review checklist; review workbooks with sign-off procedure
rows.operational_evidence_example: Analytics rule run history as continuous-review evidence; dated workbook review notes; incident-tracking exports

related_microsoft.note: Purview Audit generates the M365 audit-log and access-report records this review consumes (the stacked Purview row)
Defender XDR supplies the security-incident-tracking slice for Microsoft workloads (the stacked Defender row)

rows.external_dependencies: Documented review procedure, cadence, and assigned reviewer; log generation on EHR/clinical systems
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027. Free data sources: Office 365 audit activity (SharePoint/Exchange/Teams), Azure Activity, and all Microsoft Defender security alerts/incidents ingest at no charge. Microsoft 365 E7/E5/A5/F5/G5 and E5 Security tenants additionally get a data grant of up to 5 MB/user/day covering Entra ID sign-in/audit logs, Defender for Cloud Apps shadow-IT discovery, Purview Information Protection logs, and Defender XDR advanced-hunting tables; raw Microsoft logs beyond the grant are paid ingestion.

rows.control_intent: Audit controls: implement mechanisms that record and examine activity in systems containing or using ePHI.
rows.capability_detail: The record-and-examine mechanism at estate scope: connectors capture activity from every system containing or using ePHI (EHR and clinical applications, database and server logs via AMA, network devices via CEF/Syslog, and M365 via the Office 365 connector) into one examinable store with KQL as the examination surface and per-table retention holding the record for the required lookback
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements the audit-controls mechanism for the systems the stacked Purview row defers: Purview Audit is the mechanism inside M365; Sentinel is the mechanism that records and examines activity across the clinical and infrastructure estate, and the single place both records are examined together. Source systems must emit activity records; Sentinel cannot record what a system never logs.
rows.config_evidence_example: Connector coverage mapped to the ePHI system inventory; DCRs selecting security-relevant events; retention aligned to the organization's HIPAA documentation retention decisions
rows.operational_evidence_example: Connector health per ePHI source; KQL examination query library; per-table retention configuration export

related_microsoft.note: Purview Audit is the audit-controls mechanism within M365 itself (the stacked Purview row); seam: Purview records M365, Sentinel records and examines the estate

rows.external_dependencies: ePHI system inventory; native audit logging enabled on EHR/clinical systems; examination procedure
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027. Consumption-priced retention: analytics-tier retention beyond the included 90 days is billed per GB (extendable to 2 years); Microsoft Sentinel data lake total retention up to 12 years is billed per compressed GB/month (6:1 compression assumption) plus per-GB query/processing meters when archived data is scanned. These are the meters behind 12-month (PCI DSS) and multi-year (CMMC/800-53) retention mandates.

rows.control_intent: Security incident procedures, response and reporting (Required): identify and respond to suspected/known incidents, mitigate, and document incidents and outcomes.
rows.capability_detail: Identify-respond-document across the ePHI estate: analytics detect suspected incidents on any connected source including clinical systems neither Purview nor Defender sees; SOAR automation orchestrates the response procedure (triage, notification, containment calls into enforcement products); the incident record (timeline, tasks, actions, resolution) is the documented-outcomes artifact the control requires, retained per workspace policy
rows.how_it_supports: Implements identification for the estate beyond Microsoft workloads and the document-incidents-and-outcomes obligation as a durable, exportable record. Sentinel orchestrates response; mitigation executes in the enforcement products, and breach-notification decisioning under the Breach Notification Rule stays organizational; the stacked Purview and Defender rows carry the forensic and Microsoft-estate response slices.
rows.config_evidence_example: Analytics covering clinical/infrastructure sources; response playbooks per incident type; incident task templates encoding the procedure
rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident documentation exports (timeline, actions, outcomes); playbook run history; incident metrics for the review cadence

related_microsoft.note: Audit (Premium) scope-of-access forensics and eDiscovery preservation document what ePHI was touched (the stacked Purview row)
Defender XDR identifies and remediates Microsoft-estate incidents; Sentinel extends identification and documentation to the rest (the stacked Defender row)

rows.external_dependencies: Incident response procedures; HIPAA Breach Notification Rule assessment and notification duties
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027. Microsoft Sentinel automation rules are included with the workspace (consumption-priced service); Logic Apps playbooks are billed separately under Azure Logic Apps consumption meters.

rows.control_intent: Risk analysis (Required): accurately and thoroughly assess risks and vulnerabilities to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of ePHI.
rows.capability_detail: Risk-analysis input for ePHI hosted on cloud infrastructure: agentless vulnerability scanning across VMs/containers/databases, attack path analysis tracing exploitable chains from internet exposure toward data stores, sensitive data discovery locating cloud storage and databases that contain health-data-pattern sensitive information (Purview SIT-powered), and risk prioritization ranking findings by real exploitability
rows.how_it_supports: Feeds the risk analysis with continuously refreshed technical evidence for the cloud slice of the ePHI estate: where ePHI-bearing resources are, what is vulnerable, and which attack paths reach them. The risk analysis itself (scope, likelihood/impact determination, documentation across all ePHI locations including M365 and on-premises) is an organizational obligation the stacked rows also feed.
rows.config_evidence_example: Defender CSPM with agentless scanning and sensitive data discovery enabled on subscriptions hosting ePHI workloads; health-data SITs active in data sensitivity settings
rows.operational_evidence_example: Attack-path and vulnerability reports scoped to ePHI resources; sensitive-data discovery findings; risk-prioritized recommendation exports for the risk-analysis file

related_microsoft.note: MDVM supplies the endpoint-device vulnerability input to the same risk analysis (the stacked Defender XDR row)
Purview classifies and protects ePHI in M365; its SITs power Defender for Cloud's sensitive data discovery; different classification scopes, one taxonomy

rows.external_dependencies: The documented risk analysis itself (scope, likelihood, impact, all ePHI locations); on-premises clinical system assessment; BAA/contractual scope
rows.license_requirement: Defender CSPM: consumption-priced per billable resource per month. It protects all multicloud workloads but bills only on servers, storage accounts, databases (OSS/SQL incl. servers-on-machines), and serverless resources (conversion ratios apply). 30-day free trial on first enablement; a 1-year pre-purchase plan (Commit Units) discounts up to 22%.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Azure Government gaps: Defender for APIs, Defender for App Service, AI Services threat protection, Defender for Cosmos DB, and DevOps security are not available; within Defender CSPM, CIEM, the Data & AI security dashboard, EASM integration, ServiceNow integration, and code-to-runtime mapping are not available; Defender for Storage sensitive data threat detection is not available (DSPM sensitive data scanning is GA).

rows.control_intent: Evaluation: perform periodic technical and nontechnical evaluations of security safeguards against Security Rule requirements.
rows.capability_detail: Continuous technical evaluation for cloud-hosted ePHI workloads: the regulatory compliance dashboard assesses onboarded Azure/AWS/GCP resources against assignable built-in standards including HIPAA and HITRUST CSF, reporting per-control pass/fail with remediation recommendations and downloadable reports: a standing technical-evaluation mechanism rather than a point-in-time review
rows.how_it_supports: Contributes the technical-evaluation half of this control for the cloud resource scope only. Assessment-scope boundary: it evaluates cloud resources onboarded to Defender for Cloud against the standard's technically-assessable subset. It does not evaluate M365 workloads (where much ePHI lives, the stacked Purview row's Compliance Manager territory), on-premises clinical systems, or the nontechnical/procedural half of the evaluation, and it is not an attestation or audit.
rows.config_evidence_example: HIPAA/HITRUST standard assigned to ePHI-hosting subscriptions (requires a paid plan); evaluation cadence documented in the security program
rows.operational_evidence_example: Per-standard compliance reports retained as evaluation records; control pass/fail history; remediation-tracking evidence

related_microsoft.note: Purview Compliance Manager carries the HIPAA assessment for the M365 estate with improvement actions (the stacked Purview row); complementary evaluation scopes, a seam not an overlap

rows.external_dependencies: Nontechnical (administrative/procedural) evaluation; evaluation of M365 and on-premises safeguards; the documented evaluation program
rows.license_requirement: Regulatory compliance dashboard: the default Microsoft cloud security benchmark (MCSB) assessment is included free with Defender for Cloud. Assigning additional built-in or custom compliance standards requires at least one paid Defender for Cloud plan on the assessed scope, meaning any plan except Defender for Servers Plan 1 and Defender for APIs Plan 1.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Regulatory compliance dashboard: GA in Azure Government (configuration via the Azure portal experience; available standards vary by cloud, so verify the standard list in the target cloud).

rows.control_intent: Integrity: protect ePHI from improper alteration or destruction, with mechanisms to corroborate integrity ((c)(2) Addressable).
rows.capability_detail: Integrity-corroboration mechanisms for servers hosting ePHI: file integrity monitoring (Plan 2) examines operating system files, registries, and application files for changes indicating tampering, with a retained change record; ransomware-behavior and fileless-attack detection alerts on the destruction/alteration attack classes; Defender for Storage detects anomalous mass-modification and deletion patterns on ePHI-bearing storage
rows.how_it_supports: Supplies the corroborate-integrity mechanism the (c)(2) implementation specification names, for cloud/hybrid server and storage estates: unauthorized change is detected and evidenced, not prevented. Preventing improper alteration is the access-control and backup story (stacked and external rows); FIM detects and records, and its scope is machines with Plan 2 and monitored paths.
rows.config_evidence_example: Defender for Servers Plan 2 with file integrity monitoring enabled on ePHI-hosting servers; monitored-path rules covering ePHI application/system files; Defender for Storage on ePHI storage accounts
rows.operational_evidence_example: FIM change reports for monitored entities; tamper/ransomware alert history; storage anomaly alert records

related_microsoft.note: Purview retention/records controls protect M365-resident ePHI from improper deletion (the stacked Purview row); different plane: M365 content vs server file systems

rows.external_dependencies: Backup/recovery for restoring integrity (Azure Backup, outside this product); access controls preventing alteration; database-level integrity mechanisms
rows.license_requirement: Defender for Servers Plan 2: consumption-priced per protected server per hour, billed monthly. Includes everything in Plan 1 (incl. the integrated MDE for Servers license) plus agentless machine scanning, premium Defender Vulnerability Management capabilities, file integrity monitoring, just-in-time VM access, OS baseline/system-updates assessment, DNS-layer alerts, and a 500 MB/day free security-data ingestion allowance on the associated Log Analytics/Microsoft Sentinel workspace (applies to designated security tables).
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender for Servers P1/P2: GA in Azure Government; file integrity monitoring GA (not supported in GovCon Cloud Moderate).

rows.control_intent: Security awareness, protection from malicious software (Addressable): procedures for guarding against, detecting, and reporting malicious software.
rows.capability_detail: Malicious-software protection for the server side of clinical/ePHI workloads: Defender for Servers deploys the integrated MDE anti-malware engine on Windows/Linux servers across Azure/AWS/GCP/Arc (EHR application servers, database servers) with endpoint-protection assessment verifying agents are present and healthy; Defender for Storage malware scanning guards ePHI file exchange through cloud storage, a common ransomware entry path in healthcare
rows.how_it_supports: Extends the guarding-and-detecting procedures to server estates licensed per server and to the cloud-storage upload path. The engine on machines is Defender Antivirus under MDE (the stacked Defender XDR row); Intune enforces device AV policy (the stacked Intune row); the awareness/training half of §164.308(a)(5) remains organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Defender for Servers on ePHI-hosting subscriptions; storage malware scanning on file-exchange accounts; unhealthy-protection recommendations remediated
rows.operational_evidence_example: Server protection coverage reports; malware detection and storage-scan verdict logs; reporting-procedure records fed by alert notifications

related_microsoft.note: Defender Antivirus/MDE is the anti-malware engine (the stacked Defender XDR row); Defender for Cloud is the per-server deployment/licensing vehicle plus the storage-scanning plane
Intune enforces AV policy on managed clinical endpoints (the stacked Intune row)

rows.external_dependencies: Security awareness and training program; malware procedures for devices outside cloud/endpoint management
rows.license_requirement: Defender for Servers Plan 1: consumption-priced per protected server per hour, billed monthly. Plan 1 centers on the integrated Microsoft Defender for Endpoint for Servers license (EDR); customers already licensed for MDE for Servers can request a price adjustment through Azure support (the MDE portion is not double-charged). Consumption-priced per protected resource, metered by plan: Defender for Storage per storage account/month plus per-transaction overage above 73M/month and per-GB malware scanning; Defender for Databases per database server (Cosmos DB per provisioned RU); Defender for Containers per vCore of Kubernetes worker nodes (includes a monthly image-scan allowance); Defender for App Service per app instance; Defender for Key Vault per vault/month; Defender for Resource Manager per subscription/month; Defender for APIs by call-volume plan tier (Plans 1–5); AI Services per 1K tokens. 30-day free trial on first enablement (malware scanning excluded).
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender for Servers P1/P2: GA in Azure Government; file integrity monitoring GA (not supported in GovCon Cloud Moderate).

rows.control_intent: Control the flow of CUI per approved authorizations: regulate where CUI can travel within and between systems.
rows.capability_detail: DLP policies keyed to CUI labels/SITs across Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, Teams + Endpoint DLP; sharing restricted to authorized destinations
rows.how_it_supports: Implements CUI flow enforcement for the M365 enclave: blocks unauthorized email/sharing/upload/USB flows, allows authorized ones. Network-level flow control (boundary devices, cross-domain) is outside.

rows.how_it_supports: Implements the port/device-class layer of removable-media control on managed Windows endpoints. Content-aware control of what CUI reaches allowed media is the Purview Endpoint DLP seam; enforcement on the endpoint is performed by Defender device control.
rows.config_evidence_example: Device-control policy (allow/deny classes, read-only); BitLocker To Go requirements
rows.operational_evidence_example: Device-control block/audit events; policy status reports

related_microsoft.note: Endpoint DLP applies content-aware audit/warn/block for CUI copied to allowed removable media
Defender for Endpoint device control performs the on-device enforcement the Intune policy configures

rows.external_dependencies: Media marking/handling procedures; physical control of media
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Intune Plan 1 (the base Intune service; included in Microsoft 365 bundles such as E3/E5/E7 and Enterprise Mobility + Security E3/E5, also available standalone)
rows.cloud_availability_note: Endpoint security policies, incl. Defender for Endpoint security settings management, are supported in GCC, GCC High, and DoD.

rows.control_intent: Protect the confidentiality of CUI at rest.
rows.capability_detail: Disk-encryption policy enforcing BitLocker/FileVault on endpoints storing CUI, with recovery-key escrow and per-device attestation; compliance policies requiring encryption before access
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the endpoint-storage slice of CUI-at-rest protection: enforcing and attesting platform encryption on managed devices. At-rest protection inside M365 content and non-endpoint stores are other layers (content encryption is the Purview seam).
rows.config_evidence_example: Disk-encryption profiles across CUI-handling endpoints; compliance policy requiring encryption
rows.operational_evidence_example: Encryption status report; compliance encryption state per device

related_microsoft.note: Label-based encryption and Customer Key protect CUI at rest at the content layer in M365

rows.external_dependencies: M365 service encryption (inherited); encryption for servers and non-M365 stores
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Intune Plan 1 (the base Intune service; included in Microsoft 365 bundles such as E3/E5/E7 and Enterprise Mobility + Security E3/E5, also available standalone)
rows.cloud_availability_note: Core Intune MDM (compliance policies, configuration profiles, app policies) is available in GCC High/DoD; Intune for GCC runs on the commercial Intune instance.

rows.control_intent: Verify and control/limit connections to and use of external systems.
rows.capability_detail: Cloud Discovery identifying which external cloud services users actually connect to (from Defender for Endpoint and network telemetry), risk scoring against the Cloud App Catalog, sanction/unsanction governance, and unsanctioned-app blocking enforced on endpoints via Defender for Endpoint
rows.how_it_supports: Implements verify-and-control for the external-SaaS class of external systems: discovery of actual usage, risk evaluation, and block enforcement. External systems reached outside SaaS (partner networks, portable storage on foreign systems) and the organizational use policy remain external.
rows.config_evidence_example: Cloud Discovery via MDE integration; sanctioned/unsanctioned app policy; endpoint enforcement of unsanctioned blocks
rows.operational_evidence_example: Discovered-app reports with risk scores; unsanctioned-app block events; app-governance review records

related_microsoft.note: Conditional Access and app-consent governance limit which external cloud apps organizational identities can use

rows.external_dependencies: Policy on use of external systems; firewall/proxy controls for non-SaaS external connections; CUI flow-down terms
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps: standalone or included in Enterprise Mobility + Security E5, Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, Microsoft Purview Suite/EDU/GOV/FLW, Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW, or Microsoft 365 E5/F5/G5 Information Protection and Governance. Conditional Access App Control additionally requires Microsoft Entra ID P1 licensing.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender for Cloud Apps: available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD as dedicated gov offerings (commercial-tenant MDCA must transition to the GCC version for gov eligibility).

rows.control_intent: Operate an incident-handling capability (preparation through recovery); track, document, and report incidents to designated officials/authorities.
rows.capability_detail: Operational incident handling for the Microsoft estate: cross-workload detection correlated into incidents, response tooling (device isolation, account disable, file quarantine, mailbox remediation), automated investigation & remediation, and Action center records giving each incident a tracked, documented timeline
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the detect/analyze/contain/eradicate phases and produces the incident tracking/documentation the requirement demands, stacking with the Purview row's forensic-scoping and preservation role. The IR capability itself, DFARS 252.204-7012 72-hour DIBNet reporting, and recovery procedures are organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: AIR remediation levels per device group; incident notification rules; IR playbooks referencing Defender response actions
rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident records with timeline, Action center actions, and resolution classification; monthly incident metrics

related_microsoft.note: Audit (Premium) scope-of-access forensics and eDiscovery preservation document which CUI an incident touched (the stacked Purview row)
Sentinel extends incident handling to non-Microsoft log sources

rows.external_dependencies: IR plan and playbooks; DFARS/DIBNet reporting duties; recovery procedures
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 2: standalone or included in Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender XDR (unified portal, incidents, advanced hunting, attack disruption): available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD; Microsoft Threat Experts is not available in gov clouds; preview features are commercial-only.

rows.control_intent: Scan for vulnerabilities in organizational systems and applications periodically and when new vulnerabilities are identified; remediate vulnerabilities in accordance with risk assessments.
rows.capability_detail: Defender Vulnerability Management: continuous agent-based vulnerability assessment (endpoints/servers onboarded to Defender) with automatic reassessment as new CVEs are disclosed, application-level vulnerability detection via software inventory, risk-based prioritization (exploit availability, threat context, asset exposure), and remediation requests with tracking
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the scanning and risk-prioritized remediation cycle for the onboarded estate: continuous assessment exceeds 'periodic', and new-CVE matching satisfies the when-new-vulnerabilities-identified trigger. Network devices, printers/scanners, and non-onboarded systems need complementary scanning.
rows.config_evidence_example: MDVM assessments active across the CUI enclave's devices; remediation workflow integrated with ticketing; risk-acceptance records for exceptions
rows.operational_evidence_example: Vulnerability assessment exports on a defined cadence; remediation tracking history against risk-assessment priorities

related_microsoft.note: Intune update rings and configuration remediate the vulnerabilities MDVM prioritizes

rows.external_dependencies: Scanning for network devices, printers, and non-onboarded systems; documented risk assessment driving remediation priorities
rows.license_requirement: Defender Vulnerability Management core capabilities: included with Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 2 (Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or standalone P2). Premium capabilities (security baselines assessment, block vulnerable applications, browser-extension/certificate/hardware-firmware assessment) require the Defender Vulnerability Management add-on, available to Defender for Endpoint Plan 2, Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Defender Suite/EDU/GOV/FLW, Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW, and Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5/G5 customers; an MDVM standalone exists for non-P2 customers.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender Vulnerability Management core (with MDE Plan 2): available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD; the MDVM add-on trial is not available in GCC High/DoD, so confirm add-on purchase options for gov tenants.

rows.control_intent: Provide protection from malicious code at designated locations within organizational systems.
rows.capability_detail: Microsoft Defender Antivirus next-generation protection at the endpoint/server location (real-time, behavior-based, cloud-delivered, tamper-protected) across Windows/macOS/Linux; Safe Attachments/Safe Links at the email and collaboration entry/exit location (Defender for Office 365); attack surface reduction rules hardening common malware vectors
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements malicious-code protection at the two designated locations the discussion emphasizes: system entry/exit points (email, web) and endpoints/servers. Perimeter-appliance malware inspection for non-M365 mail routes remains external.
rows.config_evidence_example: AV active mode with cloud protection on all enclave devices; ASR rules in block mode; Safe Attachments/Safe Links on all mailboxes
rows.operational_evidence_example: Malware detection/remediation reports; blocked-attachment reports; AV health reports per device

related_microsoft.note: Intune antivirus policy configures and attests the Defender Antivirus settings on managed devices

rows.external_dependencies: Malware protection at non-Microsoft boundary devices (perimeter gateways); coverage for non-onboarded systems
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 1: standalone or included in Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3. P1 covers next-gen antivirus, attack surface reduction, device control, endpoint firewall, network protection, and manual response actions. EDR, automated investigation & remediation, vulnerability management, and threat analytics require Plan 2. Microsoft Defender for Office 365 Plan 1: standalone, Microsoft 365 Business Premium, Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Office 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW. Effective July 1, 2026 it is also included in Microsoft 365 E3/G3 and Office 365 E3/G3; that rollout began June 2026 and Microsoft expects it to complete during 2026.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender for Endpoint: available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD (G3 includes Plan 1; G5/G5 Security include Plan 2); some capabilities lag commercial, so verify per feature on the MDE US Government page.

rows.control_intent: Monitor system security alerts and advisories and take action in response.
rows.capability_detail: Threat analytics delivering Microsoft-researcher advisories (active campaigns, actors, techniques) pre-correlated with the organization's incidents, impacted assets, and exposure state, with recommended mitigations tracked to completion; incident alerts as the operational alert stream
rows.how_it_supports: Implements a monitored, actioned advisory channel for the Microsoft estate: each advisory arrives with the tenant's own exposure and a mitigation worklist. External advisory sources (CISA, vendors outside Microsoft) and the organizational response procedure remain external.
rows.config_evidence_example: Threat analytics review cadence; mitigation tracking from advisory reports; alert notification rules
rows.operational_evidence_example: Threat analytics review records with mitigation status; advisory-driven remediation history

related_microsoft.note: Sentinel ingests non-Microsoft advisories and threat intelligence feeds

rows.external_dependencies: External advisory subscriptions (CISA, US-CERT, vendor bulletins); documented response procedure

rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender XDR (unified portal, incidents, advanced hunting, attack disruption): available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD; Microsoft Threat Experts is not available in gov clouds; preview features are commercial-only.

rows.control_intent: Update malicious code protection mechanisms when new releases are available; perform periodic scans of systems and real-time scans of files from external sources as they are downloaded, opened, or executed.
rows.capability_detail: Cloud-delivered protection with security-intelligence updates multiple times daily and engine/platform auto-updates; real-time on-access scanning of files as they are downloaded, opened, or executed; scheduled periodic full/quick scans; scanning of archives and removable media
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements both requirements (current protection mechanisms and the periodic + real-time scanning regime) on onboarded systems. Update/scan configuration for non-Microsoft AV on other systems remains external.
rows.config_evidence_example: Cloud protection enabled with daily-or-faster intelligence updates; scheduled scan policy; real-time protection enforced and tamper-protected
rows.operational_evidence_example: AV currency reports (engine/definition versions per device); scheduled-scan completion reports; on-access detection events

related_microsoft.note: Intune antivirus policy sets the update cadence and scan schedule Defender executes

rows.external_dependencies: Equivalent update/scan evidence for systems not running Defender

EDR, automated investigation & remediation, vulnerability management, and threat analytics require Plan 2.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender for Endpoint: available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD (G3 includes Plan 1; G5/G5 Security include Plan 2); some capabilities lag commercial, so verify per feature on the MDE US Government page.

rows.control_intent: Monitor organizational systems, including inbound and outbound communications traffic, to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.
rows.capability_detail: Attack detection across the estate: EDR behavioral sensors on endpoints/servers with device network-connection telemetry, network protection inspecting outbound web traffic, Defender for Office 365 monitoring inbound/outbound mail flow, Defender for Identity watching AD authentication traffic for attack indicators, correlated into unified incidents
rows.how_it_supports: Implements system monitoring and the mail/web/endpoint slices of communications-traffic monitoring with attack-indicator detection. Network-boundary traffic analysis (firewall logs, NDR, netflow) for non-endpoint paths requires SIEM/network tooling.
rows.config_evidence_example: EDR onboarding coverage across the enclave; network protection in block mode; MDO policies on all mail; MDI sensors on all DCs
rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident and detection trend reports; network-protection block events; mail-flow threat reports

related_microsoft.note: Insider Risk Management detects unauthorized data activity inside M365 (the stacked Purview 3.14.7 seam)
Sentinel monitors firewall/netflow and non-Microsoft communications paths

rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Defender XDR has no separate license; the unified portal and correlation come with any qualifying workload license (Microsoft 365 E5/A5; Microsoft 365 E3 with the Microsoft Defender Suite or Enterprise Mobility + Security E5 add-on; Microsoft 365 A3 with the A5 Security add-on; Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5; Enterprise Mobility + Security E5/A5; Office 365 E5/A5; Microsoft 365 Business Premium; or standalone Defender workload licenses). Automatic attack disruption and threat analytics require Defender for Endpoint Plan 2.

rows.control_intent: Identify unauthorized use of organizational systems.
rows.capability_detail: EDR detecting unauthorized software execution, persistence mechanisms, and anomalous process/network behavior on systems; Defender for Identity flagging unauthorized identity use in on-prem AD (unusual authentication, privilege abuse, lateral movement); Defender for Cloud Apps detecting anomalous SaaS usage (impossible travel, unusual download patterns)
rows.how_it_supports: Implements system-level unauthorized-use identification: the layer the stacked Purview row explicitly defers to Defender (its scope is unauthorized *data* use inside M365). Definitions of authorized use and non-Microsoft system coverage remain organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: EDR in block mode; MDI sensors deployed; MDCA anomaly policies tuned to enclave apps; custom detections for org-specific unauthorized-use patterns
rows.operational_evidence_example: Unauthorized-use detection alerts with resolution; MDI/MDCA anomaly reports

related_microsoft.note: Insider Risk Management identifies unauthorized data use via behavioral analytics (the stacked Purview row)
Entra ID Protection flags anomalous cloud sign-ins, the cloud seam of MDI's on-prem coverage

rows.external_dependencies: Authorized-use definitions (policy); monitoring for non-Microsoft systems
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Defender XDR has no separate license; the unified portal and correlation come with any qualifying workload license (Microsoft 365 E5/A5; Microsoft 365 E3 with the Microsoft Defender Suite or Enterprise Mobility + Security E5 add-on; Microsoft 365 A3 with the A5 Security add-on; Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5; Enterprise Mobility + Security E5/A5; Office 365 E5/A5; Microsoft 365 Business Premium; or standalone Defender workload licenses). Automatic attack disruption and threat analytics require Defender for Endpoint Plan 2.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender XDR (unified portal, incidents, advanced hunting, attack disruption): available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD; Microsoft Threat Experts is not available in gov clouds; preview features are commercial-only.

rows.control_intent: Create and retain system audit logs/records sufficient for monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting of unlawful or unauthorized activity.
rows.capability_detail: Estate-wide audit-record collection and retention for the CUI enclave: Windows Security Events via AMA from enclave servers, CEF/Syslog from network devices, the Office 365 and Defender connectors for Microsoft workloads, retained per table on the analytics tier (to 2 years) and in the data lake (to 12 years) to whatever period the enclave's audit-retention policy defines
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements create-and-retain at enclave scope: the aggregation and retention layer for the non-M365 sources the stacked Purview row defers (endpoints, network, servers). The retention decision itself and log generation at each source remain the organization's; the multi-year retention CMMC assessors expect is a consumption cost driven by the data-lake meters, not a license tier.
rows.config_evidence_example: Connector coverage mapped to the enclave system inventory; DCRs selecting auditable events; per-table retention matching the documented audit-retention period
rows.operational_evidence_example: Connector health per source; retention configuration export; ingestion volume trend as records-are-being-created evidence

related_microsoft.note: Purview Audit creates and retains the M365 audit record the Office 365 connector ingests (the stacked Purview row)

rows.external_dependencies: Documented audit-retention period; native audit logging enabled on enclave systems; time synchronization
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027. Consumption-priced retention: analytics-tier retention beyond the included 90 days is billed per GB (extendable to 2 years); Microsoft Sentinel data lake total retention up to 12 years is billed per compressed GB/month (6:1 compression assumption) plus per-GB query/processing meters when archived data is scanned. These are the meters behind 12-month (PCI DSS) and multi-year (CMMC/800-53) retention mandates.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Sentinel in the Defender portal: all generally-available unified-SecOps features are supported in GCC, GCC High, and DoD (portal URLs security.microsoft.us / security.apps.mil); preview features are commercial-cloud only; in GCC, advanced-hunting queries spanning both Sentinel and Defender XDR tables are not supported. Azure Government feature gaps: summary rules, SOC optimization, SIEM migration, repositories, MDTI matching analytics, URL detonation, and TI GeoLocation/WhoIs enrichment are not available; scheduled/NRT/Fusion analytics, automation rules, playbooks, watchlists, and core UEBA are GA (peer/blast-radius enrichments commercial-only). The M365 E5/G5 Sentinel data-grant benefit includes USGOV_GCCHIGH and USGOV_DOD SKUs.

rows.control_intent: Alert in the event of an audit logging process failure.
rows.capability_detail: Logging-failure alerting for the collection pipeline and its sources: SentinelHealth telemetry raising alerts on connector failure, data-source disconnection, and ingestion errors; scheduled analytics detecting ingestion flat-lines per table (a source whose events stop arriving) so a failed logging process on an enclave system surfaces as an alert
rows.how_it_supports: Implements alert-on-logging-failure for the centralized pipeline and, via flat-line detection, for connected sources whose audit stream stops. A source-local failure that keeps emitting some events (partial audit failure on the endpoint itself) is visible only to that system's own instrumentation; hence Partial, not Direct.
rows.config_evidence_example: Health monitoring enabled with alert rules on SentinelHealth; per-source flat-line analytics with expected-cadence thresholds
rows.operational_evidence_example: SentinelHealth alert history; flat-line detection alerts with time-to-restore records

related_microsoft.note: Purview Audit's own service health governs the M365 record upstream of the connector

rows.external_dependencies: Source-local audit-failure detection on enclave systems; response procedure for logging failures

rows.control_intent: Correlate audit record review, analysis, and reporting processes for investigation of and response to indications of unlawful, unauthorized, suspicious, or unusual activity.
rows.capability_detail: Audit-record correlation as the resident engine: KQL analytics joining records across sources (endpoint + identity + network + M365) into single detections, Fusion multistage correlation, alert grouping merging related indications into one incident, workbooks unifying the review-and-reporting surface over the whole enclave record
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements the correlation this control names: the requirement is precisely that audit review, analysis, and reporting operate across records as one process, which is the definition of SIEM correlation. Investigation and response procedures consuming the correlated output are organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Cross-source analytics rules with entity mapping; Fusion enabled; workbooks consolidating review/reporting per the audit procedure
rows.operational_evidence_example: Correlated incident history showing multi-source joins; workbook review records

related_microsoft.note: Unified audit log records are a correlated source via the Office 365 connector (the stacked 3.3.1/3.3.2 Purview rows' output)
Defender XDR alerts join the correlation as first-class signals (free ingestion)

rows.external_dependencies: Audit review/analysis/reporting procedures; investigation and response process
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. rows.control_intent: Operational incident-handling capability (preparation, detection, analysis, containment, recovery); track, document, and report incidents to designated officials/authorities.
rows.capability_detail: The track-document-report backbone of the enclave's incident-handling capability: unified incident queue correlating detections from every connected source into tracked, owned, documented cases; incident tasks encoding the handling procedure; SOAR automation driving reporting steps (notification playbooks to designated officials, ticketing) with run history; SOC incident metrics evidencing the capability operates

rows.control_intent: Monitor organizational systems, including inbound and outbound communications traffic, to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.
rows.capability_detail: The communications-traffic slice both stacked rows defer: firewall, proxy, DNS, and netflow telemetry from enclave boundary devices ingested via CEF/Syslog-AMA and analyzed by scheduled/NRT analytics for attack indicators (beaconing, exfiltration patterns, anomalous destinations), correlated with endpoint and identity signal into unified incidents
rows.how_it_supports: Implements monitoring of inbound/outbound communications by analyzing the traffic records the enclave's network devices emit, which closes the boundary-monitoring gap the Defender row names as external. The devices themselves perform inspection/blocking; Sentinel is the monitoring and detection layer over their telemetry, and only for sources routed in.

rows.operational_evidence_example: Network detection and incident history; boundary-source connector health; traffic-anomaly workbook reviews

Insider Risk Management watches data-exfiltration behavior at the user layer

A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Sentinel in the Defender portal: all generally-available unified-SecOps features are supported in GCC, GCC High, and DoD (portal URLs security.microsoft.us / security.apps.mil); preview features are commercial-cloud only; in GCC, advanced-hunting queries spanning both Sentinel and Defender XDR tables are not supported. Azure Government feature gaps: summary rules, SOC optimization, SIEM migration, repositories, MDTI matching analytics, URL detonation, and TI GeoLocation/WhoIs enrichment are not available; scheduled/NRT/Fusion analytics, automation rules, playbooks, watchlists, and core UEBA are GA (peer/blast-radius enrichments commercial-only). The M365 E5/G5 Sentinel data-grant benefit includes USGOV_GCCHIGH and USGOV_DOD SKUs.

rows.control_intent: Identify unauthorized use of organizational systems.
rows.capability_detail: Behavioral identification of unauthorized use: UEBA baselining normal per-user/per-entity behavior across Entra ID, on-prem AD (Active Directory sync via Defender for Identity, public preview), and connected activity, scoring deviations (unusual access times, resource access, geographic anomalies); anomaly detections and hunting queries surfacing misuse patterns that signature detections miss; cross-source correlation distinguishing account compromise from insider misuse
rows.how_it_supports: Implements unauthorized-use identification at the behavioral layer across the enclave: the estate-wide complement to the stacked Purview row (data-activity misuse in M365) and Defender row (workload detections). Baselines need weeks of data to mature, and definitions of authorized use remain policy.
rows.config_evidence_example: UEBA enabled on Entra ID + AD sources; anomaly rules active; hunting library covering misuse scenarios
rows.operational_evidence_example: UEBA anomaly and insight history; hunting session records with escalation outcomes

related_microsoft.note: Insider Risk Management identifies data-activity misuse with user context (the stacked Purview row)
Defender XDR detects unauthorized-use techniques on workloads (the stacked Defender row)
Entra ID Protection scores sign-in risk, the authentication edge of unauthorized use

rows.external_dependencies: Authorized-use policy definitions; investigation procedure for behavioral alerts
rows.license_requirement: Included with Microsoft Sentinel on the workspace (no separate license); cost follows the consumption-priced data volume the capability operates over. Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Azure Government feature gaps: summary rules, SOC optimization, SIEM migration, repositories, MDTI matching analytics, URL detonation, and TI GeoLocation/WhoIs enrichment are not available; scheduled/NRT/Fusion analytics, automation rules, playbooks, watchlists, and core UEBA are GA (peer/blast-radius enrichments commercial-only). The M365 E5/G5 Sentinel data-grant benefit includes USGOV_GCCHIGH and USGOV_DOD SKUs.

rows.control_intent: Scan for vulnerabilities in organizational systems and applications periodically and when new vulnerabilities are identified; remediate vulnerabilities in accordance with risk assessments.
rows.capability_detail: Continuous, agentless vulnerability scanning for CUI-processing cloud infrastructure: machine scanning across Azure/AWS/GCP VMs (OS and software CVEs, exposed secrets), container image and runtime assessment, SQL vulnerability assessment, all refreshed continuously rather than on scan windows, with risk prioritization and attack path analysis ordering remediation by exploitability and governance rules tracking it to closure
rows.how_it_supports: Directly performs the scan-for-vulnerabilities activity for cloud systems in the CUI boundary, and implements the in-accordance-with-risk-assessments ordering through exploitability-based prioritization. The stacked Defender XDR row's MDVM covers endpoint devices; seam, not overlap. Remediation execution (patching, redeployment) and non-cloud system scanning remain external.
rows.config_evidence_example: Defender CSPM with agentless scanning on CUI-scope subscriptions; container registry assessment active; governance rules with remediation SLAs aligned to the risk assessment
rows.operational_evidence_example: Vulnerability findings history with risk ranking; remediation closure metrics from governance rules; attack-path reports for the CUI enclave

related_microsoft.note: MDVM scans managed endpoint devices (the stacked Defender XDR row); seam: endpoints vs cloud workloads

rows.external_dependencies: Patch/remediation execution (Azure Update Manager, IaC, all outside this product); scanning for on-premises and non-cloud systems; documented remediation SLAs
rows.license_requirement: Defender CSPM: consumption-priced per billable resource per month. It protects all multicloud workloads but bills only on servers, storage accounts, databases (OSS/SQL incl. servers-on-machines), and serverless resources (conversion ratios apply). 30-day free trial on first enablement; a 1-year pre-purchase plan (Commit Units) discounts up to 22%.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Available in Azure Government: Foundational CSPM (asset inventory, MCSB recommendations, secure score), Defender CSPM, Defender for Servers (P1/P2), Defender for Storage, Containers, Key Vault, Resource Manager, and the SQL/open-source database plans are GA. Azure Government gaps: Defender for APIs, Defender for App Service, AI Services threat protection, Defender for Cosmos DB, and DevOps security are not available; within Defender CSPM, CIEM, the Data & AI security dashboard, EASM integration, ServiceNow integration, and code-to-runtime mapping are not available; Defender for Storage sensitive data threat detection is not available (DSPM sensitive data scanning is GA).

rows.control_intent: Periodically assess security controls for effectiveness; monitor controls on an ongoing basis.
rows.capability_detail: Ongoing technical control monitoring for the cloud slice of the CUI environment: the regulatory compliance dashboard continuously assesses onboarded cloud resources against assignable standards (NIST 800-171 and CMMC Level 2 are built-in standards; verify the offered revision against the assessed baseline, as the catalog tracks current NIST publications), with per-control pass/fail, and secure score provides the ongoing effectiveness trend
rows.how_it_supports: Contributes continuous technical assessment for cloud resources in the CUI boundary: the 3.12.3 ongoing-monitoring activity is genuinely performed for that scope. Assessment-scope boundary: it assesses onboarded cloud resources against the standard's technically-assessable subset, not the GCC High/M365 environment where CUI content controls live (the stacked Purview row's Compliance Manager territory), not procedural/administrative controls, and it is neither a self-assessment submission (SPRS) nor a C3PAO certification artifact.
rows.config_evidence_example: NIST 800-171/CMMC standard assigned to CUI-scope subscriptions (requires a paid plan); dashboard review integrated into the continuous-monitoring procedure
rows.operational_evidence_example: Per-standard compliance reports retained as assessment records; control pass/fail history; secure score trend for the CUI enclave

related_microsoft.note: Purview Compliance Manager carries the NIST 800-171 assessment for the M365/GCC High estate with improvement actions (the stacked Purview row); complementary assessment scopes, a seam not an overlap

rows.external_dependencies: The security assessment program, system security plan, and POA&M; SPRS self-assessment scoring and C3PAO assessment; assessment of non-cloud and procedural controls
rows.license_requirement: Regulatory compliance dashboard: the default Microsoft cloud security benchmark (MCSB) assessment is included free with Defender for Cloud. Assigning additional built-in or custom compliance standards requires at least one paid Defender for Cloud plan on the assessed scope, meaning any plan except Defender for Servers Plan 1 and Defender for APIs Plan 1.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Regulatory compliance dashboard: GA in Azure Government (configuration via the Azure portal experience; available standards vary by cloud, so verify the standard list in the target cloud). Available in Azure Government: Foundational CSPM (asset inventory, MCSB recommendations, secure score), Defender CSPM, Defender for Servers (P1/P2), Defender for Storage, Containers, Key Vault, Resource Manager, and the SQL/open-source database plans are GA.

rows.control_intent: Establish and maintain baseline configurations and inventories of organizational systems (hardware, software, firmware, documentation) through their life cycles.
rows.capability_detail: The cloud-infrastructure inventory and baseline-conformance layer: asset inventory maintains a continuously refreshed register of every onboarded Azure/AWS/GCP resource with security state; agentless scanning adds installed-software inventory for machines; MCSB recommendations and Defender for Servers Plan 2 OS security-baseline assessment measure drift from secure-configuration baselines
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the maintain-inventories half for cloud systems in the CUI boundary (the resource register is automatic and current) and monitors conformance to configuration baselines. Establishing the baselines and enforcing them is performed by Azure Policy/IaC (and Intune for endpoints, the stacked Intune row); Defender for Cloud reports the drift, it does not apply the baseline.
rows.config_evidence_example: All CUI-scope cloud estates onboarded (Azure auto, AWS/GCP connectors); Defender for Servers P2 for OS baseline assessment; inventory export cadence for the system inventory record
rows.operational_evidence_example: Asset inventory exports as the system-inventory artifact; software inventory from agentless scanning; baseline-conformance recommendation history

related_microsoft.note: Intune establishes/enforces endpoint baselines and inventories managed devices (the stacked Intune row); seam: devices vs cloud resources

rows.external_dependencies: Baseline configuration documentation; Azure Policy/IaC enforcement (Azure platform, outside this product); firmware/documentation inventory; non-cloud system inventory
rows.license_requirement: Foundational CSPM is free on every subscription/account onboarded to Defender for Cloud (secure score, MCSB security recommendations, asset inventory, multicloud connectors): no per-user license and no charge. The paid plans below are consumption-priced per protected resource. Defender for Servers Plan 2: consumption-priced per protected server per hour, billed monthly. Includes everything in Plan 1 (incl. the integrated MDE for Servers license) plus agentless machine scanning, premium Defender Vulnerability Management capabilities, file integrity monitoring, just-in-time VM access, OS baseline/system-updates assessment, DNS-layer alerts, and a 500 MB/day free security-data ingestion allowance on the associated Log Analytics/Microsoft Sentinel workspace (applies to designated security tables).
rows.cloud_availability_note: Available in Azure Government: Foundational CSPM (asset inventory, MCSB recommendations, secure score), Defender CSPM, Defender for Servers (P1/P2), Defender for Storage, Containers, Key Vault, Resource Manager, and the SQL/open-source database plans are GA.

rows.control_intent: Monitor organizational systems, including inbound and outbound communications traffic, to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.
rows.capability_detail: Attack detection for the cloud-workload plane of the CUI environment: threat detection over storage access, database queries, Kubernetes runtime and control plane, Key Vault and Resource Manager operations, and server behavior (network-layer and DNS-layer alerts with Servers Plan 2); Microsoft Threat Intelligence-backed analytics correlate into Defender portal incidents
rows.how_it_supports: Adds cloud resources and the cloud control plane to this control's monitoring stack: the plane the stacked Defender XDR (endpoints) and Sentinel (cross-source correlation) rows do not natively sense. Network-appliance traffic inspection and the SOC process remain external; coverage follows enabled plans.
rows.config_evidence_example: Workload protection plans on CUI-scope subscriptions; Servers P2 for network/DNS-layer alerts; alert routing to the SOC and Sentinel
rows.operational_evidence_example: Cloud alert history by plan; control-plane detection records; incident correlation reports

related_microsoft.note: Defender XDR monitors endpoints and correlates cloud alerts into shared incidents (the stacked Defender row); seam, not overlap
Sentinel ingests Defender for Cloud alerts free and correlates estate-wide (the stacked Sentinel row)

rows.external_dependencies: Network-appliance traffic monitoring; SOC analysis and response process; systems outside plan coverage
rows.license_requirement: Consumption-priced per protected resource, metered by plan: Defender for Storage per storage account/month plus per-transaction overage above 73M/month and per-GB malware scanning; Defender for Databases per database server (Cosmos DB per provisioned RU); Defender for Containers per vCore of Kubernetes worker nodes (includes a monthly image-scan allowance); Defender for App Service per app instance; Defender for Key Vault per vault/month; Defender for Resource Manager per subscription/month; Defender for APIs by call-volume plan tier (Plans 1–5); AI Services per 1K tokens. 30-day free trial on first enablement (malware scanning excluded). Defender for Servers Plan 2: consumption-priced per protected server per hour, billed monthly. Includes everything in Plan 1 (incl. the integrated MDE for Servers license) plus agentless machine scanning, premium Defender Vulnerability Management capabilities, file integrity monitoring, just-in-time VM access, OS baseline/system-updates assessment, DNS-layer alerts, and a 500 MB/day free security-data ingestion allowance on the associated Log Analytics/Microsoft Sentinel workspace (applies to designated security tables).
rows.cloud_availability_note: Available in Azure Government: Foundational CSPM (asset inventory, MCSB recommendations, secure score), Defender CSPM, Defender for Servers (P1/P2), Defender for Storage, Containers, Key Vault, Resource Manager, and the SQL/open-source database plans are GA. Azure Government gaps: Defender for APIs, Defender for App Service, AI Services threat protection, Defender for Cosmos DB, and DevOps security are not available; within Defender CSPM, CIEM, the Data & AI security dashboard, EASM integration, ServiceNow integration, and code-to-runtime mapping are not available; Defender for Storage sensitive data threat detection is not available (DSPM sensitive data scanning is GA).

rows.control_intent: Periodically assess the risk to organizational operations, assets, and individuals resulting from the operation of organizational systems that process, store, or transmit CUI.
rows.capability_detail: Contextual cloud risk assessment feeding the organizational risk process: attack path analysis traces exploitable chains from internet exposure through vulnerabilities and permissions toward CUI-bearing resources; risk prioritization ranks findings by exploitability and blast radius; cloud security explorer answers ad-hoc risk questions over the cloud security graph; sensitive data discovery identifies which cloud stores actually hold CUI-pattern data
rows.how_it_supports: Supplies a continuously refreshed, exploitability-ranked technical risk picture for the cloud slice of the CUI environment. The risk assessment this control requires (organizational scope, likelihood/impact, documentation, acceptance decisions) is a program activity that consumes this input; it is not performed by the product.
rows.config_evidence_example: Defender CSPM on CUI-scope subscriptions; critical CUI assets tagged so the security graph weights them; attack-path review integrated into the risk-assessment cadence
rows.operational_evidence_example: Attack-path reports over time; risk-prioritized findings exports for the risk-assessment file; security-graph query results for identified concerns

related_microsoft.note: MDVM exposure scores contribute the endpoint-device slice of the same risk picture

rows.external_dependencies: The documented risk assessment program and methodology; risk acceptance decisions; assessment of non-cloud systems and non-technical risk
rows.license_requirement: Defender CSPM: consumption-priced per billable resource per month. rows.cloud_availability_note: Available in Azure Government: Foundational CSPM (asset inventory, MCSB recommendations, secure score), Defender CSPM, Defender for Servers (P1/P2), Defender for Storage, Containers, Key Vault, Resource Manager, and the SQL/open-source database plans are GA. Azure Government gaps: Defender for APIs, Defender for App Service, AI Services threat protection, Defender for Cosmos DB, and DevOps security are not available; within Defender CSPM, CIEM, the Data & AI security dashboard, EASM integration, ServiceNow integration, and code-to-runtime mapping are not available; Defender for Storage sensitive data threat detection is not available (DSPM sensitive data scanning is GA).

rows.control_intent: Respond to information spills: identify the spilled information and where it went, alert responders, isolate and eradicate it, and assess exposure.
rows.capability_detail: DLP/SIT detection identifying spilled sensitive information in unauthorized locations; eDiscovery search establishing spread and executing removal; Audit tracing who accessed the spill
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the identify/locate/trace core of spillage response for M365: detection raises the alert, search maps the spread, audit shows exposure, and targeted deletion supports eradication: the classic CUI-spillage-in-collaboration workflow. Sanitization decisions and system-level remediation are organizational.

rows.control_intent: Operate incident-handling capability covering preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery.

rows.how_it_supports: Implements detection-and-analysis through eradication for incidents touching endpoints, email, identities, and SaaS, stacking with the Purview row's forensic/preservation role. Preparation, recovery, and agency/US-CERT reporting are organizational.

rows.control_intent: Monitor and scan for system vulnerabilities on a defined frequency and when new vulnerabilities are identified; analyze reports, remediate per risk, and share vulnerability information.
rows.capability_detail: Defender Vulnerability Management: continuous agent-based vulnerability monitoring (no scan windows) with automatic reassessment as new CVEs are disclosed, breadth/depth-of-coverage reporting via device and software inventories, risk-based prioritization with threat context, remediation requests with tracking, and exportable reports for information sharing
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements vulnerability monitoring/scanning, analysis, and risk-prioritized remediation for the onboarded estate: the continuous model satisfies frequency-defined and update-triggered scanning. Credentialed scanning of network devices and non-onboarded infrastructure needs complementary scanners.
rows.config_evidence_example: MDVM assessments active; remediation SLAs per severity; vulnerability-report distribution to system owners
rows.operational_evidence_example: Vulnerability assessment and exposure exports per cadence; remediation tracking against SLAs

rows.control_intent: Identify, report, and correct system flaws; test updates before installation; install security-relevant updates within organization-defined timeframes.
rows.capability_detail: Defender Vulnerability Management identifying missing security updates and exploitable flaws per device with threat-based prioritization, remediation requests driving correction, and time-to-remediate reporting against defined timeframes; software end-of-support identification
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the identify/report dimensions and drives correction for the onboarded estate. The patching mechanism (Intune/WSUS/Autopatch), update testing, and the defined timeframes are external to Defender.
rows.config_evidence_example: MDVM active with remediation workflow; per-severity remediation timeframes tracked in reports
rows.operational_evidence_example: Missing-update and time-to-remediate reports against defined timeframes; remediation-request history

related_microsoft.note: Intune Windows update rings/Autopatch and configuration policies install the updates that correct identified flaws

rows.control_intent: Implement malicious code protection at system entry/exit points and endpoints; update mechanisms automatically; configure periodic and real-time scans with defined response actions.
rows.capability_detail: Microsoft Defender Antivirus next-generation protection (real-time, behavior-based, cloud-delivered, tamper-protected) with automatic security-intelligence updates and scheduled scans across Windows/macOS/Linux; attack surface reduction rules; Safe Attachments/Safe Links covering the email/collaboration entry point; configurable block/quarantine response actions
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements malicious code protection at endpoints and the mail entry/exit point, with the automatic-update and periodic/real-time scan regime the control specifies. Perimeter-appliance inspection for non-M365 routes remains external.
rows.config_evidence_example: AV active mode, cloud protection, tamper protection; scheduled scans; ASR rules in block mode; Safe Attachments/Safe Links on all mailboxes
rows.operational_evidence_example: Malware detection/remediation reports; AV currency reports; blocked-attachment reports
