Microsoft Defender for Office 365 Plan 1: standalone, Microsoft 365 Business Premium, Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Office 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW. Effective July 1, 2026 it is also included in Microsoft 365 E3/G3 and Office 365 E3/G3; that rollout began June 2026 and Microsoft expects it to complete during 2026.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender for Endpoint: available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD (G3 includes Plan 1; G5/G5 Security include Plan 2); some capabilities lag commercial, so verify per feature on the MDE US Government page.

rows.control_intent: Monitor the system to detect attacks, indicators of potential attacks, and unauthorized use.

rows.how_it_supports: Implements attack detection across hosts, identities, email, and SaaS: the layer the stacked Purview row explicitly defers to Defender (its slice is unauthorized data use). Network-boundary monitoring (netflow, NDR, firewall analytics) and non-Microsoft systems require SIEM coverage.
rows.config_evidence_example: EDR onboarding coverage; MDI sensors on all DCs/AD FS/AD CS; MDCA anomaly policies; attack disruption enabled
rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident/detection trend reports across workloads; attack-disruption action history

related_microsoft.note: Insider Risk Management supplies the unauthorized-data-use slice (the stacked Purview row)

rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender XDR (unified portal, incidents, advanced hunting, attack disruption): available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD; Microsoft Threat Experts is not available in gov clouds; preview features are commercial-only.

rows.control_intent: Employ spam protection at system entry/exit points to detect and act on unsolicited messages; update mechanisms automatically.

rows.control_intent: Select the event types the system must log (AU-2) and generate audit records for them across system components (AU-12).
rows.capability_detail: The event-selection and collection pipeline for non-M365 components: data collection rules define which event types AMA-managed sources send (Windows Security Events all/common/minimal or custom XPath, the operational form of the AU-2 selection decision), CEF/Syslog streams carry network and appliance events, and the consolidated workspace becomes the system-wide audit trail assessors sample
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the selection mechanics and centralized collection for the components the stacked Purview row leaves outside M365. Record generation itself remains a per-component capability: Sentinel collects what components emit and cannot generate records for a source with logging disabled, so the row stays Partial despite owning the selection surface.
rows.config_evidence_example: DCRs encoding the approved AU-2 event-type list per source class; connector inventory mapped to system components
rows.operational_evidence_example: DCR configuration exports as the event-selection record; per-source ingestion evidence for assessor sampling

related_microsoft.note: Purview Audit selects and generates the M365 record slice (the stacked Purview row)

rows.external_dependencies: Event-selection decisions (organization-defined types); native logging capability on each component
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Sentinel in the Defender portal: all generally-available unified-SecOps features are supported in GCC, GCC High, and DoD (portal URLs security.microsoft.us / security.apps.mil); preview features are commercial-cloud only; in GCC, advanced-hunting queries spanning both Sentinel and Defender XDR tables are not supported. Azure Government feature gaps: summary rules, SOC optimization, SIEM migration, repositories, MDTI matching analytics, URL detonation, and TI GeoLocation/WhoIs enrichment are not available; scheduled/NRT/Fusion analytics, automation rules, playbooks, watchlists, and core UEBA are GA (peer/blast-radius enrichments commercial-only). The M365 E5/G5 Sentinel data-grant benefit includes USGOV_GCCHIGH and USGOV_DOD SKUs.

rows.control_intent: Review and analyze audit records for indications of inappropriate activity, and report findings.
rows.capability_detail: Automated review-analysis-reporting at the frequency the control assigns: scheduled analytics rules reviewing audit records continuously across every connected source, cross-source correlation (the AU-6(3) correlate-repositories enhancement is native behavior), findings reported as incidents routed to assigned personnel via automation rules, workbooks as the recurring review-and-report surface
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements the review, analysis, and reporting activity this control names: organization-defined frequency becomes rule scheduling, inappropriate-activity definitions become detection logic, and reporting lines become incident assignment. The stacked Purview row supplies the M365 review surface; the review procedure, tuning decisions, and findings disposition remain organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Analytics rules mapped to the organization-defined inappropriate-activity list; automation rules routing findings to defined roles; review workbooks on the documented cadence
rows.operational_evidence_example: Rule execution history as continuous-review evidence; incident routing records; dated workbook reviews

related_microsoft.note: Audit search and Activity explorer are the M365-native review surfaces (the stacked Purview row)

rows.control_intent: Protect audit information and audit logging tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion.
rows.capability_detail: Protection of the centralized audit store: ingested Log Analytics records are append-only (no user-facing modify operation), access is governed by Azure RBAC with workspace and table-level scoping, query activity against the workspace is itself auditable, and retention settings resist early truncation of the record
rows.how_it_supports: Implements access control and tamper resistance for the aggregated audit copy: the SIEM-side log protection the stacked Purview row names as external. Administrative reach remains real: workspace contributors can shorten retention and the purge API can delete data, so privileged-access governance over the workspace (PIM, change control) completes the control.
rows.config_evidence_example: Workspace RBAC with table-level scoping for audit tables; query auditing enabled; retention changes under change control
rows.operational_evidence_example: RBAC assignment review for the workspace; query-audit extracts; retention-setting change history

related_microsoft.note: The M365-side audit store is service-managed and tamper-resistant (the stacked Purview row)
PIM and access reviews govern who holds workspace-privileged roles

rows.external_dependencies: Change-control procedure for retention/purge operations; protection of exported log copies

rows.control_intent: Retain audit records for an organization-defined period consistent with the records-retention policy, to support after-the-fact investigations.
rows.capability_detail: Policy-driven retention per table across the whole estate record: analytics-tier retention to 2 years for investigation-hot data, data-lake total retention to 12 years for the after-the-fact record, lake-only tier for high-volume sources, and search jobs/KQL jobs retrieving aged records inside the window, covering the multi-year periods federal schedules assign
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements organization-defined audit retention for every source in the workspace: the SIEM/archive retention layer the stacked Purview row defers for non-M365 logs. The retention schedule itself (e.g., NARA GRS alignment) is organizational, and the cost story is consumption: multi-year retention is priced per compressed GB/month in the lake, not by license tier; that is the number to model in any 800-53/CMMC engagement.
rows.config_evidence_example: Per-table retention matching the approved schedule; lake-tier assignments for long-horizon tables; retrieval procedure tested
rows.operational_evidence_example: Retention configuration export per table; sample retrieval of aged records; retention-cost review records

related_microsoft.note: Audit (Premium) 1-year/10-year retention governs the M365-side record (the stacked Purview row); seam: Purview retains the M365 source record, Sentinel retains the estate copy

rows.external_dependencies: Approved records-retention schedule; budget ownership for retention consumption costs
rows.license_requirement: Consumption-priced retention: analytics-tier retention beyond the included 90 days is billed per GB (extendable to 2 years); Microsoft Sentinel data lake total retention up to 12 years is billed per compressed GB/month (6:1 compression assumption) plus per-GB query/processing meters when archived data is scanned. These are the meters behind 12-month (PCI DSS) and multi-year (CMMC/800-53) retention mandates.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Sentinel in the Defender portal: all generally-available unified-SecOps features are supported in GCC, GCC High, and DoD (portal URLs security.microsoft.us / security.apps.mil); preview features are commercial-cloud only; in GCC, advanced-hunting queries spanning both Sentinel and Defender XDR tables are not supported.

rows.control_intent: Operate incident-handling capability covering preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery.
rows.capability_detail: Handling-capability orchestration across the estate: detection-and-analysis from cross-source correlation (joining Defender XDR incidents with network/third-party signal), automation rules executing consistent triage, playbooks coordinating containment across Microsoft and non-Microsoft systems via Logic Apps connectors (firewall block, ITSM, identity actions), and the incident record documenting the handling lifecycle
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the coordination-and-workflow layer of incident handling, the third card in this stack: Purview supplies forensics/preservation, Defender executes Microsoft-estate containment, Sentinel detects across sources, orchestrates the workflow, and reaches the non-Microsoft systems the other two cannot. Sentinel coordinates response; it does not itself eradicate; enforcement products and responders do. Preparation, recovery, and US-CERT/agency reporting remain organizational.

rows.operational_evidence_example: Playbook run history per incident; cross-source incident records with handling timeline; automation execution log

related_microsoft.note: Audit (Premium) forensics and eDiscovery preservation supply the evidence layer (the stacked Purview row)

rows.control_intent: Track and document security incidents, maintaining records of status, forensic details, and handling.
rows.capability_detail: The incident system of record: every incident tracked with status, ownership, severity, timeline, entities, comments, and analyst actions; incident tasks recording handling steps; manual incident creation covering incidents reported outside telemetry; SOC incident metrics (volumes, times-to-respond, trends) supporting the evaluation dimension the control's discussion describes
rows.how_it_supports: Implements tracking and documentation for incidents flowing through the SOC: durable, exportable, auditable records per incident. Held at Partial because completeness depends on organizational process: incidents identified outside connected telemetry reach the record only if the procedure routes them in (manual creation or ITSM integration), and the org's incident-records requirements may span systems beyond the SOC.

rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident record exports with full timeline and disposition; incident metrics trend reports

rows.control_intent: Monitor the system to detect attacks, indicators of potential attacks, and unauthorized use.

rows.control_intent: Establish and maintain a cyber threat hunting capability that searches for indicators of compromise and detects threats that evade existing controls, exercised at a defined frequency.
rows.capability_detail: The hunting toolchain: curated and custom KQL hunting queries (GA) with the hunts experience organizing hypothesis-driven investigations (public preview; not available in Azure Government), bookmarks preserving findings, livestream for real-time hypothesis testing, UEBA context enriching entities under investigation, and search jobs reaching years of data-lake history so hunts cover threats that evaded controls long ago; validated hunts promote to analytics rules

The capability itself is people and cadence: hunters, hypotheses, and the defined frequency are organizational, and hunts see only connected sources.

rows.cloud_availability_note: Azure Government feature gaps: summary rules, SOC optimization, SIEM migration, repositories, MDTI matching analytics, URL detonation, and TI GeoLocation/WhoIs enrichment are not available; scheduled/NRT/Fusion analytics, automation rules, playbooks, watchlists, and core UEBA are GA (peer/blast-radius enrichments commercial-only). The M365 E5/G5 Sentinel data-grant benefit includes USGOV_GCCHIGH and USGOV_DOD SKUs.

rows.control_intent: Monitor and scan for system vulnerabilities on a defined frequency and when new vulnerabilities are identified; analyze reports, remediate per risk, and share vulnerability information.
rows.capability_detail: Continuous agentless vulnerability monitoring for cloud infrastructure: machine scanning across Azure/AWS/GCP VMs (CVEs, installed software, exposed secrets), container image and runtime assessment, SQL vulnerability assessment, with risk prioritization and attack path analysis performing the analyze-and-rank step and governance rules assigning remediation with owners and SLAs
rows.how_it_supports: Directly performs the monitor-and-scan activity for the cloud resource estate, continuously rather than on a scan schedule, and implements the analyze/prioritize step through the cloud security graph. The stacked Defender XDR row's MDVM covers endpoint devices; seam, not overlap. Remediation itself executes via patching/IaC outside the product; organization-defined frequency and information-sharing are program elements.
rows.config_evidence_example: Defender CSPM with agentless scanning enabled (Subscription Owner); container registry assessment on; governance rules with remediation SLAs
rows.operational_evidence_example: Vulnerability findings with risk ranking over time; governance-rule closure metrics; attack-path analysis reports

related_microsoft.note: MDVM performs vulnerability monitoring for managed endpoints (the stacked Defender XDR row); seam: endpoints vs cloud workloads

rows.external_dependencies: Patch and remediation execution (Azure Update Manager, IaC, all outside this product); scanning of non-cloud systems; vulnerability-information sharing procedures
rows.license_requirement: Defender CSPM: consumption-priced per billable resource per month. It protects all multicloud workloads but bills only on servers, storage accounts, databases (OSS/SQL incl. servers-on-machines), and serverless resources (conversion ratios apply). 30-day free trial on first enablement; a 1-year pre-purchase plan (Commit Units) discounts up to 22%.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Available in Azure Government: Foundational CSPM (asset inventory, MCSB recommendations, secure score), Defender CSPM, Defender for Servers (P1/P2), Defender for Storage, Containers, Key Vault, Resource Manager, and the SQL/open-source database plans are GA. Azure Government gaps: Defender for APIs, Defender for App Service, AI Services threat protection, Defender for Cosmos DB, and DevOps security are not available; within Defender CSPM, CIEM, the Data & AI security dashboard, EASM integration, ServiceNow integration, and code-to-runtime mapping are not available; Defender for Storage sensitive data threat detection is not available (DSPM sensitive data scanning is GA).

rows.control_intent: Develop and implement a continuous monitoring strategy: ongoing assessments of control effectiveness with metrics, status monitoring, analysis, and reporting to support risk-based decisions.
rows.capability_detail: The ongoing-control-assessment engine for cloud infrastructure: the regulatory compliance dashboard continuously re-assesses onboarded Azure/AWS/GCP resources against assigned standards (NIST SP 800-53 R5 is a built-in standard) on an automatic cycle, secure score supplies the effectiveness metric and trend, per-control pass/fail supplies status monitoring, and downloadable reports feed risk-based decision-making
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements the ongoing-assessment, metrics, status-monitoring, and reporting activities this control names, for the cloud resource scope onboarded to Defender for Cloud and the technically-assessable control subset. The continuous monitoring strategy itself, the organization-defined metrics/frequencies, coverage of M365/on-premises tiers (the stacked product rows and Compliance Manager), and assessment of procedural controls remain organizational. Not an authorization decision or assessment attestation.
rows.config_evidence_example: NIST SP 800-53 R5 standard assigned to in-scope subscriptions (requires a paid plan); continuous-monitoring strategy documents the dashboard as the cloud-tier mechanism; secure score thresholds tied to reporting
rows.operational_evidence_example: Per-standard compliance status over time; secure score trend reports; control pass/fail history feeding the continuous-monitoring record

related_microsoft.note: Purview Compliance Manager provides continuous assessment for the M365 tier (its NIST 800-53 template); complementary scopes, a seam not an overlap
Sentinel supplies the event-level security monitoring leg of the continuous-monitoring strategy (the stacked Sentinel rows at SI-4/AU-6)

rows.external_dependencies: The continuous monitoring strategy and organization-defined metrics; authorization decisions; monitoring of procedural and non-cloud controls
rows.license_requirement: Regulatory compliance dashboard: the default Microsoft cloud security benchmark (MCSB) assessment is included free with Defender for Cloud. Assigning additional built-in or custom compliance standards requires at least one paid Defender for Cloud plan on the assessed scope, meaning any plan except Defender for Servers Plan 1 and Defender for APIs Plan 1.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Regulatory compliance dashboard: GA in Azure Government (configuration via the Azure portal experience; available standards vary by cloud, so verify the standard list in the target cloud). Available in Azure Government: Foundational CSPM (asset inventory, MCSB recommendations, secure score), Defender CSPM, Defender for Servers (P1/P2), Defender for Storage, Containers, Key Vault, Resource Manager, and the SQL/open-source database plans are GA.

rows.control_intent: Assess the controls in the system to determine effectiveness, produce assessment reports, and convey results to designated officials; assessments performed per an assessment plan by assessors.
rows.capability_detail: Automated technical-assessment input for control assessments of cloud systems: per-control pass/fail assessment against the NIST SP 800-53 R5 built-in standard with resource-level findings, exportable per-standard summary reports suitable as assessment evidence, and continuous re-assessment keeping results current between formal assessment cycles
rows.how_it_supports: Supplies assessment evidence and automated testing of technically-assessable controls for the cloud scope: it accelerates and evidences the CA-2 assessment, it does not constitute it. The assessment plan, independent assessors, determination of effectiveness across all control types (administrative, physical, procedural), and conveyance to authorizing officials are the assessment program's; the dashboard's output is machine assessment of cloud resource state, not an assessor's judgment.
rows.config_evidence_example: NIST SP 800-53 R5 standard assigned; dashboard reports incorporated into the assessment evidence package; assessed scope documented
rows.operational_evidence_example: Per-standard assessment reports archived per assessment cycle; control findings exports; resource-level evidence for sampled controls

related_microsoft.note: Purview Compliance Manager assessments cover the M365 tier for the same assessment package; complementary scopes

rows.external_dependencies: The assessment plan, assessor independence, and designated officials; assessment of non-cloud and procedural controls; the authorization package
rows.license_requirement: Regulatory compliance dashboard: the default Microsoft cloud security benchmark (MCSB) assessment is included free with Defender for Cloud. Assigning additional built-in or custom compliance standards requires at least one paid Defender for Cloud plan on the assessed scope, meaning any plan except Defender for Servers Plan 1 and Defender for APIs Plan 1.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Regulatory compliance dashboard: GA in Azure Government (configuration via the Azure portal experience; available standards vary by cloud, so verify the standard list in the target cloud). Available in Azure Government: Foundational CSPM (asset inventory, MCSB recommendations, secure score), Defender CSPM, Defender for Servers (P1/P2), Defender for Storage, Containers, Key Vault, Resource Manager, and the SQL/open-source database plans are GA.

rows.control_intent: Develop and maintain an accurate, current inventory of system components, at the level of granularity needed for accountability, reviewed and updated on a defined frequency.
rows.capability_detail: Automatic, continuously current component inventory for cloud systems: asset inventory enumerates every onboarded Azure resource, AWS account resource, and GCP project resource with security state, agent status, and filtering by type/subscription/tag; agentless scanning adds installed-software inventory per machine; inventory is queryable (Azure Resource Graph) and exportable for the system-inventory record
rows.how_it_supports: Directly maintains the accurate-and-current component inventory this control names for the cloud tier: enumeration is automatic on onboarding, not manually curated, so currency and accuracy hold by construction for connected estates. Granularity/accountability fields (owners, criticality) depend on tagging discipline; non-cloud components (endpoints, the stacked Intune device inventory; on-premises hardware) and the defined review cadence remain organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: All in-scope cloud estates onboarded; tagging standard (owner, environment, criticality) applied so inventory granularity supports accountability; export cadence for the inventory record
rows.operational_evidence_example: Asset inventory exports as the CM-8 artifact; software inventory reports; inventory-completeness reconciliation against the CMDB

related_microsoft.note: Intune device inventory covers managed endpoints, the device slice of the component inventory (its CM-family rows)

rows.external_dependencies: CMDB and inventory procedures; on-premises/non-cloud component inventory; resource tagging discipline for accountability fields
rows.license_requirement: Foundational CSPM is free on every subscription/account onboarded to Defender for Cloud (secure score, MCSB security recommendations, asset inventory, multicloud connectors): no per-user license and no charge. The paid plans below are consumption-priced per protected resource.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Available in Azure Government: Foundational CSPM (asset inventory, MCSB recommendations, secure score), Defender CSPM, Defender for Servers (P1/P2), Defender for Storage, Containers, Key Vault, Resource Manager, and the SQL/open-source database plans are GA.

rows.control_intent: Configuration settings: establish, document, implement, and monitor configuration settings reflecting the most restrictive mode consistent with operational requirements.
rows.capability_detail: Configuration-settings monitoring for cloud resources: Microsoft cloud security benchmark recommendations continuously evaluate resource configuration (network exposure, encryption settings, access configuration, logging state) across Azure/AWS/GCP against secure-configuration guidance; Defender for Servers Plan 2 adds OS security-baseline assessment against compute baselines; deviations are flagged, risk-ranked, and tracked via governance rules
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the monitor half of this control for cloud infrastructure: drift from required settings is detected and reported continuously. Establishing, documenting, and implementing the settings is performed by Azure Policy assignments, IaC, and platform operators (and by Intune for endpoints, the stacked Intune row); Defender for Cloud identifies deviations, it does not enforce settings.
rows.config_evidence_example: MCSB active with parameters tuned to the documented settings baseline; Servers P2 OS baseline assessment on; governance rules assigning deviations
rows.operational_evidence_example: Recommendation/deviation history per resource; OS baseline assessment reports; governance closure metrics

related_microsoft.note: Intune enforces configuration settings on managed endpoints (the stacked Intune row); seam: Intune enforces device settings, Defender for Cloud monitors cloud resource settings

rows.external_dependencies: Azure Policy/IaC enforcement of settings (Azure platform, outside this product); documented configuration baselines; settings for non-cloud components
rows.license_requirement: Foundational CSPM is free on every subscription/account onboarded to Defender for Cloud (secure score, MCSB security recommendations, asset inventory, multicloud connectors): no per-user license and no charge. The paid plans below are consumption-priced per protected resource. Defender for Servers Plan 2: consumption-priced per protected server per hour, billed monthly. Includes everything in Plan 1 (incl. the integrated MDE for Servers license) plus agentless machine scanning, premium Defender Vulnerability Management capabilities, file integrity monitoring, just-in-time VM access, OS baseline/system-updates assessment, DNS-layer alerts, and a 500 MB/day free security-data ingestion allowance on the associated Log Analytics/Microsoft Sentinel workspace (applies to designated security tables).
rows.cloud_availability_note: Available in Azure Government: Foundational CSPM (asset inventory, MCSB recommendations, secure score), Defender CSPM, Defender for Servers (P1/P2), Defender for Storage, Containers, Key Vault, Resource Manager, and the SQL/open-source database plans are GA.

rows.control_intent: Employ integrity verification tools to detect unauthorized changes to software, firmware, and information, and respond to detected violations.
rows.capability_detail: The integrity-verification tool for server estates: file integrity monitoring (Plan 2, delivered through the MDE agent) examines operating system files, registries, application software, and Linux system files for changes indicating an attack, compares against known-good baselines, and retains the change record; tamper-indicating behaviors (ransomware, fileless attacks) alert through the same plan
rows.how_it_supports: Directly employs the integrity-verification-tool activity this control names for machines under Plan 2 with FIM enabled: unauthorized change to software and configuration files is detected and evidenced. Respond-to-violations executes through SOC workflow and the enforcement products; firmware verification and information-integrity beyond monitored paths (databases, documents) are out of FIM's scope.
rows.config_evidence_example: Defender for Servers Plan 2 with FIM enabled; monitored-entity rules covering the software/configuration paths the integrity policy names; change-alert routing to the SOC
rows.operational_evidence_example: FIM change reports per monitored entity; integrity-violation alert history; baseline-comparison records

related_microsoft.note: FIM is delivered through the MDE agent, and MDE EDR carries the respond-to-violation actions (the stacked Defender XDR rows); seam: Defender for Servers licenses/configures, MDE executes

rows.external_dependencies: Response procedures for integrity violations; firmware verification tooling; integrity controls for information stores outside monitored file paths
rows.license_requirement: Defender for Servers Plan 2: consumption-priced per protected server per hour, billed monthly. Includes everything in Plan 1 (incl. the integrated MDE for Servers license) plus agentless machine scanning, premium Defender Vulnerability Management capabilities, file integrity monitoring, just-in-time VM access, OS baseline/system-updates assessment, DNS-layer alerts, and a 500 MB/day free security-data ingestion allowance on the associated Log Analytics/Microsoft Sentinel workspace (applies to designated security tables).
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender for Servers P1/P2: GA in Azure Government; file integrity monitoring GA (not supported in GovCon Cloud Moderate). Available in Azure Government: Foundational CSPM (asset inventory, MCSB recommendations, secure score), Defender CSPM, Defender for Servers (P1/P2), Defender for Storage, Containers, Key Vault, Resource Manager, and the SQL/open-source database plans are GA.

rows.control_intent: Monitor the system to detect attacks, indicators of potential attacks, and unauthorized use.
rows.capability_detail: Attack detection for the cloud-infrastructure plane: workload threat detection across storage, databases, containers/Kubernetes, Key Vault, Resource Manager control-plane operations, and servers (behavioral, fileless, network-layer, DNS-layer analytics), backed by Microsoft Threat Intelligence, correlating into Defender portal incidents and streaming to Sentinel
rows.how_it_supports: Adds the cloud-workload and control-plane sensor to this control's existing three-product stack (Purview: data-exfiltration plane; Defender XDR: endpoint/email/identity workloads; Sentinel: cross-source monitor). Each plane is a seam, not an overlap. Detection coverage follows enabled plans; network-boundary sensing and SOC response remain external.
rows.config_evidence_example: Workload protection plans enabled per the monitoring strategy; alert routing to Defender portal/Sentinel; suppression tuned
rows.operational_evidence_example: Cloud detection and incident trends by plan; control-plane alert history; coverage reports against the cloud estate

related_microsoft.note: Defender XDR is the endpoint/email/identity workload sensor (the stacked Defender row); seam: workloads vs cloud infrastructure
Sentinel correlates Defender for Cloud alerts estate-wide with free alert ingestion (the stacked Sentinel row)

rows.external_dependencies: Network-boundary monitoring appliances; SOC procedures; monitoring strategy and objectives
rows.license_requirement: Consumption-priced per protected resource, metered by plan: Defender for Storage per storage account/month plus per-transaction overage above 73M/month and per-GB malware scanning; Defender for Databases per database server (Cosmos DB per provisioned RU); Defender for Containers per vCore of Kubernetes worker nodes (includes a monthly image-scan allowance); Defender for App Service per app instance; Defender for Key Vault per vault/month; Defender for Resource Manager per subscription/month; Defender for APIs by call-volume plan tier (Plans 1–5); AI Services per 1K tokens. 30-day free trial on first enablement (malware scanning excluded). Defender for Servers Plan 2: consumption-priced per protected server per hour, billed monthly. Includes everything in Plan 1 (incl. the integrated MDE for Servers license) plus agentless machine scanning, premium Defender Vulnerability Management capabilities, file integrity monitoring, just-in-time VM access, OS baseline/system-updates assessment, DNS-layer alerts, and a 500 MB/day free security-data ingestion allowance on the associated Log Analytics/Microsoft Sentinel workspace (applies to designated security tables).
rows.cloud_availability_note: Available in Azure Government: Foundational CSPM (asset inventory, MCSB recommendations, secure score), Defender CSPM, Defender for Servers (P1/P2), Defender for Storage, Containers, Key Vault, Resource Manager, and the SQL/open-source database plans are GA. Azure Government gaps: Defender for APIs, Defender for App Service, AI Services threat protection, Defender for Cosmos DB, and DevOps security are not available; within Defender CSPM, CIEM, the Data & AI security dashboard, EASM integration, ServiceNow integration, and code-to-runtime mapping are not available; Defender for Storage sensitive data threat detection is not available (DSPM sensitive data scanning is GA).

rows.capability_detail: Regulation templates as the managed register of applicable requirements, mapped to improvement actions with ongoing tracking
rows.how_it_supports: Evidences that requirements are cataloged, mapped to controls, and tracked over time for the M365 estate. Determining applicability is a legal/GRC function.
rows.config_evidence_example: Active assessments matching the org's regulatory profile
rows.operational_evidence_example: Assessment inventory and update-acceptance history
rows.license_requirement: Compliance Manager: baseline included with Microsoft 365/Office 365 plans; premium regulation templates licensed separately (3 premium templates included at E5/A5/G5)

rows.legacy_dependencies: Inventory for non-M365 data platforms; data owners assigning designations

rows.operational_evidence_example: Retention event logs; disposition records

rows.cloud_availability_note: eDiscovery standard and premium features: Available in GCC High (a few premium items, e.g., Teams transcript collection, in development).

rows.legacy_dependencies: Forensic imaging tooling; chain-of-custody procedure

rows.control_intent: Manage identities and credentials for authorized users, services, and hardware.
rows.capability_detail: Identity lifecycle (provisioning/deprovisioning, lifecycle workflows), unique identities, and credential management (authentication methods, SSPR) across the workforce
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements identity and credential management for Entra identities: the primary PR.AA-01 outcome. Identities in non-Entra systems are a dependency.
rows.config_evidence_example: Lifecycle workflows; provisioning connectors; authentication-methods policy
rows.operational_evidence_example: Provisioning/deprovisioning logs; credential-registration reports
rows.external_dependencies: Identity/credential management in non-Entra systems
rows.license_requirement: Lifecycle Workflows: Microsoft Entra ID Governance SKU or Microsoft Entra Suite (not included in standalone Entra ID P2). Credentials: Multifactor authentication & authentication methods: Microsoft Entra ID Free (basic) and P1; authentication strengths and full policy control require Entra ID P1.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Access reviews, entitlement management, PIM, and Lifecycle Workflows (Microsoft Entra ID Governance): available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD. MFA and authentication methods (incl. passwordless/FIDO2, certificate-based auth): available in Azure Government (GCC/GCC High/DoD).

rows.control_intent: Proof and bind identities to credentials based on the context of interactions.
rows.capability_detail: Credential binding via strong authentication methods (FIDO2/Windows Hello, certificate-based) and onboarding verification; Verified ID for higher-assurance proofing
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the credential-binding dimension for Entra identities. Formal identity proofing at onboarding is largely an organizational/Verified-ID process.
rows.config_evidence_example: Authentication-methods policy; certificate-based auth; onboarding verification
rows.operational_evidence_example: Authentication-method registration; credential-binding records
rows.external_dependencies: Identity proofing process at onboarding; HR verification
rows.license_requirement: Multifactor authentication & authentication methods: Microsoft Entra ID Free (basic) and P1; authentication strengths and full policy control require Entra ID P1.
rows.cloud_availability_note: MFA and authentication methods (incl. passwordless/FIDO2, certificate-based auth): available in Azure Government (GCC/GCC High/DoD).

rows.control_intent: Authenticate users, services, and hardware.
rows.capability_detail: Entra ID authentication with MFA and phishing-resistant methods; Conditional Access enforcing authentication for users, workload identities, and device-bound access
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements authentication for Entra-integrated users/services/devices: the primary PR.AA-03 outcome.
rows.config_evidence_example: Authentication-strengths policy; Conditional Access requiring MFA; workload-identity authentication
rows.operational_evidence_example: Sign-in logs by method; MFA-enforcement evidence
rows.external_dependencies: Authentication in non-Entra systems
rows.license_requirement: Multifactor authentication & authentication methods: Microsoft Entra ID Free (basic) and P1; authentication strengths and full policy control require Entra ID P1. Enforcement: Microsoft Entra Conditional Access: Microsoft Entra ID P1 (included in Microsoft 365 E3/E5/E7, Microsoft 365 F1/F3, Enterprise Mobility + Security E3, and Business Premium). Risk-based conditions additionally require Entra ID P2.
rows.cloud_availability_note: MFA and authentication methods (incl. passwordless/FIDO2, certificate-based auth): available in Azure Government (GCC/GCC High/DoD). Conditional Access: available in Azure Government (GCC, GCC High, DoD).

rows.control_intent: Define, manage, enforce, and review access permissions, entitlements, and authorizations, incorporating least privilege and separation of duties.
rows.capability_detail: Entitlement management (access packages), access reviews/recertification, PIM for least privilege, and incompatible access packages for separation of duties
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements the define-manage-enforce-review cycle for access permissions with least privilege and SoD in Entra-governed resources, a strong PR.AA-05 fit.
rows.config_evidence_example: Access packages with approval; incompatible-package SoD; PIM; recurring reviews
rows.operational_evidence_example: Entitlement/approval records; SoD-denial events; PIM activation; review decisions
rows.external_dependencies: Access permissions in non-Entra systems
rows.license_requirement: Access reviews & entitlement management (baseline capabilities previously GA in Entra ID P2): Microsoft Entra ID P2; advanced governance requires the Microsoft Entra ID Governance SKU or Microsoft Entra Suite. Privileged Identity Management: Microsoft Entra ID P2 or the Microsoft Entra ID Governance SKU.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Access reviews, entitlement management, PIM, and Lifecycle Workflows (Microsoft Entra ID Governance): available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD.

rows.control_intent: Monitor personnel activity and technology usage to find potentially adverse events.
rows.capability_detail: Sign-in and user risk detection (impossible travel, leaked credentials, anomalous token) and risky-user/sign-in reporting for identity-based adverse events
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the identity-activity slice of personnel monitoring: Entra ID Protection detects risky identity behavior. Data-handling and endpoint activity monitoring are other products' domains.
rows.config_evidence_example: ID Protection risk policies; risk-detection SIEM export
rows.operational_evidence_example: Risky-users/risky-sign-ins reports; risk-remediation history

related_microsoft.note: Insider Risk Management monitors data-handling behavior for adverse events
Defender XDR correlates identity risk with endpoint/email signals

rows.external_dependencies: Endpoint and physical-activity monitoring; SOC process
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Entra ID Protection (risk-based policies, risky users/sign-ins): Microsoft Entra ID P2 (included in Microsoft 365 E5/E7, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security), and Enterprise Mobility + Security E5) or Microsoft Entra Suite.
rows.cloud_availability_note: ID Protection risk detections, risky-account investigation, and SIEM connectivity: available in Azure Government; Microsoft Entra threat-intelligence detection is not available in gov clouds.

rows.control_intent: Maintain inventories of hardware managed by the organization.
rows.capability_detail: Intune device inventory as the living hardware inventory for managed endpoints: ownership type, primary user, hardware/OS attributes, refreshed at check-in; enrollment and retirement keeping the inventory current through the device life cycle
rows.how_it_supports: Implements hardware inventory for the managed endpoint estate. Servers, network equipment, unmanaged and IoT hardware need discovery/CMDB tooling; the authoritative org-wide inventory remains external.
rows.config_evidence_example: Enrollment coverage across the estate; hardware-properties collection enabled
rows.operational_evidence_example: All-devices inventory export reconciled to the asset register; stale-device cleanup records
rows.external_dependencies: CMDB/asset register; discovery for unmanaged, server, network, and IoT hardware
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Intune Plan 1 (the base Intune service; included in Microsoft 365 bundles such as E3/E5/E7 and Enterprise Mobility + Security E3/E5, also available standalone)

rows.control_intent: Establish and apply configuration management practices.
rows.capability_detail: Security baselines and settings-catalog profiles as established, versioned configuration management for managed endpoints, applied automatically with per-setting status and drift reporting; compliance policies validating configuration state
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements configuration management for the endpoint estate: established (baselines), applied (policy deployment), and verified (per-setting reporting). Configuration management for servers, network, and cloud platforms requires other tooling.
rows.config_evidence_example: Deployed baselines with documented deltas; settings-catalog profiles; drift/conflict reporting
rows.operational_evidence_example: Per-setting status reports; baseline version history; compliance drift reports
rows.external_dependencies: Configuration management for non-endpoint platforms; configuration standards documentation
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Intune Plan 1 (the base Intune service; included in Microsoft 365 bundles such as E3/E5/E7 and Enterprise Mobility + Security E3/E5, also available standalone)
rows.cloud_availability_note: Core Intune MDM (compliance policies, configuration profiles, app policies) is available in GCC High/DoD; Intune for GCC runs on the commercial Intune instance.

rows.control_intent: Maintain, replace, and remove software commensurate with risk.
rows.capability_detail: Windows update rings and update policies (feature/quality/expedite/driver) plus Windows Autopatch for OS and app currency; compliance policies enforcing minimum OS versions; app lifecycle (deploy, supersede, uninstall) for managed software
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the maintain/replace/remove cycle for OS and managed applications on endpoints: staged update rollout, version enforcement, and removal of unsanctioned software. Patch management for servers and vulnerability-driven prioritization are other layers.
rows.config_evidence_example: Update rings with ring-staged deferrals; compliance minimum-OS rules; managed app catalog with supersedence
rows.operational_evidence_example: Update ring compliance reports; per-device OS version reports; app install/uninstall status

related_microsoft.note: Defender Vulnerability Management supplies the risk signal that prioritizes what to update or remove

rows.external_dependencies: Server/application patching outside Intune; vulnerability management program
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Intune Plan 1 (the base Intune service; included in Microsoft 365 bundles such as E3/E5/E7 and Enterprise Mobility + Security E3/E5, also available standalone)
rows.cloud_availability_note: Windows Autopatch and Windows feature/quality/expedite/driver update policies are not yet available in GCC High/DoD (planned).

rows.control_intent: Prevent installation and execution of unauthorized software.
rows.capability_detail: App Control for Business policies (application allowlisting) governing what can execute on Windows devices; managed app deployment as the sanctioned installation path; device restrictions limiting user installs and unsanctioned stores
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the policy layer preventing unauthorized software on managed endpoints: allowlisting execution and constraining installation paths. On-device enforcement is performed by the OS/Defender; software control for servers is external.
rows.config_evidence_example: App Control for Business policy in enforce mode; device restrictions on installations; managed app catalog
rows.operational_evidence_example: App Control policy status; blocked-execution events (Defender reporting); discovered-apps review

related_microsoft.note: Defender for Endpoint reports and enforces execution control on the device

rows.external_dependencies: Software allowlist governance process; execution control for servers
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Intune Plan 1 (the base Intune service; included in Microsoft 365 bundles such as E3/E5/E7 and Enterprise Mobility + Security E3/E5, also available standalone)
rows.cloud_availability_note: Endpoint security policies, incl. Defender for Endpoint security settings management, are supported in GCC, GCC High, and DoD.

rows.control_intent: Protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-at-rest.
rows.capability_detail: Disk-encryption policy enforcing BitLocker/FileVault on managed endpoints with recovery-key escrow; compliance policies requiring encryption; app protection policies encrypting org data at rest inside managed mobile apps
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the endpoint slice of data-at-rest protection: enforcing and attesting platform encryption on devices and container encryption on mobile. Content-layer at-rest protection in M365 is the Purview seam; backups and server stores are external.
rows.config_evidence_example: Disk-encryption profiles; compliance encryption requirement; app protection policies with encryption
rows.operational_evidence_example: Encryption status report; compliance encryption state; app-protection delivery status

related_microsoft.note: Label-based encryption with customer key options protects at-rest content in M365

rows.external_dependencies: Backup protection; database/server encryption; platform storage encryption (inherited)
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Intune Plan 1 (the base Intune service; included in Microsoft 365 bundles such as E3/E5/E7 and Enterprise Mobility + Security E3/E5, also available standalone)

rows.control_intent: Identify, validate, and record vulnerabilities in assets.
rows.capability_detail: Defender Vulnerability Management: continuous vulnerability identification across onboarded devices with per-CVE validation context (exploit availability, whether the vulnerable component is actually present and running), recorded in queryable device/software inventories with exposure history
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements identify-validate-record for the onboarded endpoint/server estate: continuous discovery, threat-context validation, and a durable record. Network devices, cloud resources (Defender for Cloud, a separate product), and application-layer testing need complementary sources.
rows.config_evidence_example: MDVM assessments active across the estate; vulnerability records integrated with the risk register
rows.operational_evidence_example: Vulnerability inventory exports with exposure history; new-CVE identification reports

related_microsoft.note: Intune remediates the identified OS/software vulnerabilities via update policies

rows.external_dependencies: Vulnerability sources for network/cloud/application assets; organizational risk register
rows.license_requirement: Defender Vulnerability Management core capabilities: included with Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 2 (Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or standalone P2). Premium capabilities (security baselines assessment, block vulnerable applications, browser-extension/certificate/hardware-firmware assessment) require the Defender Vulnerability Management add-on, available to Defender for Endpoint Plan 2, Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Defender Suite/EDU/GOV/FLW, Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW, and Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5/G5 customers; an MDVM standalone exists for non-P2 customers.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender Vulnerability Management core (with MDE Plan 2): available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD; the MDVM add-on trial is not available in GCC High/DoD, so confirm add-on purchase options for gov tenants.

rows.control_intent: Monitor networks and network services to find potentially adverse events.
rows.capability_detail: Device-level network monitoring: EDR network-connection telemetry per process, network protection evaluating outbound destinations against threat intelligence, device discovery spotting unmanaged endpoints on the network, and Defender for Office 365 monitoring the mail path
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the endpoint-vantage slice of network monitoring: every managed device reports its network behavior. Infrastructure-level network monitoring (netflow, firewall analytics, NDR appliances) is outside Defender XDR's scope.
rows.config_evidence_example: Network protection in block mode; device discovery enabled; EDR onboarding coverage
rows.operational_evidence_example: Network-protection events; device-discovery reports of unmanaged endpoints; network-connection hunting queries

related_microsoft.note: Sentinel ingests firewall/netflow telemetry for infrastructure-level network monitoring

rows.external_dependencies: Network-infrastructure monitoring (netflow, NDR, firewall analytics)
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 2: standalone or included in Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender for Endpoint: available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD (G3 includes Plan 1; G5/G5 Security include Plan 2); some capabilities lag commercial, so verify per feature on the MDE US Government page.

rows.control_intent: Monitor personnel activity and technology usage to find potentially adverse events.
rows.capability_detail: SaaS technology-usage monitoring: activity logs from connected apps, anomaly detection on user behavior (impossible travel, mass download, unusual admin activity), Cloud Discovery of shadow IT including generative-AI apps in use, OAuth app governance watching third-party app grants
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the cloud-app slice of personnel-activity/technology-usage monitoring, stacking with the Purview rows (data-layer and AI-interaction slices) and the Entra row (sign-in risk). Physical access and non-cloud IT usage monitoring remain external.
rows.config_evidence_example: App connectors for sanctioned apps; anomaly policies tuned; Cloud Discovery continuous reports; OAuth app policies
rows.operational_evidence_example: MDCA activity/anomaly alert exports; discovered-app usage trends including AI apps; OAuth grant reviews

related_microsoft.note: Insider Risk Management and DSPM for AI monitor the data-handling and AI-interaction slices (the stacked Purview rows)
Entra ID Protection monitors sign-in/user risk (the stacked Entra row)

rows.external_dependencies: Physical access monitoring; non-cloud usage monitoring; acceptable-use policy
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps: standalone or included in Enterprise Mobility + Security E5, Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, Microsoft Purview Suite/EDU/GOV/FLW, Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW, or Microsoft 365 E5/F5/G5 Information Protection and Governance. Conditional Access App Control additionally requires Microsoft Entra ID P1 licensing.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender for Cloud Apps: available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD as dedicated gov offerings (commercial-tenant MDCA must transition to the GCC version for gov eligibility).

rows.control_intent: Monitor computing hardware and software, runtime environments, and their data to find potentially adverse events.
rows.capability_detail: EDR behavioral sensors monitoring processes, file system, registry, memory, and inter-process activity on endpoints and servers in real time; automated correlation raising incidents from adverse runtime behavior; deep file analysis; device health and configuration monitoring
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements computing-hardware/software/runtime monitoring for the onboarded estate: this subcategory is EDR's namesake outcome. Unmanaged systems and specialized runtimes (OT, mainframe) are outside.
rows.config_evidence_example: EDR in block mode across the estate; behavioral-detection policies current; device health monitoring
rows.operational_evidence_example: EDR detection and incident reports; device timeline evidence; runtime-behavior hunting queries

related_microsoft.note: Intune supplies the managed-device inventory and configuration state the monitoring runs on

rows.external_dependencies: Monitoring for unmanaged systems, OT, and specialized runtimes
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 2: standalone or included in Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender for Endpoint: available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD (G3 includes Plan 1; G5/G5 Security include Plan 2); some capabilities lag commercial, so verify per feature on the MDE US Government page.

rows.control_intent: Analyze potentially adverse events to better understand the associated activities.
rows.capability_detail: Cross-workload event analysis: alerts auto-correlated into incidents with full attack story (entities, timeline, MITRE ATT&CK mapping), automated investigation determining scope and verdict, advanced hunting over 30 days of raw telemetry for analyst-driven analysis
rows.how_it_supports: Implements adverse-event analysis for endpoint/email/identity/SaaS signal: correlation, enrichment, and verdicting stack over the Purview row's data-layer analysis substrate. Non-Microsoft event sources analyze in the SIEM.
rows.config_evidence_example: Incident correlation enabled; AIR per device group; hunting query library for recurring analyses
rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident attack-story documentation; AIR investigation records; hunting analysis exports

related_microsoft.note: Audit and Activity explorer supply the data-layer who/what/where substrate (the stacked Purview row)
Sentinel analyzes events from non-Microsoft sources

rows.external_dependencies: SOC analysis procedures; non-Microsoft event analysis
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Defender XDR has no separate license; the unified portal and correlation come with any qualifying workload license (Microsoft 365 E5/A5; Microsoft 365 E3 with the Microsoft Defender Suite or Enterprise Mobility + Security E5 add-on; Microsoft 365 A3 with the A5 Security add-on; Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5; Enterprise Mobility + Security E5/A5; Office 365 E5/A5; Microsoft 365 Business Premium; or standalone Defender workload licenses). Automatic attack disruption and threat analytics require Defender for Endpoint Plan 2.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender XDR (unified portal, incidents, advanced hunting, attack disruption): available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD; Microsoft Threat Experts is not available in gov clouds; preview features are commercial-only.

rows.control_intent: Perform analysis to establish what took place during an incident and its root cause.
rows.capability_detail: Incident forensics: device timelines reconstructing process/file/network/registry activity around the incident, the incident graph tracing the attack path across devices, identities, and mailboxes to the root cause, live response sessions for direct forensic collection from endpoints, deep file analysis (detonation)
rows.how_it_supports: Implements what-happened and root-cause analysis for incidents in the Microsoft estate, stacking with the Purview row's which-data-was-touched record. Forensics for non-Microsoft systems and formal forensic-evidence handling (chain of custody) remain external.
rows.config_evidence_example: EDR onboarding with full telemetry; live response enabled for responders; investigation SOPs referencing device timeline and incident graph
rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident investigation reports with root-cause narrative from the incident graph; live-response transcripts

related_microsoft.note: Audit (Premium) crucial events reconstruct exactly which data was accessed (the stacked Purview row)
Sentinel correlates non-Microsoft telemetry into the same investigation

rows.external_dependencies: Forensic procedures and chain-of-custody handling; analysis for non-Microsoft systems
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 2: standalone or included in Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender XDR (unified portal, incidents, advanced hunting, attack disruption): available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD; Microsoft Threat Experts is not available in gov clouds; preview features are commercial-only.

rows.control_intent: Contain and eradicate incidents.
rows.capability_detail: Containment and eradication tooling: device isolation (full/selective), process termination and file quarantine, user-account disable and session revocation, mailbox remediation (soft/hard delete, ZAP), OAuth-app disablement; automated investigation & remediation executing these at scale; automatic attack disruption containing high-confidence attacks machine-speed
rows.how_it_supports: Implements containment and eradication for incidents touching endpoints, mailboxes, identities, and SaaS apps: the operational hands of RS.MI for the Microsoft estate. Containment for network segments and non-Microsoft systems, and the decision authority, remain organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: AIR remediation levels; attack disruption enforced after report-only pilot; containment playbooks referencing Defender actions
rows.operational_evidence_example: Action center containment/eradication history per incident; attack-disruption action reviews

related_microsoft.note: Sentinel SOAR playbooks extend containment to firewalls and non-Microsoft systems

rows.external_dependencies: Containment decision authority and playbooks; network-level and non-Microsoft containment
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 2: standalone or included in Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender XDR (unified portal, incidents, advanced hunting, attack disruption): available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD; Microsoft Threat Experts is not available in gov clouds; preview features are commercial-only.

rows.control_intent: Generate log records and make them available for continuous monitoring.
rows.capability_detail: The made-available-for-continuous-monitoring half of this outcome: connectors bring log records from every plane: M365 (free Office 365 connector consuming what the stacked Purview row generates), Entra, Defender, Azure, servers via AMA, network and third-party via CEF/Syslog, into the workspace where continuous monitoring actually runs, with per-table retention keeping records available
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements availability-for-monitoring at estate scope: this subcategory's outcome is only met when generated records reach the monitoring layer, and Sentinel is that layer. Generation remains per-system (the stacked Purview row for M365; native logging elsewhere), and unconnected sources stay dark.
rows.config_evidence_example: Connector estate mapped to the logging standard; DCR event selection; retention settings per monitoring horizon
rows.operational_evidence_example: Connector health/continuity reports; ingestion volume by source; monitoring-coverage review against the asset inventory

related_microsoft.note: Purview Audit generates the M365 log records and exposes them to SIEM ingestion (the stacked Purview row); seam: Purview generates, Sentinel makes available and monitors
Defender XDR alerts and hunting tables are first-party sources (alerts free; raw tables benefit-eligible)

rows.external_dependencies: Log generation on each system; logging standard defining required records
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027. Free data sources: Office 365 audit activity (SharePoint/Exchange/Teams), Azure Activity, and all Microsoft Defender security alerts/incidents ingest at no charge. Microsoft 365 E7/E5/A5/F5/G5 and E5 Security tenants additionally get a data grant of up to 5 MB/user/day covering Entra ID sign-in/audit logs, Defender for Cloud Apps shadow-IT discovery, Purview Information Protection logs, and Defender XDR advanced-hunting tables; raw Microsoft logs beyond the grant are paid ingestion.

rows.control_intent: Analyze potentially adverse events to better understand the associated activities.
rows.capability_detail: Adverse-event analysis across every connected source: scheduled/NRT analytics evaluating events against detection logic, Fusion correlating multistage activity, ML anomalies flagging deviations, UEBA enriching events with behavioral context, and entity mapping assembling the associated-activities picture spanning Microsoft and non-Microsoft telemetry in one incident
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements the analysis activity this outcome names at estate scope: events from any source are analyzed by the same engine, and cross-source context is precisely what understanding associated activities requires. The stacked Defender row analyzes the Microsoft-workload slice natively; SOC analysis procedures and verdict authority remain organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Analytics and anomaly rules across the connector estate; Fusion enabled; entity mapping configured per rule
rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident analysis records showing cross-source event joins; anomaly dispositions; analysis workbook reviews

related_microsoft.note: Audit and Activity explorer supply the data-layer who/what/where substrate (the stacked Purview row)
Defender XDR analyzes Microsoft-workload events natively with AIR verdicts (the stacked Defender row); seam: Defender analyzes its workloads, Sentinel analyzes the estate

rows.external_dependencies: SOC analysis procedures; analysis for sources not connected
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027.

rows.control_intent: Correlate information from multiple sources.
rows.capability_detail: The subcategory's namesake as core product function: 350+ connectors feed one workspace where KQL joins, Fusion multistage correlation, alert grouping, and entity resolution correlate endpoint, identity, email, network, cloud, and application information into single detections and incidents, including joining Defender XDR incidents with non-Microsoft telemetry no workload product can see together
rows.how_it_supports: Directly and fully implements multi-source correlation: this outcome is the definition of a SIEM, and no other product in the atlas performs it estate-wide. Correlation quality follows connector coverage and entity-mapping hygiene; deciding which sources matter is the monitoring strategy's call.
rows.config_evidence_example: Connector estate spanning the planes to correlate; entity mapping per analytics rule; Fusion and alert grouping enabled
rows.operational_evidence_example: Correlated incidents evidencing multi-source joins (e.g., firewall + identity + endpoint in one incident); correlation-rule inventory

related_microsoft.note: Defender XDR correlates within Microsoft workloads; its incidents join Sentinel's estate-wide correlation as one source among many; seam, not overlap
Purview Audit records are a correlated source via the Office 365 connector

rows.external_dependencies: Monitoring strategy selecting sources; entity-mapping standards
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027. Free data sources: Office 365 audit activity (SharePoint/Exchange/Teams), Azure Activity, and all Microsoft Defender security alerts/incidents ingest at no charge. Microsoft 365 E7/E5/A5/F5/G5 and E5 Security tenants additionally get a data grant of up to 5 MB/user/day covering Entra ID sign-in/audit logs, Defender for Cloud Apps shadow-IT discovery, Purview Information Protection logs, and Defender XDR advanced-hunting tables; raw Microsoft logs beyond the grant are paid ingestion.

rows.control_intent: Monitor networks and network services to find potentially adverse events.
rows.capability_detail: Infrastructure-level network monitoring, the slice the stacked Defender row names as external: firewall, netflow, DNS, proxy, and WAF telemetry ingested via CEF/Syslog-AMA and native Azure connectors, analyzed by network-focused detections (beaconing, DNS anomalies, exfiltration patterns, anomalous destinations) and correlated with the endpoint-vantage signal Defender contributes
rows.how_it_supports: Implements network monitoring at the infrastructure vantage by analyzing the telemetry network devices emit, completing the two-vantage picture with Defender's device-level view. The devices perform the packet-level sensing; Sentinel is the monitoring and detection layer over their output, and only for sources routed in.
rows.config_evidence_example: Network-device sources connected (firewall/DNS/proxy/netflow); network detection rules enabled; cross-vantage correlation with endpoint signal
rows.operational_evidence_example: Network detection and incident trends; boundary-source connector health; network-monitoring workbook reviews

related_microsoft.note: Defender for Endpoint monitors network behavior from the device vantage (the stacked Defender row); two vantages, one picture

rows.external_dependencies: Network devices generating telemetry; NDR/packet-capture tooling where deep inspection is required
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027.

rows.control_intent: Perform analysis to establish what took place during an incident and its root cause.
rows.capability_detail: Cross-source incident reconstruction: the incident's entity timeline assembled from every connected plane (network sessions, identity events, endpoint activity, application logs), KQL investigation over the full record, UEBA context distinguishing anomalous from baseline behavior, and search jobs pulling data-lake history when root cause predates hot retention: what-took-place across systems no single workload product can reconstruct alone
rows.how_it_supports: Implements incident analysis for the cross-source and historical dimensions, stacking with the Defender row's deep endpoint forensics and the Purview row's which-data-was-touched record. Sentinel establishes what happened across the estate; artifact-level forensics (disk, memory) and chain-of-custody handling remain external.
rows.config_evidence_example: Investigation procedures referencing entity timelines and KQL; lake retention sized for lookback analysis; bookmark discipline for findings
rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident investigation reports with cross-source timeline; search-job retrievals supporting root-cause work

related_microsoft.note: Audit (Premium) crucial events reconstruct exactly which data was accessed (the stacked Purview row)

Defender XDR device timeline and incident graph supply deep endpoint/workload forensics (the stacked Defender row); seam: Defender reconstructs the device story, Sentinel reconstructs the estate story

rows.external_dependencies: Forensic procedures and chain-of-custody; artifact-level forensics tooling
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027. Consumption-priced retention: analytics-tier retention beyond the included 90 days is billed per GB (extendable to 2 years); Microsoft Sentinel data lake total retention up to 12 years is billed per compressed GB/month (6:1 compression assumption) plus per-GB query/processing meters when archived data is scanned. These are the meters behind 12-month (PCI DSS) and multi-year (CMMC/800-53) retention mandates.

rows.control_intent: Contain and eradicate incidents.
rows.capability_detail: Containment orchestration beyond the Microsoft estate: playbooks invoking firewall block rules, third-party identity suspensions, ticketing, and network isolation through Logic Apps connectors, alongside calls into Defender and Entra actions, with automation rules triggering them at incident creation for machine-speed initiation across every plane the SOC controls
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the orchestration layer of containment/eradication: reaching the network and non-Microsoft systems the stacked Defender row names as external, and sequencing multi-system response as one workflow. Sentinel coordinates; the containment itself executes in the enforcement products and devices, and decision authority stays with the SOC.
rows.config_evidence_example: Containment playbooks per incident class incl. third-party integrations; automation rules binding them; decision-authority gates for destructive actions
rows.operational_evidence_example: Playbook run history per incident; multi-system containment timelines; automation execution log

related_microsoft.note: Defender XDR executes endpoint/mailbox/identity containment natively (the stacked Defender row); seam: Defender contains its workloads, Sentinel orchestrates across everything else

rows.external_dependencies: Containment decision authority; enforcement capability in target systems; playbook connector credentials
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027. Microsoft Sentinel automation rules are included with the workspace (consumption-priced service); Logic Apps playbooks are billed separately under Azure Logic Apps consumption meters.

rows.control_intent: Identify, validate, and record vulnerabilities in assets.
rows.capability_detail: Continuous vulnerability identification for cloud assets: agentless machine scanning (CVEs, installed software, exposed secrets) across Azure/AWS/GCP, container image and runtime assessment, SQL vulnerability assessment, with findings recorded per resource with severity and exploitability context, validated against the cloud security graph (internet exposure, permissions, data sensitivity) via attack path analysis
rows.how_it_supports: Directly performs identify-validate-record for the cloud asset estate: the exploitability validation the subcategory implies is what the security graph adds over raw scanning. The stacked Defender XDR row's MDVM performs the same activity for endpoint devices; seam, not overlap. Remediation and non-cloud asset scanning remain external.
rows.config_evidence_example: Defender CSPM with agentless scanning across cloud estates; container assessment on; attack-path review cadence
rows.operational_evidence_example: Vulnerability findings register per asset; attack-path validation reports; new-finding trend metrics

related_microsoft.note: MDVM identifies vulnerabilities on managed endpoints (the stacked Defender XDR row); seam: endpoints vs cloud assets

rows.external_dependencies: Remediation execution (patching/IaC, outside this product); penetration testing; non-cloud asset scanning
rows.license_requirement: Defender CSPM: consumption-priced per billable resource per month. It protects all multicloud workloads but bills only on servers, storage accounts, databases (OSS/SQL incl. servers-on-machines), and serverless resources (conversion ratios apply). 30-day free trial on first enablement; a 1-year pre-purchase plan (Commit Units) discounts up to 22%.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Azure Government gaps: Defender for APIs, Defender for App Service, AI Services threat protection, Defender for Cosmos DB, and DevOps security are not available; within Defender CSPM, CIEM, the Data & AI security dashboard, EASM integration, ServiceNow integration, and code-to-runtime mapping are not available; Defender for Storage sensitive data threat detection is not available (DSPM sensitive data scanning is GA).

rows.how_it_supports: Directly maintains the services-and-systems inventory for cloud estates: enumeration is automatic on onboarding, so the register stays current with the elastic resource lifecycle no manual inventory can track. Software inventory covers scanned machines; SaaS application inventory is the Defender XDR row's MDCA discovery territory (adjacent subcategory), and on-premises software/systems remain external.

rows.control_intent: Identify improvements from evaluations.

rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements identify-improvements-from-evaluations for the cloud estate: evaluation runs continuously and each finding is itself an identified improvement, ranked and routed. The subcategory's broader sweep (improvements from audits, exercises, and assessments of non-cloud and procedural controls) remains the program's; the stacked-scope caveat applies: evaluations here cover onboarded cloud resources and technically-assessable controls only.

rows.operational_evidence_example: Recommendation-to-remediation closure history; secure score trend; governance-rule SLA compliance metrics

Defender CSPM: consumption-priced per billable resource per month. It protects all multicloud workloads but bills only on servers, storage accounts, databases (OSS/SQL incl. servers-on-machines), and serverless resources (conversion ratios apply). 30-day free trial on first enablement; a 1-year pre-purchase plan (Commit Units) discounts up to 22%.

Establishing and applying the configurations is done by Azure Policy, IaC pipelines, and platform teams (and by Intune for managed endpoints, the stacked Intune row); Defender for Cloud detects nonconformance, it does not apply configuration.

rows.control_intent: Protect audit logs from destruction and unauthorized modification. 10.3.3: promptly back up audit log files, including for external-facing technologies, to a secure, central, internal log server or other media that is difficult to modify.
rows.capability_detail: The central log server the requirement names: CDE component logs (firewalls, servers, network devices, external-facing systems) shipped promptly via CEF/Syslog-AMA and agent-based connectors into the workspace, where ingested records are append-only and access is RBAC-scoped: a difficult-to-modify central copy independent of the originating system
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements the prompt-central-backup mechanism for in-scope logs: near-real-time forwarding into an append-only store is the modern form of this control, and QSAs assess Sentinel workspaces as exactly that. Log generation on each CDE component and forwarding coverage of every in-scope system remain the entity's to establish.

rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements automated daily log review for every in-scope source routed to the workspace; 10.4.1.1 exists precisely because manual daily review does not scale, and a tuned SIEM analytics estate is its intended implementation. Defining which events are security-relevant, the exception-follow-up procedure, and reviews of sources not forwarded remain the entity's.
