rows.control_intent: Retain audit log history for at least 12 months, with at least the most recent 3 months immediately available for analysis.
rows.capability_detail: The retention mechanics for CDE logs: analytics-tier retention covering the immediately-available window (90 days included for Sentinel tables, effectively the 3-month bar, extendable to 2 years), with total retention in the data lake holding the full 12+ months queryable via search jobs and KQL jobs; per-table settings so high-volume sources meet the mandate at lake rates rather than analytics rates
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements 12-month retention with a 3-month hot window for every in-scope log source: the SIEM-retention layer the stacked Purview row names as primary for CDE logs. The consulting insight is cost shape: this is a consumption meter, so the 12-month bill is a function of daily ingest volume and tiering choices, and tiering verbose sources to the lake is what keeps the mandate affordable.

rows.license_requirement: Consumption-priced retention: analytics-tier retention beyond the included 90 days is billed per GB (extendable to 2 years); Microsoft Sentinel data lake total retention up to 12 years is billed per compressed GB/month (6:1 compression assumption) plus per-GB query/processing meters when archived data is scanned. These are the meters behind 12-month (PCI DSS) and multi-year (CMMC/800-53) retention mandates.

rows.control_intent: Detect, alert on, and promptly address failures of critical security control systems (including audit logging, IDS/IPS, anti-malware, and network security controls).
rows.capability_detail: Failure detection for the security control estate: SentinelHealth alerting on connector and ingestion failures (the audit-logging control watching itself), flat-line analytics detecting when a critical control goes silent (firewall logs stop, AV stops reporting, IDS emits nothing), and alert routing ensuring prompt response with an incident trail evidencing the 10.7.3 follow-through
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the detect-and-alert dimensions by treating control telemetry as a heartbeat: a security control that stops logging surfaces as an anomaly. Held at Partial: silence-based detection is indirect (a control can fail while still emitting logs), native health signals from each control system are the entity's to forward, and the prompt-restore response is operational.
rows.config_evidence_example: SentinelHealth alert rules; per-control flat-line detections with expected-cadence thresholds; escalation automation for control-failure incidents
rows.operational_evidence_example: Control-failure alert history with time-to-restore; SentinelHealth records; failure-response incident documentation

The IR plan itself, its 12.10.x companion elements (roles, training, testing), and alert sources not forwarded to the workspace remain the entity's.

rows.control_intent: Perform internal vulnerability scans at least once every three months, resolve high/critical vulnerabilities, and rescan to confirm; authenticated scanning per 11.3.1.2.
rows.capability_detail: Continuous internal vulnerability assessment for cloud-hosted CDE components: agentless machine scanning across Azure/AWS/GCP VMs reads disk snapshots with full visibility into installed software and CVEs (an authenticated-equivalent inspection without credentials on the wire), container and SQL assessment cover those component classes, and findings re-evaluate continuously; every quarterly window is covered by construction, with rescan-confirmation implicit in the refresh cycle
rows.how_it_supports: Exceeds the quarterly internal-scan cadence for cloud CDE components and evidences resolve-and-rescan through finding-closure history. PCI DSS v4.0.1 is a built-in dashboard standard for the compliance view. Assessor acceptance of continuous agentless assessment in lieu of a traditional credentialed scanner is a scoping conversation; external ASV scans (11.3.2) are never covered, and non-cloud CDE segments need their own scanner. The stacked Defender XDR row's MDVM covers endpoint components.
rows.config_evidence_example: Defender CSPM with agentless scanning on CDE subscriptions; PCI DSS v4.0.1 standard assigned to the dashboard; finding-closure SLAs for high/critical per 11.3.1.1
rows.operational_evidence_example: Vulnerability finding and closure history per CDE component; scan-coverage evidence for the quarterly assessment record; dashboard PCI compliance reports

rows.license_requirement: Defender CSPM: consumption-priced per billable resource per month. It protects all multicloud workloads but bills only on servers, storage accounts, databases (OSS/SQL incl. servers-on-machines), and serverless resources (conversion ratios apply). 30-day free trial on first enablement; a 1-year pre-purchase plan (Commit Units) discounts up to 22%.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Azure Government gaps: Defender for APIs, Defender for App Service, AI Services threat protection, Defender for Cosmos DB, and DevOps security are not available; within Defender CSPM, CIEM, the Data & AI security dashboard, EASM integration, ServiceNow integration, and code-to-runtime mapping are not available; Defender for Storage sensitive data threat detection is not available (DSPM sensitive data scanning is GA).

rows.control_intent: Deploy an anti-malware solution on all system components (or document those not at risk), able to detect, remove, and block all known types of malware.
rows.capability_detail: Anti-malware coverage for cloud-hosted CDE system components: Defender for Servers deploys and licenses the integrated MDE/Defender Antivirus engine per server across Azure/AWS/GCP/Arc (the consumption-priced path for server estates the per-user story leaves out), with endpoint-protection assessment recommendations evidencing that every component is protected and healthy; Defender for Storage malware scanning covers files transiting CDE-adjacent cloud storage
rows.how_it_supports: Closes the server-licensing slice this requirement's stack previously carried only as a note: CDE servers get the same detect/remove/block engine as endpoints, priced per server-hour. The engine and its verdicts are MDE's (the stacked Defender XDR row); Intune enforces the policy on managed endpoints (the stacked Intune row); the all-components sweep and not-at-risk documentation remain the entity's.
rows.config_evidence_example: Defender for Servers on every CDE server subscription; endpoint-protection assessment clean; storage malware scanning on in-scope accounts
rows.operational_evidence_example: Per-server protection coverage evidence for the 5.2 assessment; unhealthy-protection remediation history; storage scan verdict logs

related_microsoft.note: Defender Antivirus/MDE is the detect-remove-block engine (the stacked Defender XDR row); Defender for Cloud is the per-server licensing/deployment vehicle for CDE server estates
Intune enforces AV policy on managed endpoint components (the stacked Intune row)

rows.external_dependencies: Not-at-risk component documentation (5.2.3); anti-malware for non-cloud/unmanaged components; periodic evaluation procedures
rows.license_requirement: Defender for Servers Plan 1: consumption-priced per protected server per hour, billed monthly. Plan 1 centers on the integrated Microsoft Defender for Endpoint for Servers license (EDR); customers already licensed for MDE for Servers can request a price adjustment through Azure support (the MDE portion is not double-charged). Consumption-priced per protected resource, metered by plan: Defender for Storage per storage account/month plus per-transaction overage above 73M/month and per-GB malware scanning; Defender for Databases per database server (Cosmos DB per provisioned RU); Defender for Containers per vCore of Kubernetes worker nodes (includes a monthly image-scan allowance); Defender for App Service per app instance; Defender for Key Vault per vault/month; Defender for Resource Manager per subscription/month; Defender for APIs by call-volume plan tier (Plans 1–5); AI Services per 1K tokens. 30-day free trial on first enablement (malware scanning excluded).
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender for Servers P1/P2: GA in Azure Government; file integrity monitoring GA (not supported in GovCon Cloud Moderate).

rows.control_intent: Develop, implement, and maintain configuration standards for system components, consistent with industry-accepted hardening standards.
rows.capability_detail: Hardening-conformance monitoring for cloud CDE components: MCSB recommendations (built on CIS and NIST guidance) continuously evaluate resource configurations across Azure/AWS/GCP; CIS benchmark standards are assignable in the dashboard for explicit industry-standard mapping; Defender for Servers Plan 2 adds OS security-baseline assessment on machines; drift from hardened configuration is flagged and risk-ranked
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the maintain-and-verify dimension of configuration standards for cloud components: continuous evidence that hardening holds, mapped to industry benchmarks. Developing the standards and implementing them on components is performed by the entity via Azure Policy, IaC, and build processes (Intune for endpoints, the stacked Intune row); Defender for Cloud verifies conformance, it does not harden.
rows.config_evidence_example: MCSB active; CIS benchmark standard assigned for CDE subscriptions; Servers P2 OS baseline assessment; drift findings routed via governance rules
rows.operational_evidence_example: Hardening-conformance reports per component class; OS baseline assessment history; drift remediation records for the 2.2 assessment

rows.external_dependencies: The documented configuration standards; hardening implementation (Azure Policy/IaC/build images, outside this product); non-cloud component hardening
rows.license_requirement: Foundational CSPM is free on every subscription/account onboarded to Defender for Cloud (secure score, MCSB security recommendations, asset inventory, multicloud connectors): no per-user license and no charge. The paid plans below are consumption-priced per protected resource. Defender for Servers Plan 2: consumption-priced per protected server per hour, billed monthly. Includes everything in Plan 1 (incl. the integrated MDE for Servers license) plus agentless machine scanning, premium Defender Vulnerability Management capabilities, file integrity monitoring, just-in-time VM access, OS baseline/system-updates assessment, DNS-layer alerts, and a 500 MB/day free security-data ingestion allowance on the associated Log Analytics/Microsoft Sentinel workspace (applies to designated security tables).
rows.cloud_availability_note: Available in Azure Government: Foundational CSPM (asset inventory, MCSB recommendations, secure score), Defender CSPM, Defender for Servers (P1/P2), Defender for Storage, Containers, Key Vault, Resource Manager, and the SQL/open-source database plans are GA.

rows.control_intent: Base the security program on a written risk assessment identifying foreseeable internal/external risks to customer information, with criteria for risk evaluation and safeguard adequacy.
rows.capability_detail: Customer-information discovery (financial SITs, Data explorer) and DSPM risk analytics feeding the written assessment's data-inventory and exposure inputs
rows.how_it_supports: Supplies the where-is-customer-information and how-exposed-is-it evidence the written risk assessment must rest on. The assessment document, criteria, and periodic refresh are the Qualified Individual's program work.
rows.config_evidence_example: Financial SITs enabled; DSPM assessments over customer-data locations
rows.operational_evidence_example: Data explorer exports and DSPM reports cited in the risk assessment
rows.license_requirement: Data classification analytics (Data/Content/Activity explorer): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Office 365 E5, Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5 Compliance (Purview Suite), or Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5 Information Protection & Governance; E3/A3/G3 tenants keep the underlying Content explorer data aggregation without the explorer interfaces; DSPM: Microsoft 365 E5 or Microsoft Purview Suite (per DSPM get-started documentation; not yet a service-description row)
rows.external_dependencies: Written risk-assessment methodology; Qualified Individual ownership
rows.legacy_dependencies: Written risk-assessment methodology; Qualified Individual ownership

rows.control_intent: Implement and periodically review access controls, limiting access to customer information to authorized users and to what each needs.

rows.how_it_supports: Adds data-layer need-to-know enforcement over customer information in M365. Account provisioning, RBAC, and periodic access reviews are the identity plane.
rows.config_evidence_example: Encrypted 'Customer NPI' label scoped to servicing roles
rows.operational_evidence_example: RMS access logs; label coverage on customer-data stores
rows.license_requirement: Label-based encryption: included with sensitivity labeling entitlements (manual E3+; automatic application E5-tier)

rows.legacy_dependencies: Entra RBAC + access reviews (primary); application entitlements

rows.control_intent: Encrypt all customer information in transit over external networks and at rest (or use approved equivalent controls).
rows.capability_detail: Auto-labeling with encryption on customer-information content; Customer Key for M365 at-rest key control; Purview Message Encryption for external email
rows.how_it_supports: Implements item-level encryption for customer information in M365, layered on inherited platform encryption, and encrypts external mail flows. Database/application-layer encryption and TLS management are outside.
rows.config_evidence_example: Auto-label policy: financial SITs → encrypted label; Message Encryption rules for external sends
rows.operational_evidence_example: Encrypted-item distribution; encrypted-mail logs
rows.license_requirement: Automatic/policy-based sensitivity labeling: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Office 365 E5/A5, Microsoft Purview Suite/EDU/GOV/FLW, Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW, or Microsoft 365 E5/A5/F5/G5 Information Protection & Governance; Customer Key: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Office 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite/EDU/GOV/FLW, or Microsoft 365 E5/A5/F5/G5 Information Protection & Governance
rows.cloud_availability_note: Customer Key and Double Key Encryption: Available in GCC High.

rows.control_intent: Securely dispose of customer information no later than two years after last use for the customer (absent legitimate need/legal requirement), and periodically review retention to minimize unnecessary holding.
rows.capability_detail: Retention labels/policies enforcing the two-year disposal clock on customer-information stores; disposition review producing disposal records; event-based retention keyed to relationship end
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements the disposal mandate for M365-resident customer information (one of the few hard retention clocks in US federal rules) with reviewable proof. Non-M365 systems (loan servicing, CRM) need equivalent purge routines.
rows.config_evidence_example: Retention labels with ≤2-year delete actions (or event-based on relationship end) on NPI stores
rows.operational_evidence_example: Deletion/disposition logs; retention policy review records
rows.license_requirement: Org/location-wide retention policies & manual retention labels: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5/E3/A3/G3 or Business Premium; also Office 365 E5/A5/G5/E3/A3/G3, Microsoft Purview Suite/EDU/GOV/FLW, Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW, or Microsoft 365 E5/A5/F5/G5 Information Protection & Governance; Adaptive scopes / auto-apply retention: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Office 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite/EDU/GOV/FLW, Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW, or Microsoft 365 E5/A5/F5/G5 Information Protection & Governance; Records Management (file plan, record declaration, disposition review): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Office 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite/EDU/GOV/FLW, Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW, or Microsoft 365 E5/A5/F5/G5 Information Protection & Governance
rows.external_dependencies: Application-database purge routines; paper records destruction
rows.legacy_dependencies: Application-database purge routines; paper records destruction

rows.control_intent: Adopt change-management procedures.

rows.how_it_supports: Evidences that changes to data-security controls are attributable and timestamped. The change procedure, approvals, and testing are organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Policy export snapshots per change window
rows.operational_evidence_example: Admin audit extracts joined to change records
rows.license_requirement: Audit (Standard): included across Microsoft 365/Office 365 enterprise, government, and business plans (180-day retention)

rows.legacy_dependencies: Change-management process/tooling

rows.control_intent: Monitor and log authorized-user activity and detect unauthorized access, use, or tampering with customer information.
rows.capability_detail: Unified audit log of user activity on customer information; Insider Risk Management detecting misuse/exfiltration by authorized users; DLP alerts on unauthorized movement
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements activity logging and adds behavioral detection of exactly what the rule targets (authorized users misusing customer information) for the M365 estate. Core banking/CRM systems need their own monitoring.
rows.config_evidence_example: Audit retention policy; IRM policies over NPI locations; DLP alerting
rows.operational_evidence_example: Audit extracts; IRM alert/case history
rows.license_requirement: Audit (Standard): included across Microsoft 365/Office 365 enterprise, government, and business plans (180-day retention); Insider Risk Management: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite/EDU/GOV/FLW, Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW, or Microsoft 365 E5/A5/F5/G5 Insider Risk Management
rows.cloud_availability_note: IRM core policies (data theft by departing users, general data leaks, forensic evidence): Available in GCC High; analytics and several policy templates in preview/development.
rows.external_dependencies: SIEM for application/infrastructure logs; core-system audit trails
rows.legacy_dependencies: SIEM for application/infrastructure logs; core-system audit trails

rows.control_intent: Maintain a written incident response plan for security events materially affecting customer information (goals, roles, communication, remediation, documentation).
rows.capability_detail: DLP/IRM alerts as detection input; Audit (Premium) scope-of-access forensics; eDiscovery evidence preservation feeding the plan's documentation requirements
rows.how_it_supports: Supplies detection, forensic scope (whose customer information, how much), and preserved evidence that the written plan's documentation element requires. The plan itself is organizational.

rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident documentation packs with audit timelines
rows.license_requirement: Audit (Premium): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Office 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite/EDU/GOV/FLW, Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW, or Microsoft 365 E5/G5/F5 eDiscovery & Audit; 10-year retention needs the add-on license; DLP for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5/E3/A3/G3 or Business Premium; also Office 365 E5/A5/G5/E3/A3/G3, Microsoft Purview Suite/EDU/GOV/FLW, Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW, SharePoint Online Plan 2, OneDrive for Business (Plan 2), or Exchange Online Plan 2
rows.cloud_availability_note: Audit (Standard/Premium) incl. 1-year and 10-year retention: Available in GCC High/DoD.
rows.external_dependencies: Written IR plan; legal/communications; SIEM/SOAR
rows.legacy_dependencies: Written IR plan; legal/communications; SIEM/SOAR

rows.control_intent: Notify the FTC within 30 days of discovering a notification event involving unencrypted customer information of 500+ consumers.
rows.capability_detail: Audit (Premium) + eDiscovery quantifying affected data and consumer count: the notice's required scope facts; encryption state of affected items from label reports
rows.how_it_supports: Produces the affected-scope evidence (what was acquired, how many consumers, was it encrypted) that determines whether the 500-consumer threshold is met and populates the FTC form. The determination and filing are legal/organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Audit Premium enabled; label/encryption state reporting available
rows.operational_evidence_example: Scope-quantification workpapers from audit + search results
rows.license_requirement: Audit (Premium): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Office 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite/EDU/GOV/FLW, Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW, or Microsoft 365 E5/G5/F5 eDiscovery & Audit; 10-year retention needs the add-on license
rows.cloud_availability_note: Audit (Standard/Premium) incl. 1-year and 10-year retention: Available in GCC High/DoD.
rows.external_dependencies: Breach counsel determination; FTC filing process
rows.legacy_dependencies: Breach counsel determination; FTC filing process

Microsoft Entra Conditional Access: Microsoft Entra ID P1 (included in Microsoft 365 E3/E5/E7, Microsoft 365 F1/F3, Enterprise Mobility + Security E3, and Business Premium). rows.control_intent: Implement multifactor authentication for any individual accessing any information system (unless an approved reasonably equivalent control is used).
rows.capability_detail: Conditional Access enforcing MFA (phishing-resistant methods) for all users accessing Entra-integrated information systems

Methods: Multifactor authentication & authentication methods: Microsoft Entra ID Free (basic) and P1; authentication strengths and full policy control require Entra ID P1.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Conditional Access: available in Azure Government (GCC, GCC High, DoD). MFA and authentication methods (incl. passwordless/FIDO2, certificate-based auth): available in Azure Government (GCC/GCC High/DoD).

rows.control_intent: Monitor and log authorized-user activity and detect unauthorized access to or use of customer information.
rows.capability_detail: Sign-in and audit logs of authorized-user activity plus Entra ID Protection risk detection (leaked credentials, impossible travel) flagging unauthorized/anomalous access
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the identity slice of activity monitoring and unauthorized-access detection for Entra-authenticated access. Data-activity monitoring and non-Entra system logs are dependencies.

Sentinel ingests Entra sign-in and risk logs for correlation and retention

rows.cloud_availability_note: ID Protection risk detections, risky-account investigation, and SIEM connectivity: available in Azure Government; Microsoft Entra threat-intelligence detection is not available in gov clouds.

rows.control_intent: Identify and manage the data, personnel, devices, systems, and facilities that enable business purposes, per their importance.

rows.how_it_supports: Implements the devices slice of the required identify-and-manage inventory. The data dimension is the Purview seam; personnel, systems, and facilities dimensions live in HR/CMDB/facilities records.
rows.config_evidence_example: Enrollment coverage for devices touching customer information; inventory collection enabled
rows.operational_evidence_example: All-devices inventory export reconciled to the ITAM register; lifecycle action history

rows.control_intent: Encrypt all customer information in transit over external networks and at rest (or use approved equivalent controls).
rows.capability_detail: Disk-encryption policy enforcing BitLocker/FileVault on endpoints holding customer information, with compliance attestation; app protection policies encrypting org data in mobile apps used for customer information
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the endpoint slice of the at-rest encryption mandate: enforcing and attesting platform encryption on managed devices. Content-layer encryption in M365 is the Purview seam; database encryption and TLS on transmission paths are separate layers.
rows.config_evidence_example: Disk-encryption profiles on customer-information endpoints; compliance encryption requirement; app protection policies

rows.control_intent: Monitor and log authorized-user activity and detect unauthorized access, use, or tampering with customer information.
rows.capability_detail: The unauthorized-access detection layer: EDR flagging anomalous behavior on endpoints holding customer information, Defender for Identity detecting identity misuse and lateral movement toward data stores, Defender for Cloud Apps anomaly detection (mass download, impossible travel) on SaaS activity, correlated into incidents
rows.how_it_supports: Implements detection of unauthorized access/use across endpoints, identities, and cloud apps, stacking with the Purview row (activity logging + insider misuse) and Entra row (sign-in anomalies). Monitoring inside core banking/CRM systems requires their own audit facilities.
rows.config_evidence_example: EDR onboarding on devices handling customer information; MDI sensors deployed; MDCA anomaly policies on in-scope apps
rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident and anomaly alert reports tied to customer-information systems; detection-coverage reviews

related_microsoft.note: Unified audit log and Insider Risk Management supply activity logging and insider-misuse detection (the stacked Purview row)
Entra ID Protection detects anomalous sign-ins to systems holding customer information (the stacked Entra row)

rows.external_dependencies: Monitoring within core banking/CRM platforms; monitoring procedures and response ownership
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Defender XDR has no separate license; the unified portal and correlation come with any qualifying workload license (Microsoft 365 E5/A5; Microsoft 365 E3 with the Microsoft Defender Suite or Enterprise Mobility + Security E5 add-on; Microsoft 365 A3 with the A5 Security add-on; Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5; Enterprise Mobility + Security E5/A5; Office 365 E5/A5; Microsoft 365 Business Premium; or standalone Defender workload licenses). Automatic attack disruption and threat analytics require Defender for Endpoint Plan 2.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender XDR (unified portal, incidents, advanced hunting, attack disruption): available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD; Microsoft Threat Experts is not available in gov clouds; preview features are commercial-only.

rows.control_intent: Regularly test or monitor the effectiveness of safeguards, including attack/intrusion detection; for information systems, use continuous monitoring or, absent it, annual penetration testing plus biannual vulnerability assessments.
rows.capability_detail: Continuous monitoring of exactly the kind the rule names as the alternative to periodic testing: Defender Vulnerability Management continuously detecting system changes that create vulnerabilities (new CVEs, configuration drift, new software), EDR detecting actual and attempted attacks/intrusions, with exportable evidence of both
rows.how_it_supports: Directly supplies the 'continuous monitoring or other systems to detect, on an ongoing basis, changes in information systems that may create vulnerabilities' that §314.4(d)(2) accepts in place of annual pen tests and biannual vulnerability assessments, for the onboarded estate. Whether the deployment qualifies as effective continuous monitoring for the whole institution is a program-level determination; non-onboarded systems still need testing.
rows.config_evidence_example: MDVM assessments and EDR active across systems handling customer information; monitoring-effectiveness review cadence
rows.operational_evidence_example: Continuous vulnerability-detection exports; attack/intrusion detection reports; coverage reconciliation against the information-system inventory

related_microsoft.note: Intune remediates the vulnerabilities and configuration drift the monitoring surfaces
Sentinel extends continuous monitoring to non-Microsoft systems

rows.external_dependencies: Qualified-individual determination that continuous monitoring is effective; penetration testing where elected or required; coverage for core banking/CRM and network infrastructure
rows.license_requirement: Defender Vulnerability Management core capabilities: included with Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 2 (Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or standalone P2). Premium capabilities (security baselines assessment, block vulnerable applications, browser-extension/certificate/hardware-firmware assessment) require the Defender Vulnerability Management add-on, available to Defender for Endpoint Plan 2, Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Defender Suite/EDU/GOV/FLW, Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW, and Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5/G5 customers; an MDVM standalone exists for non-P2 customers. Attack-detection layer: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 2: standalone or included in Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Defender Vulnerability Management core (with MDE Plan 2): available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD; the MDVM add-on trial is not available in GCC High/DoD, so confirm add-on purchase options for gov tenants.

rows.control_intent: Monitor and log authorized-user activity and detect unauthorized access, use, or tampering with customer information.
rows.capability_detail: Monitor-log-detect for the whole institution: the core banking, CRM, and application audit trails all three stacked rows defer are ingested via connectors and AMA alongside M365, identity, and endpoint signal; UEBA baselines authorized-user behavior and scores deviations (unusual record access, off-hours volume, tampering patterns); analytics correlate across systems and retention preserves the activity log the rule requires
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements the monitor-and-log-and-detect activity this safeguard names, at the scope the stacked rows cannot reach (the institution's core systems), provided their logs are forwarded. Purview covers M365 activity, Entra sign-ins, Defender workload detection; Sentinel is where the institution-wide picture exists. Log generation inside core platforms and the response procedures remain the institution's.
rows.config_evidence_example: Core banking/CRM log sources connected; UEBA enabled; analytics covering unauthorized-access and tampering patterns per system; retention per the records schedule
rows.operational_evidence_example: Cross-system activity and detection reports; UEBA anomaly history for users touching customer information; connector coverage against the system inventory

related_microsoft.note: Unified audit log and Insider Risk Management cover M365 activity and insider misuse (the stacked Purview row)
Entra ID Protection detects anomalous sign-ins (the stacked Entra row)
Defender XDR detects unauthorized access on endpoints/identities/SaaS (the stacked Defender row); seam: workload sensors detect, Sentinel monitors and logs institution-wide

rows.external_dependencies: Log generation on core banking/CRM platforms; monitoring procedures and response ownership
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027. Included with Microsoft Sentinel on the workspace (no separate license); cost follows the consumption-priced data volume the capability operates over.

rows.control_intent: Regularly test or monitor the effectiveness of safeguards, including attack/intrusion detection; for information systems, use continuous monitoring or, absent it, annual penetration testing plus biannual vulnerability assessments.
rows.capability_detail: The institution-wide arm of the continuous-monitoring alternative §314.4(d)(2) accepts: always-on detection of attacks and intrusions across every connected system, including the network infrastructure and core platforms the stacked Defender row leaves external, with analytics-rule and connector-health records evidencing that monitoring itself operates continuously
rows.how_it_supports: Extends the continuous-monitoring posture to the systems Defender cannot onboard, completing the coverage argument a Qualified Individual must make for the (d)(2) alternative. Sentinel monitors and evidences; whether the combined deployment qualifies as effective continuous monitoring is a program-level determination, and vulnerability-detection depth on non-onboarded systems may still need scanners.
rows.config_evidence_example: Connector estate spanning the information-system inventory; attack/intrusion analytics per source class; monitoring-effectiveness review workbook
rows.operational_evidence_example: Continuous-detection reports across systems; monitoring-coverage reconciliation; connector uptime evidence

related_microsoft.note: Defender Vulnerability Management + EDR are the continuous-monitoring engine for the onboarded estate (the stacked Defender row)

rows.external_dependencies: Qualified-individual effectiveness determination; penetration testing where elected; vulnerability assessment for non-onboarded systems
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027.

rows.control_intent: Maintain a written incident response plan for security events materially affecting customer information (goals, roles, communication, remediation, documentation).
rows.capability_detail: Execution evidence for the written plan: each security event tracked as an incident with timeline, roles (ownership/assignment mirroring the plan's defined roles), remediation actions, and closure: the documentation-and-remediation elements of §314.4(h) materialized as exportable records; playbooks encode the plan's communication steps with run history
rows.how_it_supports: Evidences that the written plan operates. The plan itself is a document the rule requires the institution to author, and Sentinel cannot substitute for it; what Sentinel supplies is the documented execution trail (who did what, when, how communicated) each incident under the plan produces. The stacked Purview row supplies the affected-data forensics.
rows.config_evidence_example: Incident workflow mapped to the plan's roles and phases; communication playbooks per the plan; documentation export procedure
rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident records demonstrating plan execution; playbook run history for communication steps

related_microsoft.note: Audit (Premium) forensics and eDiscovery quantify the affected customer information (the stacked Purview row)
Defender XDR executes containment on Microsoft-estate events

rows.external_dependencies: The written IR plan and its required elements; legal/communications ownership; FTC notification duties (§314.4(j))
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027. Microsoft Sentinel automation rules are included with the workspace (consumption-priced service); Logic Apps playbooks are billed separately under Azure Logic Apps consumption meters.

rows.control_intent: Regularly test or monitor the effectiveness of safeguards, including attack/intrusion detection; for information systems, use continuous monitoring or, absent it, annual penetration testing plus biannual vulnerability assessments.
rows.capability_detail: The continuous-monitoring alternative for cloud-hosted customer-information systems: agentless vulnerability scanning re-assesses VMs/containers/databases continuously (standing in for the biannual vulnerability-assessment floor with a materially stronger cadence), MCSB recommendations continuously test safeguard configuration, and workload threat detection supplies the attack/intrusion-detection element §314.4(d)(1) names
rows.how_it_supports: Extends the §314.4(d)(2) continuous-monitoring path the stacked Defender XDR row establishes for endpoints to the cloud-infrastructure estate: banking workloads in Azure/AWS/GCP get continuous safeguard-effectiveness monitoring rather than point-in-time testing. Penetration testing (where the institution stays on the (d)(2)(i) path), non-cloud systems, and the documented testing program remain external.
rows.config_evidence_example: Defender CSPM with agentless scanning on customer-information subscriptions; workload protection plans for intrusion detection; monitoring approach documented as the (d)(2) election
rows.operational_evidence_example: Continuous vulnerability assessment records; safeguard-configuration finding history; intrusion-detection alert logs for the testing file

related_microsoft.note: MDVM continuous vulnerability management covers endpoint devices (the stacked Defender XDR row); together they ground the §314.4(d)(2) continuous-monitoring election across device and cloud planes
Sentinel supplies the estate-wide monitoring record (the stacked Sentinel row)

rows.external_dependencies: Annual penetration testing where elected; testing for on-premises core-banking systems; the documented testing/monitoring program
rows.license_requirement: Defender CSPM: consumption-priced per billable resource per month. It protects all multicloud workloads but bills only on servers, storage accounts, databases (OSS/SQL incl. servers-on-machines), and serverless resources (conversion ratios apply). 30-day free trial on first enablement; a 1-year pre-purchase plan (Commit Units) discounts up to 22%. Consumption-priced per protected resource, metered by plan: Defender for Storage per storage account/month plus per-transaction overage above 73M/month and per-GB malware scanning; Defender for Databases per database server (Cosmos DB per provisioned RU); Defender for Containers per vCore of Kubernetes worker nodes (includes a monthly image-scan allowance); Defender for App Service per app instance; Defender for Key Vault per vault/month; Defender for Resource Manager per subscription/month; Defender for APIs by call-volume plan tier (Plans 1–5); AI Services per 1K tokens. 30-day free trial on first enablement (malware scanning excluded).
rows.cloud_availability_note: Azure Government gaps: Defender for APIs, Defender for App Service, AI Services threat protection, Defender for Cosmos DB, and DevOps security are not available; within Defender CSPM, CIEM, the Data & AI security dashboard, EASM integration, ServiceNow integration, and code-to-runtime mapping are not available; Defender for Storage sensitive data threat detection is not available (DSPM sensitive data scanning is GA).

rows.control_intent: Identify and manage the data, personnel, devices, systems, and facilities that enable business purposes, per their importance.
rows.capability_detail: The systems half of the identify-and-manage safeguard for cloud estates: asset inventory automatically enumerates every onboarded cloud resource with security state; sensitive data discovery (Defender CSPM) identifies which cloud storage and databases actually hold customer-information-pattern data, joining the systems register to the data it carries; criticality tagging weights business importance in the security graph
rows.how_it_supports: Maintains the cloud-systems inventory and, through data-aware posture, connects systems to the customer information they hold, which is the per-their-importance dimension the safeguard names. The stacked Purview row identifies and classifies the data itself in M365 (Purview is the classification authority; its SITs power this discovery); Intune inventories devices; personnel and facilities are organizational registers.
rows.config_evidence_example: Cloud estates onboarded; sensitive data discovery enabled with financial-data SITs; criticality tags applied per business importance
rows.operational_evidence_example: Asset inventory exports; sensitive-data discovery findings mapping systems to customer information; tagged-criticality coverage reports

related_microsoft.note: Purview data classification identifies and manages the customer information itself in M365 (the stacked Purview row); its sensitive information types power Defender for Cloud's discovery; different classification scopes, one taxonomy
Intune inventories and manages the device dimension (the stacked Intune row)

rows.external_dependencies: Personnel and facilities registers; data inventory for non-cloud stores; the importance-ranking methodology
rows.license_requirement: Foundational CSPM is free on every subscription/account onboarded to Defender for Cloud (secure score, MCSB security recommendations, asset inventory, multicloud connectors): no per-user license and no charge. The paid plans below are consumption-priced per protected resource. Defender CSPM: consumption-priced per billable resource per month. It protects all multicloud workloads but bills only on servers, storage accounts, databases (OSS/SQL incl. servers-on-machines), and serverless resources (conversion ratios apply). 30-day free trial on first enablement; a 1-year pre-purchase plan (Commit Units) discounts up to 22%.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Azure Government gaps: Defender for APIs, Defender for App Service, AI Services threat protection, Defender for Cosmos DB, and DevOps security are not available; within Defender CSPM, CIEM, the Data & AI security dashboard, EASM integration, ServiceNow integration, and code-to-runtime mapping are not available; Defender for Storage sensitive data threat detection is not available (DSPM sensitive data scanning is GA).

rows.control_domain: Subpart B: Right to inspect & review
rows.control_intent: Give a parent or eligible student the opportunity to inspect and review the student's education records, responding within a reasonable period and no more than 45 days after the request.
rows.capability_detail: eDiscovery search locating a student's education-record content scattered across Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Teams; export for the review response
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the locate/collect step for the M365-resident portion of a records request inside the 45-day clock: advising emails, shared documents, counseling notes that never made it into the SIS. Request intake, identity verification, §99.12 limitation review, and the SIS extract are registrar/process work.
rows.config_evidence_example: Documented records-request runbook using an eDiscovery case per request
rows.operational_evidence_example: Per-request search and export records with dates against the 45-day window
rows.license_requirement: eDiscovery (search, cases, hold, export): Microsoft 365 E3 or Office 365 E3/A3/G3/F3; premium features (custodians, review sets, analytics): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/F5/G5, Office 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite/EDU/GOV/FLW, or Microsoft 365 E5/A5/F5/G5 eDiscovery & Audit
rows.external_dependencies: SIS/registrar extract (primary record store); request intake and verification process; §99.12 redaction review
rows.legacy_dependencies: SIS/registrar extract (primary record store); request intake and verification process; §99.12 redaction review

rows.control_intent: Use reasonable methods (the regulation names physical or technological access controls) to ensure school officials access only education records in which they have legitimate educational interests.
rows.capability_detail: Sensitivity labels with encryption scoping student-record content to authorized official groups; DLP restricting movement outside those groups; IRM flagging anomalous bulk access to student records
rows.how_it_supports: Supplies the 'technological access controls' the regulation prefers, for education records living in M365: labeled and encrypted content opens only for officials with the configured interest basis, and out-of-scope movement raises alerts. SIS role-based access and the legitimate-educational-interest criteria themselves are institutional.
rows.config_evidence_example: 'Student records' label tier with encryption scoped to official roles; DLP rules on student-record SITs/label
rows.operational_evidence_example: RMS access-denied events; DLP alerts; IRM anomalous-access cases for student-record stores
rows.license_requirement: Label-based encryption: included with sensitivity labeling entitlements (manual E3+; automatic application E5-tier); Automatic/policy-based sensitivity labeling: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Office 365 E5/A5, Microsoft Purview Suite/EDU/GOV/FLW, Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW, or Microsoft 365 E5/A5/F5/G5 Information Protection & Governance; DLP for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5/E3/A3/G3 or Business Premium; also Office 365 E5/A5/G5/E3/A3/G3, Microsoft Purview Suite/EDU/GOV/FLW, Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW, SharePoint Online Plan 2, OneDrive for Business (Plan 2), or Exchange Online Plan 2

rows.control_intent: Obtain signed, dated written consent before disclosing personally identifiable information from education records (outside §99.31 exceptions), and bind recipients against redisclosure without consent.
rows.capability_detail: DLP rules guarding student-record content against egress to unauthorized external recipients across email, Teams, and sharing links
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the prevention layer under the consent rule: unconsented disclosure paths from M365 are blocked or forced through justification, shrinking the accidental-disclosure surface §99.30 exists to police. Consent capture, exception determinations, and recipient redisclosure obligations are process and contract work.

rows.operational_evidence_example: Blocked/justified external-send events for student-record content

rows.how_it_supports: Evidences who accessed and shared M365-resident student-record content, feeding the formal §99.32 disclosure log, and enforces the log's own keep-as-long-as-the-records retention. Microsoft Entra Conditional Access: Microsoft Entra ID P1 (included in Microsoft 365 E3/E5/E7, Microsoft 365 F1/F3, Enterprise Mobility + Security E3, and Business Premium). Risk-based conditions additionally require Entra ID P2.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Access reviews, entitlement management, PIM, and Lifecycle Workflows (Microsoft Entra ID Governance): available in GCC, GCC High, and DoD.

rows.control_intent: Security of processing: pseudonymisation and encryption of personal data as risk-appropriate measures.
rows.capability_detail: Disk-encryption policy enforcing BitLocker/FileVault on endpoints processing personal data, with compliance attestation; app protection policies encrypting personal data handled in managed mobile apps on corporate and BYO devices
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the device-layer encryption measure, a recognised technical and organisational measure (TOM) under Art. 32, for managed endpoints and mobile apps. Content-layer encryption in M365 is the Purview seam; pseudonymisation engineering and database crypto are external.
rows.config_evidence_example: Disk-encryption profiles; compliance encryption requirement; app protection policies for mobile processing
rows.operational_evidence_example: Encryption status report; compliance encryption state; app-protection delivery status: TOM evidence for Art. 30 records and processor assurances

related_microsoft.note: Label-based encryption with customer key options implements content-layer encryption of personal data

rows.external_dependencies: Pseudonymisation tooling; application/database encryption; key management
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Intune Plan 1 (the base Intune service; included in Microsoft 365 bundles such as E3/E5/E7 and Enterprise Mobility + Security E3/E5, also available standalone)

rows.control_intent: Security of processing: ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience of processing systems, including access control as a technical measure.
rows.capability_detail: Device compliance and configuration policies keeping endpoints that process personal data in a continuously verified secure state (encryption, OS currency, credential, threat level), with noncompliant devices losing access via Conditional Access; selective wipe removing personal/org data from lost or departing devices
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the endpoint-confidentiality slice of the ongoing-security obligation, a demonstrable TOM: devices must prove a secure state to keep processing access, and data on lost devices is recoverable-proof. Identity access control is the Entra layer; availability/resilience of processing systems is infrastructure.
rows.config_evidence_example: Compliance policies per platform; require-compliant-device Conditional Access on personal-data systems; wipe/retire runbook for lost devices
rows.operational_evidence_example: Compliance status reports; Conditional Access grant/block evidence; wipe action records

related_microsoft.note: Conditional Access and MFA implement the identity access-control measure under the same article
DLP and label encryption implement content-layer confidentiality measures

rows.external_dependencies: Availability/resilience measures (backup, DR); security of non-endpoint processing systems
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Intune Plan 1; enforcing the compliance signal through Conditional Access additionally requires Microsoft Entra ID P1

rows.control_intent: Security of processing: ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience of processing systems, including access control as a technical measure.
rows.capability_detail: Threat prevention, detection, and response protecting the confidentiality and integrity of processing systems: next-gen antivirus and attack surface reduction preventing compromise, EDR and cross-workload correlation detecting it, automated investigation and attack disruption containing it before personal data is exposed
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the threat-defense dimension of ongoing confidentiality/integrity: a system compromised by malware or an intruder can guarantee neither. Stacks with the Entra row (access control) and Intune row (device hardening); availability/resilience engineering (backup, redundancy) is separate.
rows.config_evidence_example: AV and ASR in block mode; EDR onboarding across systems processing personal data; attack disruption enabled
rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident detection/containment reports for systems processing personal data; threat-protection status reports

related_microsoft.note: Conditional Access and MFA implement the access-control measure (the stacked Entra row)
Intune enforces device encryption and hardening (the stacked Intune rows)
DLP and sensitivity labels protect the personal data itself

rows.external_dependencies: Availability and resilience engineering (backup, redundancy, DR); protection for non-Microsoft processing systems
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 2: standalone or included in Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW.

rows.control_intent: Regularly test, assess, and evaluate the effectiveness of technical and organisational security measures.
rows.capability_detail: Recurring technical-measure assessment: Defender Vulnerability Management continuously assessing vulnerabilities and secure-configuration posture against baselines, Microsoft Secure Score in the Defender portal quantifying security posture and tracking improvement, attack simulation training (Defender for Office 365 Plan 2) empirically testing the phishing-resistance measure
rows.how_it_supports: Implements a continuously running test-assess-evaluate mechanism for the technical measures on the Microsoft estate, stacking with the Purview row's Compliance Manager evidence. Penetration testing, audits, and evaluation of organisational measures remain the broader program.
rows.config_evidence_example: MDVM secure-configuration assessment active; Secure Score review cadence; recurring attack-simulation campaigns
rows.operational_evidence_example: Configuration-assessment and Secure Score trend exports; attack-simulation result reports as measure-effectiveness evidence

related_microsoft.note: Compliance Manager continuous control testing evidences the assessment process (the stacked Purview row)

rows.external_dependencies: Penetration testing and independent audits; evaluation of organisational measures
rows.license_requirement: Defender Vulnerability Management core capabilities: included with Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 2 (Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or standalone P2). Premium capabilities (security baselines assessment, block vulnerable applications, browser-extension/certificate/hardware-firmware assessment) require the Defender Vulnerability Management add-on, available to Defender for Endpoint Plan 2, Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Defender Suite/EDU/GOV/FLW, Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW, and Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5/G5 customers; an MDVM standalone exists for non-P2 customers. Attack simulation training: Microsoft Defender for Office 365 Plan 2: standalone or included in Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Office 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW. Threat Explorer, automated investigation & response, attack simulation training, campaign views, and Defender XDR integration are Plan 2-only.

rows.control_intent: Notify the supervisory authority within 72 hours (with nature, categories, and approximate numbers of subjects/records), document all breaches (33(5)), and communicate high-risk breaches to data subjects.
rows.capability_detail: Breach detection and scoping that starts and feeds the 72-hour clock: cross-workload detection surfacing the breach promptly ('awareness' under Art. 33(1)), incident investigation establishing attack path and affected systems, containment limiting the breach's scope, and Action center/incident records feeding the Art. 33(5) documentation duty
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the detect-investigate-contain foundation the notification obligations rest on: a breach cannot be notified within 72 hours if it is not detected and scoped quickly. Stacks with the Purview row (which data/subjects were affected). The risk assessment, notification decision, and filings are legal/organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Detection coverage across systems processing personal data; incident notification rules to the DPO/privacy team; investigation SOPs aligned to the 72-hour timeline
rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident timeline records (first-detection timestamps to containment); investigation reports feeding breach documentation

related_microsoft.note: Audit (Premium) forensics quantify categories and approximate numbers of data subjects/records, the Art. 33(3) content (the stacked Purview row)
Sentinel extends breach detection to non-Microsoft processing systems

rows.external_dependencies: Breach risk assessment and notification decisioning (DPO/legal); supervisory-authority filings; data-subject communications
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 2: standalone or included in Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Windows 11/10 Enterprise E5/A5, Microsoft Defender Suite (formerly Microsoft 365 E5 Security)/EDU/GOV/FLW, or Microsoft Defender + Purview Suite FLW.

rows.control_intent: Security of processing: ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience of processing systems, including access control as a technical measure.
rows.capability_detail: The ongoing-assurance monitoring measure across all processing systems: continuous detection over Microsoft workloads and, via connectors, the non-Microsoft processing systems the stacked Defender row leaves external (databases, applications, infrastructure); availability/error anomaly detection from operational log streams contributing to the availability-and-resilience dimension; alerting that turns a CIA failure into a response within hours, not months
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the monitoring measure that makes 'ongoing' meaningful: confidentiality and integrity cannot be ensured on an ongoing basis without a mechanism that notices when they fail, and Sentinel is that mechanism at estate scope. Stacks as the fourth technical-measure card (Entra: access control; Intune: device protection; Defender: threat defense). Resilience engineering itself (backup, redundancy) remains separate.
rows.config_evidence_example: Connector coverage across systems processing personal data; detection rules per system class; availability/error anomaly detections
rows.operational_evidence_example: Continuous-monitoring reports across processing systems; detection and response-time evidence

related_microsoft.note: Conditional Access and MFA implement the access-control measure (the stacked Entra row)
Intune enforces device encryption and hardening (the stacked Intune row)
Defender XDR prevents and detects compromise on Microsoft workloads (the stacked Defender row); Sentinel extends detection to every other processing system

rows.external_dependencies: Availability and resilience engineering (backup, redundancy, DR); log generation on non-Microsoft processing systems
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027.

rows.control_intent: Regularly test, assess, and evaluate the effectiveness of technical and organisational security measures.
rows.capability_detail: Effectiveness evidence for the detection-and-response measures themselves: MITRE ATT&CK coverage mapping showing which techniques the analytics estate detects and where gaps sit, analytics-rule health monitoring proving the measures run, and SOC incident metrics (detection volumes, response times, trend lines) as the recurring assessment artifact for the monitoring measure
rows.how_it_supports: Evidences the regular-evaluation obligation for one class of technical measures (the organisation's detection and response capability), with artifacts a DPO can put in the Art. 32 file. It does not test the measures (no simulation or scanning); the stacked Defender row carries continuous technical assessment and the Purview row the structured assessment program.
rows.config_evidence_example: MITRE coverage review cadence; rule health monitoring enabled; SOC metrics workbook in the assessment procedure
rows.operational_evidence_example: Coverage-gap review records; rule health reports; SOC metric trend exports in assessment documentation

related_microsoft.note: Compliance Manager structures the assessment program (the stacked Purview row)
Defender Vulnerability Management and attack simulation actively test technical measures (the stacked Defender row)

rows.external_dependencies: Penetration testing and audits; evaluation of organisational measures; assessment ownership
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027.

rows.control_intent: Notify the supervisory authority within 72 hours (with nature, categories, and approximate numbers of subjects/records), document all breaches (33(5)), and communicate high-risk breaches to data subjects.
rows.capability_detail: The breach-clock and documentation machinery at controller scope: detection across every connected processing system, including the non-Microsoft systems the stacked Defender row cannot see, starting Art. 33(1) awareness promptly; the incident timeline (first detection through containment) as the 72-hour narrative and the without-undue-delay evidence; playbooks driving the DPO-notification workflow the moment a qualifying incident opens; incident records feeding the Art. 33(5) breach register
rows.how_it_supports: Implements breach detection for the whole processing estate and produces the facts-and-effects documentation Art. 33(5) demands as a durable record. The stacked Purview row quantifies affected subjects/records; the risk assessment, notification decision, and filings are the DPO's and counsel's.
rows.config_evidence_example: Connector coverage across processing systems; DPO-notification playbook on qualifying incident classes; breach-register export procedure
rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident timelines evidencing awareness-to-notification elapsed time; playbook run history; breach documentation exports

related_microsoft.note: Audit (Premium) forensics quantify categories and approximate numbers of data subjects/records, the Art. 33(3) content (the stacked Purview row)
Defender XDR detects and contains Microsoft-workload breaches (the stacked Defender row); Sentinel extends detection estate-wide and holds the documented record

rows.external_dependencies: Breach risk assessment and notification decisioning (DPO/legal); supervisory-authority filings; data-subject communications
rows.license_requirement: Microsoft Sentinel is consumption-priced on data ingestion (no per-user license): pay-as-you-go per GB into the analytics tier, or commitment tiers from 100 GB/day at discounted effective rates; simplified pricing combines Log Analytics + Sentinel meters. A 50 GB/day commitment tier launched Oct 1, 2025 and remains in public preview: its promotional pricing was extended through Dec 31, 2026, and customers who sign up in that window lock in the discounted rate through Mar 31, 2027. Microsoft Sentinel automation rules are included with the workspace (consumption-priced service); Logic Apps playbooks are billed separately under Azure Logic Apps consumption meters.

rows.control_intent: Security of processing: ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience of processing systems, including access control as a technical measure.
rows.capability_detail: Technical measures for personal data processed on cloud infrastructure: sensitive data discovery locates cloud storage and databases holding personal-data patterns (powered by the Microsoft Purview classification engine, using the same SITs and sensitivity labels, importable including custom types), attack path analysis surfaces exploitable routes to those stores, CIEM right-sizes the identities that can reach them, and workload threat detection guards the processing systems at runtime
rows.how_it_supports: Contributes the appropriate-technical-measures showing for the cloud-infrastructure slice of the processing estate: the controller can demonstrate it knows where personal data sits in cloud stores, what threatens it, and that detection is active. Classification-scope seam: Purview is the classification and protection authority for M365 and connected sources (full scanning, labeling, DLP); Defender for Cloud samples cloud datastores for posture prioritization using Purview's taxonomy; it discovers and prioritizes, it does not label or protect content. The four stacked product rows carry the identity, device, endpoint, and monitoring measures.
rows.config_evidence_example: Sensitive data discovery enabled with personal-data SITs (custom types imported from Purview); attack-path review covering personal-data stores; workload plans on processing subscriptions
rows.operational_evidence_example: Sensitive-data discovery findings per cloud store; attack-path reports scoped to personal-data resources; CIEM over-permission findings and remediation history

related_microsoft.note: Purview is the classification/protection authority; its SIT taxonomy powers this discovery, and it labels and protects the content itself in M365 (different classification scopes, one taxonomy)
Entra ID carries the access-control technical measure this article names (the stacked Entra row)
Defender XDR protects the endpoint/workload processing path (the stacked Defender row)

rows.external_dependencies: Backup/resilience measures (Azure Backup/Site Recovery, outside this product); encryption implementation; DPIA and lawful-basis obligations; on-premises processing systems
rows.license_requirement: Defender CSPM: consumption-priced per billable resource per month. It protects all multicloud workloads but bills only on servers, storage accounts, databases (OSS/SQL incl. servers-on-machines), and serverless resources (conversion ratios apply). 30-day free trial on first enablement; a 1-year pre-purchase plan (Commit Units) discounts up to 22%. Consumption-priced per protected resource, metered by plan: Defender for Storage per storage account/month plus per-transaction overage above 73M/month and per-GB malware scanning; Defender for Databases per database server (Cosmos DB per provisioned RU); Defender for Containers per vCore of Kubernetes worker nodes (includes a monthly image-scan allowance); Defender for App Service per app instance; Defender for Key Vault per vault/month; Defender for Resource Manager per subscription/month; Defender for APIs by call-volume plan tier (Plans 1–5); AI Services per 1K tokens. 30-day free trial on first enablement (malware scanning excluded).
rows.cloud_availability_note: Azure Government gaps: Defender for APIs, Defender for App Service, AI Services threat protection, Defender for Cosmos DB, and DevOps security are not available; within Defender CSPM, CIEM, the Data & AI security dashboard, EASM integration, ServiceNow integration, and code-to-runtime mapping are not available; Defender for Storage sensitive data threat detection is not available (DSPM sensitive data scanning is GA).

rows.control_intent: Regularly test, assess, and evaluate the effectiveness of technical and organisational security measures.
rows.capability_detail: A standing test-assess-evaluate mechanism for cloud-hosted processing systems: the regulatory compliance dashboard continuously assesses onboarded cloud resources against the EU GDPR built-in standard and MCSB with per-control pass/fail, secure score trends the effectiveness of technical measures over time, and agentless vulnerability assessment supplies the regular technical-testing input
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the regularly-assessing-and-evaluating activity for technical measures on the cloud slice of the processing estate. Assessment-scope boundary: it assesses onboarded cloud resources against technically-assessable criteria, not organisational measures, not M365/on-premises processing systems (the stacked Purview row's Compliance Manager and the Sentinel evidence row cover their planes), and its output demonstrates technical posture, not Art. 32 compliance as such. Penetration testing and audit remain the controller's program.
rows.config_evidence_example: EU GDPR standard assigned to processing-system subscriptions (requires a paid plan); assessment reports wired into the Art. 32(1)(d) evaluation cadence
rows.operational_evidence_example: Per-standard compliance reports over time; secure score trend; vulnerability assessment history for the evaluation record

related_microsoft.note: Purview Compliance Manager's GDPR assessment covers the M365 processing estate (the stacked Purview row); complementary evaluation scopes, a seam not an overlap
Sentinel evidences monitoring-measure operation estate-wide (the stacked Sentinel row)

rows.external_dependencies: Penetration testing and security audits; evaluation of organisational measures; DPO oversight and documentation
rows.license_requirement: Regulatory compliance dashboard: the default Microsoft cloud security benchmark (MCSB) assessment is included free with Defender for Cloud. Assigning additional built-in or custom compliance standards requires at least one paid Defender for Cloud plan on the assessed scope, meaning any plan except Defender for Servers Plan 1 and Defender for APIs Plan 1.
rows.cloud_availability_note: Regulatory compliance dashboard: GA in Azure Government (configuration via the Azure portal experience; available standards vary by cloud, so verify the standard list in the target cloud).
